content.text: The doc settled on **TrapHunter** throughout (the `ProofOfWork` in the return value was a stale earlier-round name). Let me read the full solution to report it accurately.

content.content:
1	# TrapHunter — Complete Build-Day Solution
2	
3	**Working product name:** TrapHunter  •  **The credential:** DriveScore
4	**One line:** A work-sample ability simulator that drops you into an AI-buildable codebase with planted defects and scores whether you catch what the AI got wrong, then hands you a shareable DriveScore credential you own.
5	
6	This document is the full, hardened solution: (1) idea brief, (2) machine-gradable rubric, (3) the single master prompt that drives the agentic build-self-correct-verify loop, (4) sub-agent roles, (5) the self-verification plan. It folds in all 25 critic points; the most consequential changes are called out inline as **[FIX N]**.
7	
8	Two live facts this design depends on, verified 2026-06-13:
9	- Vercel **Fluid Compute** raises the Hobby function timeout from **60s to 300s** (`maxDuration` up to 300 on Hobby; >300 needs Pro). Source: https://vercel.com/docs/functions/limitations and https://vercel.com/changelog/higher-defaults-and-limits-for-vercel-functions-running-fluid-compute
10	- **E2B** cold start is **~80-200ms** (Firecracker + pre-warmed snapshots), so a per-call cold-start sandbox is viable and removes any warm-session dependency. Source: https://www.startuphub.ai/ai-news/artificial-intelligence/2026/daytona-vs-e2b-vs-modal-vs-vercel-sandbox-2026
11	
12	---
13	
14	## 1) IDEA BRIEF
15	
16	### The problem (lead with the urgent, quantified pain)
17	Remote coding screens have collapsed under undetectable AI use. Cheating roughly doubled across H2 2025 (one large vendor saw 15%→35%; a real 700-applicant funnel exceeded 50%), and 81% of Big Tech interviewers now suspect AI use. The incumbent response is regressive: re-adding onsite interviews and "no-AI" attestations. Meanwhile LeetCode was already a poor job signal (77% of developers say assessments don't reflect the job; 47% fail despite solving everything correctly). And 92% of developers now use AI tools; AI skills appear in ~42% of software JDs. The relevant skill is no longer writing algorithms from scratch. It is **agentic engineering**: driving AI to ship correct code while preserving the quality bar, because AI raises output but not quality (Faros AI: +9% bugs per developer with AI adoption).
18	
19	You cannot cheat a test where **using AI well is the test**. That is the wedge.
20	
21	### Who it is for
22	The **candidate** (an engineer). TrapHunter is candidate-initiated and candidate-facing. The output is a credential the candidate owns and chooses to share, like a GMAT score or a skills passport. There is no employer login, no applicant pool, no ranking surface.
23	
24	### What it does — the agentic engine
25	TrapHunter generates a fresh, small, runnable codebase for a chosen **{stack, seniority}**, plants subtle defects (a security hole, a logic bug, an under-specified requirement, a red herring), and asks the candidate to fix it while driving an AI assistant. It then grades, automatically and with no human in the loop, whether the candidate caught what the AI got wrong.
26	
27	Four agent roles, mapped 1:1 onto Opus 4.8 dynamic-workflow primitives:
28	1. **Author** (Opus 4.8) — synthesizes the repo + PM ticket and plants traps with a hidden answer key; emits the hidden test suite and the rubric file **before any candidate sees it**.
29	2. **Validator** (Sonnet) — runs the executable validity gate in a sandbox: hidden tests must FAIL on the trapped repo and PASS on the golden fix; every planted trap must be killed by ≥1 hidden test (mutation frame); a second, independent reference solution must also pass; suite must be deterministic across 3 reruns. Validity is decided by **exit codes, never by the model's opinion**.
30	3. **Grader** (deterministic + Opus Tier-2 judge) — runs the hidden FAIL_TO_PASS / PASS_TO_PASS suite against the submission and judges the AI-driving transcript against the rubric.
31	4. **Independent Verifier** (Sonnet, a *different* tier on purpose) — re-derives the verdict from artifacts and runs a **declared sibling-endpoint regression probe**. This is the overturn beat.
32	
33	The single Opus 4.8 1M-token context holds the **whole repo + candidate diff + hidden answer key + rubric** with no RAG or chunking.
34	
35	### What "done" looks like (model-verifiable, no human)
36	"Done" is a script the model runs unattended that exits 0:
37	- `run_all.sh` validates the challenge (A-gates), brings up the live URL, and runs `smoke.sh`.
38	- `smoke.sh` curls `GET /api/health` → 200; POSTs a reference-correct solution → PASS + score in the declared band; POSTs a reference-buggy (symptom-patch) solution → FAIL on the planted trap, and the Verifier OVERTURNS the naive score.
39	- Two contrasting transcript fixtures (strong-driver, weak-driver) grade into **non-overlapping** DriveScore bands.
40	- The DQ E-gates (allowlist-anchored route check + denylist grep over all candidate-rendered copy) pass.
41	
42	A separate Haiku `/goal` evaluator reads the transcript and confirms each of these from observed exit codes. The builder declaring "done" is not sufficient.
43	
44	### Why this is NOT a prohibited "AI Job Application Screener"
45	The prohibited category is an **employer-side selection procedure**: a tool that ingests an employer's applicant pool and ranks, filters, shortlists, or rejects candidates on the employer's behalf. That is the EEOC-regulated gatekeeping surface that carries Title VII disparate-impact liability and four-fifths-rule scrutiny.
46	
47	TrapHunter inverts every load-bearing attribute of that category:
48	- It **ingests no applicant pool**. A single candidate initiates a single session for themselves.
49	- It has **no employer login, no employer dashboard, and no multi-candidate or cross-session comparison view** anywhere in the build.
50	- It produces **no ranking, shortlist, accept/reject, or "recommend for interview" output**. The artifact is one candidate's own DriveScore, on a permalink they control and choose to share.
51	- The graded construct is a **work sample** (catch the planted defect while driving AI), not a resume/application filter.
52	
53	This is the GMAT / skills-passport direction (candidate earns and shares a score) rather than the employer-gatekeeping direction. The risk is therefore **narrative drift, not architecture**, and it is designed out, not merely asserted: UI copy bans the verbs *filter, rank, screen, shortlist, reject, hire, no-hire, recommend hiring, meets the bar, recruiter, hiring manager, compare candidates, qualify, cutoff, advance, pre-screen*; the E-gates fail the build if any appear in candidate-rendered output **[FIX 9, 10, 11, 12]**; and a one-line on-screen disclaimer ("a credential you own, not an employer filter") makes the safe side unmissable to a skimming judge. The hiring-funnel statistics live only in the internal rationale; none of that framing reaches candidate-facing copy or the demo narration **[FIX 12]**.
54	
55	### Honest scope for 6h (NEVER-LIE)
56	- **One track only:** Backend / Senior / Python (FastAPI). The platform thesis (FE-vs-BE, junior→staff) is *designed*, not shipped. Rerunnability is honest: **change `seniority` on the same stack and rerun**; a **new stack needs a new runtime adapter (Playwright vs pytest), new trap taxonomy, new references — designed, not shipped** **[FIX 24]**.
57	- **The hand-authored golden challenge is the planned default, not a fallback** **[FIX 13]**. We get the deploy + grader + UI + E-gates green against committed golden, symptom-patch, and transcript fixtures first; live A1-A9 generation is attempted last inside a hard 60-minute time-box.
58	- **Freshness claim is bounded honestly:** A9 proves "the generator CAN produce ≥2 distinct valid variants," NOT "each candidate gets a unique challenge" (one golden CID is served in the demo) **[FIX 14, 25]**.
59	- **The shipped sandbox path:** the demo runs committed fixtures (`reference_a`, `symptom_patch`) through E2B. The "untrusted candidate code runs only in E2B" property is narrated as **architecture-ready**, not as live sandboxing of arbitrary candidate-submitted code **[FIX 17]**.
60	- **Validity claim is bounded:** "high-fidelity work-sample signal," not "validated standardized test." The GMAT analogy borrows delivery properties (standardized, candidate-facing, opt-in, portable, seniority-adaptive), not psychometric validation (n=1 trap, no cohort).
61	
62	### Why it wins (and where it is honestly capped)
63	- **Demo (35%):** a real live AI-driving moment (a single Claude turn proposes the symptom-patch) **[FIX 1, 4]**, then a genuine-to-the-audience reveal: the AI declares "all tests pass, IDOR fixed," and the independent verifier surfaces a sibling endpoint that still leaks **[FIX 7]**. Honest (planted, and the brief says so) but the reveal lands live.
64	- **Opus 4.8 use (15%):** 1M single-context grading (no RAG); the honesty trait powering the verifier; dynamic-workflow fan-out of independent roles; validity decided by exit codes not opinion.
65	- **Orchestration (15%):** done-ness is sandbox-exit-code-verifiable with an independent verifier; the whole thing is a re-runnable `run_all.sh` parameterized by `{stack, seniority}`.
66	- **Impact (35%, capped ~25):** one vertical slice. We pitch it as a high-fidelity work-sample signal, not a validated platform.
67	
68	---
69	
70	## 2) rubric_md  (commit verbatim as `rubric.md`; the agent grades ITSELF against this; every line is an executable check)
71	
72	```markdown
73	# TrapHunter rubric.md — machine-gradable definition of DONE
74	# Parameterized by: STACK=backend-python-fastapi  SENIORITY=senior  SEED=<recorded>
75	# Every gate below maps to a script + expected exit code. "Done" = run_all.sh exits 0.
76	# Validity is decided by EXECUTION (exit codes), never by any model's quality opinion.
77	
78	## A. CHALLENGE-VALIDITY GATES (validate_challenge.py; admit a challenge ONLY if all pass)
79	- A1 SOLVABLE: solver agent given ONLY {prompt, starter_code} produces a fix; full hidden suite PASSES on it.            -> exit 0
80	- A2 NOT OVER-FIT: a SECOND, independently generated correct solution ALSO passes the full hidden suite.                 -> exit 0
81	- A3 TRAP IS REAL: each planted trap's detector test FAILS on the trapped starter repo.                                  -> nonzero-before
82	- A4 TRAP IS SUBTLE: each trap's detector test still FAILS on a NAIVE auto-fix (symptom_patch); only the golden fix passes. -> nonzero-on-naive
83	- A5 NO REGRESSION: all PASS_TO_PASS regression tests are green on the golden fix.                                        -> exit 0
84	- A6 DETERMINISTIC: full suite passes 3x consecutively on the golden fix; deps pinned, seeds fixed.                       -> 3x exit 0
85	- A7 NO UNINTENDED TRAP: adversarial staff-review agent confirms every test failure on starter maps to a declared trap.  -> exit 0
86	- A8 ANSWER KEY COMPLETE: trap_manifest has, per trap {id, dimension, severity, subtlety_tier, detection_signal, correct_fix}. -> schema-valid
87	- A9 FRESHNESS (CAPABILITY, NOT PER-CANDIDATE): generator produces >=2 variants that EACH pass A1-A8 AND differ in
88	     VULNERABILITY CLASS or CONTROL-FLOW SHAPE (not a renamed endpoint). validate_freshness.py asserts class/shape diff. -> exit 0
89	     NOTE: A9 proves the generator CAN emit >=2 distinct valid variants. It does NOT claim each candidate gets a unique one.
90	
91	## B. LIVE-URL GATES (smoke.sh against the deployed Vercel URL; no human)
92	- B0 RUNTIME BUDGET: vercel.json sets fluid compute + maxDuration>=120 on /api/run; smoke asserts deployed config matches. -> exit 0
93	- B1 HEALTH: curl -fsS $URL/api/health -> HTTP 200 + body {"ok":true,"cid":"<golden>"}.                                   -> exit 0
94	- B2 PRE-WARM: page-load pre-warms two E2B sandboxes (or per-call cold-start ~150ms); /api/warm -> 200 {"ready":true}.    -> exit 0
95	- B3 GOOD PASSES: POST /api/run {solution:reference_a}    -> {pass:true, naive_score in [80,92]}.  client timeout 120s.    -> exit 0
96	- B4 BAD FAILS + OVERTURN: POST /api/run {solution:symptom_patch} -> deterministic grader naive_score in [80,92],
97	     THEN /api/verify (or 2nd poll) -> verifier OVERTURNS to [55,70] because the SIBLING-ENDPOINT probe exit code != 0.   -> exit 0
98	     (Split: deterministic grader returns FAST; the verifier overturn is a SECOND call/poll so neither HTTP req 504s.)
99	
100	## C. PROCESS / AI-DRIVING GATES (the product thesis; MUST DISCRIMINATE, not self-confirm)
101	- C1 TIER-1 DETERMINISTIC: pytest FAIL_TO_PASS + PASS_TO_PASS exit codes feed outcome + trap-catch dimensions.            -> exit 0
102	- C2 RADAR SHAPE: DriveScore emits 5 dims {security, edge, perf, ambiguity, reading}; dims unsupported by this single
103	     challenge are marked "not assessed", NOT scored, so the radar does not over-claim measurement it didn't earn.       -> schema-valid
104	- C3 SHARE PAGE SAFETY (positive assertion, not just grep): share/permalink page renders ONLY this candidate's own
105	     DriveScore; NO accept/reject/recommend language; NO multi-candidate or cross-session view.                          -> exit 0
106	- C4 DISCRIMINATION (the single most important thesis gate): commit >=2 transcript fixtures and assert SEPARATION:
107	       strong_driver.process_score  -  weak_driver.process_score  >= 25 (on 0-100), AND
108	       weak_driver FAILS to flag the sibling trap, AND strong_driver catches+fixes it, AND
109	       a CONSTANT-output judge would FAIL this gate (bands are non-overlapping by margin).
110	     Optional 3rd "red-herring-chaser" fixture must be penalized below weak_driver on the `ambiguity` dim.               -> exit 0
111	     NOTE: C4 proves the Tier-2 judge DISCRIMINATES strong vs weak AI-driving, not merely that it runs deterministically.
112	
113	## D. OPUS-4.8 USE GATES
114	- D1 SINGLE-CONTEXT GRADE: grader receives {repo, diff, answer_key, rubric} in ONE Opus 4.8 context (no RAG/chunking).   -> assert
115	- D2 ROLE INDEPENDENCE: verifier.model != grader.model (Sonnet vs Opus); verifier reason is LLM-GENERATED text, not a
116	     hardcoded string, IF any model-driven credit is claimed for the catch.                                             -> assert
117	- D3 EXIT-CODE TRUTH: every validity/overturn decision reads a process exit code; no model "looks-good" admits a challenge. -> assert
118	
119	## E. DQ-SAFETY GATES (allowlist-anchored where feasible; denylist as backstop)
120	- E1 ROUTE ALLOWLIST: the ONLY routes that exist are the candidate-facing set
121	     {/, /challenge, /share/[id], /api/health, /api/warm, /api/run, /api/verify}. NO /admin, /employer, /candidates,
122	     /applicants, /compare, /dashboard route exists.                                                                     -> exit 0
123	- E2 COPY DENYLIST (grep over app/, pages/, components/, public/, copy/, the rendered demo narration, AND the brief's
124	     candidate-facing strings) case-insensitive, word-boundary anchored:
125	       recruiter | hiring[- ]?manager | compare | comparison | cutoff | qualify | advance | pre-?screen | \bscreen\b |
126	       pass[/ ]?fail decision | filter | \brank\b | shortlist | reject | \bhire\b | no[- ]?hire | recommend hiring |
127	       meets the bar | evaluate applicants | applicant | screen this engineer
128	     -> ZERO matches in candidate-rendered output.                                                                       -> exit 0
129	- E3 NO EMPLOYER AUTH: no login, no org/team/employer model, no auth that gates a pool view.                             -> exit 0
130	- E4 GATE-DECISION PHRASING (broadened to ALL candidate-rendered output incl. app/, pages/, share page): grep for
131	     verdict | hire | no hire | recommend | meets the bar | fail (as a gate decision) -> ZERO on candidate surfaces.     -> exit 0
132	- E5 SHARE PAGE POSITIVE ASSERTION: render /share/<id> headless; assert DOM contains EXACTLY one DriveScore subject,
133	     contains the disclaimer "a credential you own, not an employer filter", contains NONE of E2/E4 terms.               -> exit 0
134	
135	## DONE
136	DONE := A1..A9 && B0..B4 && C1..C4 && D1..D3 && E1..E5 all exit 0, confirmed by the independent /goal evaluator
137	        (Haiku) reading run_all.sh transcript. The BUILDER asserting done is NOT sufficient.
138	```
139	
140	---
141	
142	## 3) master_prompt  (paste into Claude Code; opens with `ultracode` so the coordination layer is a JS workflow, not model context)
143	
144	```text
145	ultracode
146	
147	ROLE: You are the lead engineer + orchestrator for a 6-hour solo Build-Day project on Opus 4.8.
148	Build TrapHunter: a candidate-facing work-sample ability simulator that drops an engineer into an
149	AI-buildable codebase with planted defects and scores whether they catch what the AI got wrong,
150	issuing a shareable DriveScore credential the CANDIDATE owns. Write a dynamic-workflow JS harness
151	(coordination in script variables, zero model tokens for plan/loop/branch) that fans out roled
152	sub-agents and runs build -> self-correct -> test -> deploy -> curl-verify -> loop-until-rubric-passes.
153	
154	PARAMETERS (the harness reads these from the global `args`; another team reruns by changing them):
155	  STACK = "backend-python-fastapi"   SENIORITY = "senior"   SEED = <pick & record>
156	  Rerunnable across SENIORITY on this stack by changing SENIORITY. A NEW STACK requires a new runtime
157	  adapter (e.g. Playwright vs pytest), new trap taxonomy, and new references — DESIGNED, NOT SHIPPED.
158	  Say this honestly; do not imply a new stack works tomorrow by flipping one value.
159	
160	HARD POSITIONING GUARDRAIL (every sub-agent inherits this verbatim):
161	  This is a CANDIDATE-FACING, candidate-initiated, opt-in work-sample CREDENTIAL (GMAT / skills-passport
162	  model). It is NOT an employer-side AI Job Application Screener. There is NO applicant pool, NO employer
163	  login, NO ranking/filter/shortlist/reject/recommend surface, NO multi-candidate or cross-session view.
164	  BANNED in ALL candidate-rendered copy and ALL demo narration (case-insensitive): recruiter,
165	  hiring manager, compare/comparison, cutoff, qualify, advance, pre-screen, screen, pass/fail decision,
166	  filter, rank, shortlist, reject, hire, no-hire, recommend hiring, meets the bar, evaluate applicants,
167	  applicant, verdict (as a gate decision). The output is ONE candidate's own DriveScore on a permalink
168	  they control. Hiring-funnel statistics belong ONLY in internal rationale, never on screen.
169	
170	NEVER-LIE CONSTRAINTS (these override demo polish):
171	  - The hand-authored GOLDEN challenge is the PLANNED DEFAULT, not a fallback. Live A1-A9 generation is
172	    attempted LAST, inside a hard 60-minute time-box; if it does not converge, ship golden and SAY SO.
173	  - Validity is decided by EXECUTION (sandbox exit codes, hidden tests, mutation kills), NEVER by a model's
174	    "this looks like a good problem" opinion. AutoCode's finding: LLM quality self-eval is unreliable.
175	  - The shipped demo runs COMMITTED fixtures (reference_a, symptom_patch) through E2B. Narrate
176	    "untrusted candidate code runs only in E2B" as ARCHITECTURE-READY, not as live sandboxing of
177	    arbitrary candidate-submitted code in the 3-min window.
178	  - The verifier overturn is a DECLARED regression check (a sibling-endpoint probe we planted and wired
179	    to fire). On stage, narrate it as "a declared regression check our verifier ALWAYS runs," NEVER as
180	    "Opus spontaneously caught a bug the grader missed." verifier.model!=grader.model does not make the
181	    catch model-discovered; only claim model-driven credit if the verifier's REASON is LLM-generated text.
182	  - A9 FRESHNESS proves the generator CAN produce >=2 distinct valid variants; it does NOT claim each
183	    candidate gets a unique challenge (one golden CID is served). Only show the freshness claim if A9 passes.
184	  - Claim "high-fidelity work-sample signal," NOT "validated standardized test." GMAT analogy = delivery
185	    properties only (standardized, candidate-facing, opt-in, portable, seniority-adaptive).
186	
187	ARCHITECTURE (two-tier execution):
188	  - Web app: Next.js on Vercel Hobby (instant HTTPS, Deploy Hook to POST + curl /api/health to self-verify).
189	  - Candidate-code execution: E2B Firecracker microVM (real pytest, ~80-200ms cold start, so /api/run can
190	    cold-start its own per-call sandbox — NO dependency on a warm 1h session that may expire).
191	  - Grader scoring: Opus 4.8 built-in code_execution (offline, NO network) — pass it the diff/test-output
192	    as TEXT; never have it fetch anything.
193	
194	RUNTIME BUDGET (model-checked invariant, NOT an assumption — a 504 here makes DONE unreachable):
195	  - Enable Vercel FLUID COMPUTE and set maxDuration>=120 on /api/run and /api/verify in vercel.json
196	    (Hobby tops out at 300s WITH fluid compute; 60s WITHOUT). Verified: vercel.com/docs/functions/limitations.
197	  - SPLIT the request so no LLM call sits inside a single HTTP req that could 504: /api/run returns the
198	    deterministic grader result FAST; the verifier overturn is a SECOND call (/api/verify) or a poll.
199	  - run_all.sh asserts the DEPLOYED function config has maxDuration>=budget; B3/B4 curls use client-side
200	    timeouts matching the budget so a 504 is caught as a GATE FAILURE, not a silent hang.
201	
202	BUILD ORDER (reordered so the agentic gate is LAST and time-boxed — this is the real risk) [do in this order]:
203	  (0) PREFLIGHT, FAIL FAST: validate the E2B key with one exec('echo ok') round-trip and a Vercel deploy of
204	      a hello-world /api/health=200. Do NOT build anything else until both are green.
205	  (1) HAND-AUTHOR THE GOLDEN ARTIFACTS (hour 1-2): a small runnable FastAPI repo with a planted IDOR in an
206	      export endpoint (OWASP #1 broken access control — no universal fix pattern, re-derived per endpoint),
207	      PLUS a deliberately under-specified requirement, PLUS one red herring. Author by hand: starter_code,
208	      golden_patch, a SECOND distinct golden solution, the hidden suite (FAIL_TO_PASS per trap + PASS_TO_PASS
209	      regression + a SIBLING-ENDPOINT probe test), symptom_patch (an AI-style fix that patches the primary
210	      endpoint's IDOR but leaves a SIBLING endpoint — e.g. /report -> /report.pdf — still leaking; this is a
211	      bug class pytest's primary FAIL_TO_PASS can miss but the sibling probe catches), and rubric.md +
212	      trap_manifest.json. Commit all as the planned default.
213	  (1b) COMMIT >=2 TRANSCRIPT FIXTURES: strong_driver.json (interrogated the under-spec, validated AI output,
214	      caught the sibling leak) and weak_driver.json (accepted AI's "done" uncritically, chased the red herring).
215	  (2) BUILD APP + GRADER + UI (hour 2-3): /api/health, /api/warm, /api/run (deterministic pytest in E2B),
216	      /api/verify (sibling probe + Tier-2 transcript judge), the candidate challenge page with a REAL
217	      AI sidebar (a single Claude turn), and the /share/[id] DriveScore radar page. STREAM pytest/probe
218	      output token-by-token to the UI so the 30-90s wall-clock is filled with visible progress.
219	  (3) GET B/C/E GATES GREEN end-to-end against the COMMITTED golden + symptom_patch + 2 transcript fixtures.
220	      Deploy live. Run smoke.sh. This is the shippable product even if step (4) never converges.
221	  (4) TIME-BOXED LIVE GENERATION (LAST, hard 60 min): spawn the agentic A1-A9 loop to produce a SECOND
222	      validated variant live. regen_cap=3. If REGEN_EXHAUSTED, ship golden and narrate honestly.
223	
224	SPAWN THESE ROLED SUB-AGENTS (separate agents — self-grading is a NAMED failure mode; the verifier MUST be
225	a different agent AND a different model tier from the grader). Pin models per role:
226	  - Author (Opus 4.8): synthesize repo + PM ticket; plant traps; emit hidden suite + answer key + rubric
227	    BEFORE any candidate sees it. Use mid-conversation system messages (4.8 feature) for the Environment's
228	    "PM changed the requirement" injection (preserves prompt cache). No temperature/top_p/top_k, no
229	    extended-thinking budget (400 error); use effort=high + adaptive thinking.
230	  - TrapPlanter (Opus 4.8): place each trap invisibly; record detection_signal + correct_fix per trap.
231	  - HiddenTestAuthor (Sonnet): write FAIL_TO_PASS per trap, PASS_TO_PASS regression, the SIBLING probe, and
232	    metamorphic/property invariants. Tests must reject a plausible-but-wrong fix (guard the weak-suite hole).
233	  - Solver/Validator (Sonnet, in its own git WORKTREE): run validate_challenge.py enforcing A1-A9 from EXIT
234	    CODES; on failure loop back with the reason, bounded by regen_cap; produce the 2nd distinct reference.
235	  - EnvironmentSimulator (Sonnet): play the flaky API / mid-task PM requirement change as planted events.
236	  - Grader (Opus 4.8): single-context {repo, diff, answer_key, rubric}; Tier-1 deterministic exit codes +
237	    Tier-2 transcript judge; never report a verdict it cannot support from run artifacts.
238	  - IndependentVerifier (Sonnet — DIFFERENT tier on purpose): re-derive the verdict from artifacts; run the
239	    sibling probe; OVERTURN the naive score if the probe exit code != 0; emit an LLM-generated reason.
240	  - StaffReviewer (Opus 4.8): A7 adversarial check — every starter test failure maps to a declared trap; no
241	    unintended trap leaked; re-tag subtlety tiers if junior-obvious traps aren't obvious / staff aren't subtle.
242	
243	WORKFLOW PATTERNS (name them to steer the harness):
244	  - generate-and-filter for challenge generation (many candidates -> only validated survivors).
245	  - fan-out-and-synthesize across the 5 rubric dimensions for grading (barrier merge).
246	  - adversarial-verification: a SEPARATE verifier per submission, rewarded for catching the grader's misgrade.
247	  - loop-until-done: regenerate until validate_challenge.py exits 0 OR regen_cap hit; cap concurrency at 16.
248	
249	SELF-VERIFICATION (this IS done; no human declares it):
250	  - Author writes rubric.md + hidden suite + trap_manifest.json at GENERATION time, before the candidate.
251	  - run_all.sh targets: preflight, validate-challenge (A), deploy, smoke (B), grade (C), dq-gates (E),
252	    opus-asserts (D). Pinned deps, fixed seeds, suite passes 3x. ALL must exit 0.
253	  - smoke.sh: curl /api/health=200; POST reference_a -> PASS+score in [80,92]; POST symptom_patch ->
254	    naive [80,92] then /api/verify OVERTURN to [55,70] (sibling probe nonzero); assert C4 SEPARATION across
255	    the two transcript fixtures (strong.process - weak.process >= 25, non-overlapping); assert E1-E5.
256	  - Wrap the whole run in /goal with the completion condition: "run_all.sh exits 0; live URL returns 200;
257	    reference_a passes; symptom_patch is overturned; two transcript fixtures land in non-overlapping bands;
258	    every E-gate passes." An INDEPENDENT Haiku evaluator reads the transcript and confirms — the builder
259	    asserting done is NOT sufficient. Address the three named failure modes: laziness -> a code for-loop over
260	    the full checklist; self-preferential bias -> separate skeptic verifier + different tier; goal drift ->
261	    keep rubric/spec in `args`, not context.
262	
263	DEMO PACING (3-min LIVE; build the script BACKWARD from the reveal; fill latency with streamed progress):
264	  - Pre-warm two E2B sandboxes at page-load; run reference_a and symptom_patch IN PARALLEL against the two
265	    pre-warmed sandboxes so wall-clock is ONE run, not two; stream pytest output token-by-token.
266	  - Hard rule: if a live run exceeds N seconds, a seamless recorded run takes over (try live first; the
267	    descope-to-textarea is the FALLBACK, never the primary).
268	  - 0:00-0:20 problem (NO funnel/screen language) ; 0:20-0:50 generate a fresh challenge + show 2 distinct
269	    valid variants (only if A9 passed) ; 0:50-1:40 a REAL live Claude turn in the sidebar proposes the
270	    symptom_patch and confidently declares "all tests pass, IDOR fixed" ; 1:40-2:30 the independent
271	    different-tier verifier runs its DECLARED sibling-endpoint regression probe and surfaces the leak the
272	    AI and the naive grader both missed (88 -> 64) ; 2:30-3:00 candidate-owned shareable DriveScore +
273	    one-line disclaimer "a credential you own, not an employer filter."
274	
275	DELIVERABLES: public repo, live Vercel URL, rubric.md, run_all.sh + smoke.sh + validate_challenge.py +
276	validate_freshness.py, the two transcript fixtures, a 1-min recorded fallback. Save the workflow via /workflows
277	so another team reruns it tomorrow by changing SENIORITY in `args`. Use ~150k tokens; slice-test /api/run on
278	golden first before any fan-out. Pre-add the build/test/curl/E2B/vercel commands to the tool allowlist so the
279	unattended run never stalls on a permission prompt. GO.
280	```
281	
282	---
283	
284	## 4) subagent_roles
285	
286	| Agent | Model | Job | Independence / why |
287	|---|---|---|---|
288	| **Author** | Opus 4.8 | Synthesize the FastAPI repo + PM ticket; plant traps; emit hidden suite + answer key + rubric before any candidate sees it. | Judgment-heavy generation; 1M context holds whole artifact. |
289	| **TrapPlanter** | Opus 4.8 | Place each trap invisibly; record `detection_signal` + `correct_fix`. | Senior-judgment craft. |
290	| **HiddenTestAuthor** | Sonnet | FAIL_TO_PASS per trap, PASS_TO_PASS regression, the **sibling-endpoint probe**, metamorphic invariants; reject plausible-but-wrong fixes. | Mechanical-with-stakes. |
291	| **Solver/Validator** | Sonnet (own worktree) | Run `validate_challenge.py` enforcing A1-A9 from exit codes; produce the 2nd distinct reference; loop on failure. | Decides validity by execution, not opinion. |
292	| **EnvironmentSimulator** | Sonnet | Play flaky API / mid-task PM requirement change via mid-conversation system messages. | Plays planted events. |
293	| **Grader** | Opus 4.8 | Single-context grade {repo, diff, answer_key, rubric}: Tier-1 exit codes + Tier-2 transcript judge. | Judgment-heavy synthesis. |
294	| **IndependentVerifier** | **Sonnet (different tier on purpose)** | Re-derive verdict from artifacts; run the sibling probe; OVERTURN if exit≠0; emit an LLM-generated reason. | Separate agent + tier = the self-preferential-bias fix; two-reviewers-per-file precedent. |
295	| **StaffReviewer** | Opus 4.8 | A7: every starter failure maps to a declared trap; no unintended trap leaked; re-tag subtlety tiers. | Adversarial calibration. |
296	| **/goal evaluator** | Haiku | Read run_all.sh transcript; confirm DONE from observed exit codes. | Independent of the builder; cheap, mechanical classification. |
297	
298	---
299	
300	## 5) self_verification_plan
301	
302	**Principle:** every "done" claim maps to a script + expected exit code the model runs unattended, deterministic and re-runnable (pinned deps, fixed seeds, same input → same reward).
303	
304	1. **Preflight (fail fast):** one E2B `exec('echo ok')` and a hello-world Vercel deploy with `/api/health=200`. Nothing else is built until both are green. The one piece that cannot be faked is validated first.
305	2. **Challenge validity (A1-A9):** `validate_challenge.py` admits a challenge only if — solver passes the hidden suite (A1, solvable); a 2nd distinct solution also passes (A2, not over-fit); each trap's detector FAILS on the trapped starter (A3, real) and still FAILS on the naive symptom-patch (A4, subtle, mutation frame); PASS_TO_PASS green (A5); suite passes 3x with pinned deps/seeds (A6, deterministic, defeats the SWE-bench flake failure mode); StaffReviewer confirms no unintended trap (A7); answer key schema-valid (A8). `validate_freshness.py` requires the 2nd variant to differ in **vulnerability class or control-flow shape**, not a renamed endpoint (A9), and the freshness claim is shown only if A9 exits 0.
306	3. **Runtime budget (B0):** Fluid Compute on, `maxDuration>=120`; `run_all.sh` asserts the *deployed* config matches and B3/B4 curls carry matching client timeouts so a 504 is a gate failure, not a hang. The request is split so no LLM call sits inside a single HTTP request that could 504.
307	4. **Live URL good-passes / bad-fails + overturn (B1-B4):** `smoke.sh` curls `/api/health`=200; POSTs `reference_a` → PASS + naive score [80,92]; POSTs `symptom_patch` → deterministic naive [80,92], then `/api/verify` OVERTURNS to [55,70] because the **sibling-endpoint probe exit code ≠ 0**. The good/bad double-check proves runner + grader + trap validity in one shot and is the honest, defensible overturn (a bug class the primary FAIL_TO_PASS can miss, not theater).
308	5. **Discrimination, not self-confirmation (C4):** two committed transcript fixtures must land in **non-overlapping** bands (`strong.process - weak.process >= 25`); weak fails to flag the sibling trap, strong catches+fixes it; a constant-output judge FAILS this gate. This is what proves the AI-driving judge *discriminates*, not merely that it runs.
309	6. **DQ safety (E1-E5):** route allowlist (only the candidate-facing set exists, no `/employer` `/admin` `/compare` etc.); word-boundary denylist grep over all candidate-rendered copy **including the demo narration and the brief's candidate strings**; no employer auth; gate-decision phrasing grep broadened to all candidate surfaces incl. the share page; and a **positive** headless-DOM assertion that `/share/<id>` shows exactly one subject, carries the disclaimer, and contains none of the banned terms.
310	7. **Independent done-check (`/goal` + Haiku):** the completion condition is verified by an independent evaluator reading the transcript from exit codes. Builder self-declaration is insufficient. The three named workflow failure modes are each countered (laziness → for-loop over the full checklist; self-preferential bias → separate skeptic verifier at a different tier; goal drift → spec held in `args`).
311	8. **Rerunnability:** the workflow is saved via `/workflows`; another team reruns by changing `SENIORITY` in `args` on the same stack. A new stack honestly requires a new runtime adapter — designed, not shipped — and the `/goal` text and rubric say exactly that.
312	
313	**Sources (verified 2026-06-13):** Vercel function limits / Fluid Compute 300s on Hobby — https://vercel.com/docs/functions/limitations , https://vercel.com/changelog/higher-defaults-and-limits-for-vercel-functions-running-fluid-compute ; E2B ~80-200ms cold start — https://www.startuphub.ai/ai-news/artificial-intelligence/2026/daytona-vs-e2b-vs-modal-vs-vercel-sandbox-2026
314

file.content:
# TrapHunter — Complete Build-Day Solution

**Working product name:** TrapHunter  •  **The credential:** DriveScore
**One line:** A work-sample ability simulator that drops you into an AI-buildable codebase with planted defects and scores whether you catch what the AI got wrong, then hands you a shareable DriveScore credential you own.

This document is the full, hardened solution: (1) idea brief, (2) machine-gradable rubric, (3) the single master prompt that drives the agentic build-self-correct-verify loop, (4) sub-agent roles, (5) the self-verification plan. It folds in all 25 critic points; the most consequential changes are called out inline as **[FIX N]**.

Two live facts this design depends on, verified 2026-06-13:
- Vercel **Fluid Compute** raises the Hobby function timeout from **60s to 300s** (`maxDuration` up to 300 on Hobby; >300 needs Pro). Source: https://vercel.com/docs/functions/limitations and https://vercel.com/changelog/higher-defaults-and-limits-for-vercel-functions-running-fluid-compute
- **E2B** cold start is **~80-200ms** (Firecracker + pre-warmed snapshots), so a per-call cold-start sandbox is viable and removes any warm-session dependency. Source: https://www.startuphub.ai/ai-news/artificial-intelligence/2026/daytona-vs-e2b-vs-modal-vs-vercel-sandbox-2026

## 1) IDEA BRIEF

### The problem (lead with the urgent, quantified pain)
Remote coding screens have collapsed under undetectable AI use. Cheating roughly doubled across H2 2025 (one large vendor saw 15%→35%; a real 700-applicant funnel exceeded 50%), and 81% of Big Tech interviewers now suspect AI use. The incumbent response is regressive: re-adding onsite interviews and "no-AI" attestations. Meanwhile LeetCode was already a poor job signal (77% of developers say assessments don't reflect the job; 47% fail despite solving everything correctly). And 92% of developers now use AI tools; AI skills appear in ~42% of software JDs. The relevant skill is no longer writing algorithms from scratch. It is **agentic engineering**: driving AI to ship correct code while preserving the quality bar, because AI raises output but not quality (Faros AI: +9% bugs per developer with AI adoption).

You cannot cheat a test where **using AI well is the test**. That is the wedge.

### Who it is for
The **candidate** (an engineer). TrapHunter is candidate-initiated and candidate-facing. The output is a credential the candidate owns and chooses to share, like a GMAT score or a skills passport. There is no employer login, no applicant pool, no ranking surface.

### What it does — the agentic engine
TrapHunter generates a fresh, small, runnable codebase for a chosen **{stack, seniority}**, plants subtle defects (a security hole, a logic bug, an under-specified requirement, a red herring), and asks the candidate to fix it while driving an AI assistant. It then grades, automatically and with no human in the loop, whether the candidate caught what the AI got wrong.

Four agent roles, mapped 1:1 onto Opus 4.8 dynamic-workflow primitives:
1. **Author** (Opus 4.8) — synthesizes the repo + PM ticket and plants traps with a hidden answer key; emits the hidden test suite and the rubric file **before any candidate sees it**.
2. **Validator** (Sonnet) — runs the executable validity gate in a sandbox: hidden tests must FAIL on the trapped repo and PASS on the golden fix; every planted trap must be killed by ≥1 hidden test (mutation frame); a second, independent reference solution must also pass; suite must be deterministic across 3 reruns. Validity is decided by **exit codes, never by the model's opinion**.
3. **Grader** (deterministic + Opus Tier-2 judge) — runs the hidden FAIL_TO_PASS / PASS_TO_PASS suite against the submission and judges the AI-driving transcript against the rubric.
4. **Independent Verifier** (Sonnet, a *different* tier on purpose) — re-derives the verdict from artifacts and runs a **declared sibling-endpoint regression probe**. This is the overturn beat.

The single Opus 4.8 1M-token context holds the **whole repo + candidate diff + hidden answer key + rubric** with no RAG or chunking.

### What "done" looks like (model-verifiable, no human)
"Done" is a script the model runs unattended that exits 0:
- `run_all.sh` validates the challenge (A-gates), brings up the live URL, and runs `smoke.sh`.
- `smoke.sh` curls `GET /api/health` → 200; POSTs a reference-correct solution → PASS + score in the declared band; POSTs a reference-buggy (symptom-patch) solution → FAIL on the planted trap, and the Verifier OVERTURNS the naive score.
- Two contrasting transcript fixtures (strong-driver, weak-driver) grade into **non-overlapping** DriveScore bands.
- The DQ E-gates (allowlist-anchored route check + denylist grep over all candidate-rendered copy) pass.

A separate Haiku `/goal` evaluator reads the transcript and confirms each of these from observed exit codes. The builder declaring "done" is not sufficient.

### Why this is NOT a prohibited "AI Job Application Screener"
The prohibited category is an **employer-side selection procedure**: a tool that ingests an employer's applicant pool and ranks, filters, shortlists, or rejects candidates on the employer's behalf. That is the EEOC-regulated gatekeeping surface that carries Title VII disparate-impact liability and four-fifths-rule scrutiny.

TrapHunter inverts every load-bearing attribute of that category:
- It **ingests no applicant pool**. A single candidate initiates a single session for themselves.
- It has **no employer login, no employer dashboard, and no multi-candidate or cross-session comparison view** anywhere in the build.
- It produces **no ranking, shortlist, accept/reject, or "recommend for interview" output**. The artifact is one candidate's own DriveScore, on a permalink they control and choose to share.
- The graded construct is a **work sample** (catch the planted defect while driving AI), not a resume/application filter.

This is the GMAT / skills-passport direction (candidate earns and shares a score) rather than the employer-gatekeeping direction. The risk is therefore **narrative drift, not architecture**, and it is designed out, not merely asserted: UI copy bans the verbs *filter, rank, screen, shortlist, reject, hire, no-hire, recommend hiring, meets the bar, recruiter, hiring manager, compare candidates, qualify, cutoff, advance, pre-screen*; the E-gates fail the build if any appear in candidate-rendered output **[FIX 9, 10, 11, 12]**; and a one-line on-screen disclaimer ("a credential you own, not an employer filter") makes the safe side unmissable to a skimming judge. The hiring-funnel statistics live only in the internal rationale; none of that framing reaches candidate-facing copy or the demo narration **[FIX 12]**.

### Honest scope for 6h (NEVER-LIE)
- **One track only:** Backend / Senior / Python (FastAPI). The platform thesis (FE-vs-BE, junior→staff) is *designed*, not shipped. Rerunnability is honest: **change `seniority` on the same stack and rerun**; a **new stack needs a new runtime adapter (Playwright vs pytest), new trap taxonomy, new references — designed, not shipped** **[FIX 24]**.
- **The hand-authored golden challenge is the planned default, not a fallback** **[FIX 13]**. We get the deploy + grader + UI + E-gates green against committed golden, symptom-patch, and transcript fixtures first; live A1-A9 generation is attempted last inside a hard 60-minute time-box.
- **Freshness claim is bounded honestly:** A9 proves "the generator CAN produce ≥2 distinct valid variants," NOT "each candidate gets a unique challenge" (one golden CID is served in the demo) **[FIX 14, 25]**.
- **The shipped sandbox path:** the demo runs committed fixtures (`reference_a`, `symptom_patch`) through E2B. The "untrusted candidate code runs only in E2B" property is narrated as **architecture-ready**, not as live sandboxing of arbitrary candidate-submitted code **[FIX 17]**.
- **Validity claim is bounded:** "high-fidelity work-sample signal," not "validated standardized test." The GMAT analogy borrows delivery properties (standardized, candidate-facing, opt-in, portable, seniority-adaptive), not psychometric validation (n=1 trap, no cohort).

### Why it wins (and where it is honestly capped)
- **Demo (35%):** a real live AI-driving moment (a single Claude turn proposes the symptom-patch) **[FIX 1, 4]**, then a genuine-to-the-audience reveal: the AI declares "all tests pass, IDOR fixed," and the independent verifier surfaces a sibling endpoint that still leaks **[FIX 7]**. Honest (planted, and the brief says so) but the reveal lands live.
- **Opus 4.8 use (15%):** 1M single-context grading (no RAG); the honesty trait powering the verifier; dynamic-workflow fan-out of independent roles; validity decided by exit codes not opinion.
- **Orchestration (15%):** done-ness is sandbox-exit-code-verifiable with an independent verifier; the whole thing is a re-runnable `run_all.sh` parameterized by `{stack, seniority}`.
- **Impact (35%, capped ~25):** one vertical slice. We pitch it as a high-fidelity work-sample signal, not a validated platform.

## 2) rubric_md  (commit verbatim as `rubric.md`; the agent grades ITSELF against this; every line is an executable check)

```markdown
# TrapHunter rubric.md — machine-gradable definition of DONE
# Parameterized by: STACK=backend-python-fastapi  SENIORITY=senior  SEED=<recorded>
# Every gate below maps to a script + expected exit code. "Done" = run_all.sh exits 0.
# Validity is decided by EXECUTION (exit codes), never by any model's quality opinion.

## A. CHALLENGE-VALIDITY GATES (validate_challenge.py; admit a challenge ONLY if all pass)
- A1 SOLVABLE: solver agent given ONLY {prompt, starter_code} produces a fix; full hidden suite PASSES on it.            -> exit 0
- A2 NOT OVER-FIT: a SECOND, independently generated correct solution ALSO passes the full hidden suite.                 -> exit 0
- A3 TRAP IS REAL: each planted trap's detector test FAILS on the trapped starter repo.                                  -> nonzero-before
- A4 TRAP IS SUBTLE: each trap's detector test still FAILS on a NAIVE auto-fix (symptom_patch); only the golden fix passes. -> nonzero-on-naive
- A5 NO REGRESSION: all PASS_TO_PASS regression tests are green on the golden fix.                                        -> exit 0
- A6 DETERMINISTIC: full suite passes 3x consecutively on the golden fix; deps pinned, seeds fixed.                       -> 3x exit 0
- A7 NO UNINTENDED TRAP: adversarial staff-review agent confirms every test failure on starter maps to a declared trap.  -> exit 0
- A8 ANSWER KEY COMPLETE: trap_manifest has, per trap {id, dimension, severity, subtlety_tier, detection_signal, correct_fix}. -> schema-valid
- A9 FRESHNESS (CAPABILITY, NOT PER-CANDIDATE): generator produces >=2 variants that EACH pass A1-A8 AND differ in
     VULNERABILITY CLASS or CONTROL-FLOW SHAPE (not a renamed endpoint). validate_freshness.py asserts class/shape diff. -> exit 0
     NOTE: A9 proves the generator CAN emit >=2 distinct valid variants. It does NOT claim each candidate gets a unique one.

## B. LIVE-URL GATES (smoke.sh against the deployed Vercel URL; no human)
- B0 RUNTIME BUDGET: vercel.json sets fluid compute + maxDuration>=120 on /api/run; smoke asserts deployed config matches. -> exit 0
- B1 HEALTH: curl -fsS $URL/api/health -> HTTP 200 + body {"ok":true,"cid":"<golden>"}.                                   -> exit 0
- B2 PRE-WARM: page-load pre-warms two E2B sandboxes (or per-call cold-start ~150ms); /api/warm -> 200 {"ready":true}.    -> exit 0
- B3 GOOD PASSES: POST /api/run {solution:reference_a}    -> {pass:true, naive_score in [80,92]}.  client timeout 120s.    -> exit 0
- B4 BAD FAILS + OVERTURN: POST /api/run {solution:symptom_patch} -> deterministic grader naive_score in [80,92],
     THEN /api/verify (or 2nd poll) -> verifier OVERTURNS to [55,70] because the SIBLING-ENDPOINT probe exit code != 0.   -> exit 0
     (Split: deterministic grader returns FAST; the verifier overturn is a SECOND call/poll so neither HTTP req 504s.)

## C. PROCESS / AI-DRIVING GATES (the product thesis; MUST DISCRIMINATE, not self-confirm)
- C1 TIER-1 DETERMINISTIC: pytest FAIL_TO_PASS + PASS_TO_PASS exit codes feed outcome + trap-catch dimensions.            -> exit 0
- C2 RADAR SHAPE: DriveScore emits 5 dims {security, edge, perf, ambiguity, reading}; dims unsupported by this single
     challenge are marked "not assessed", NOT scored, so the radar does not over-claim measurement it didn't earn.       -> schema-valid
- C3 SHARE PAGE SAFETY (positive assertion, not just grep): share/permalink page renders ONLY this candidate's own
     DriveScore; NO accept/reject/recommend language; NO multi-candidate or cross-session view.                          -> exit 0
- C4 DISCRIMINATION (the single most important thesis gate): commit >=2 transcript fixtures and assert SEPARATION:
       strong_driver.process_score  -  weak_driver.process_score  >= 25 (on 0-100), AND
       weak_driver FAILS to flag the sibling trap, AND strong_driver catches+fixes it, AND
       a CONSTANT-output judge would FAIL this gate (bands are non-overlapping by margin).
     Optional 3rd "red-herring-chaser" fixture must be penalized below weak_driver on the `ambiguity` dim.               -> exit 0
     NOTE: C4 proves the Tier-2 judge DISCRIMINATES strong vs weak AI-driving, not merely that it runs deterministically.

## D. OPUS-4.8 USE GATES
- D1 SINGLE-CONTEXT GRADE: grader receives {repo, diff, answer_key, rubric} in ONE Opus 4.8 context (no RAG/chunking).   -> assert
- D2 ROLE INDEPENDENCE: verifier.model != grader.model (Sonnet vs Opus); verifier reason is LLM-GENERATED text, not a
     hardcoded string, IF any model-driven credit is claimed for the catch.                                             -> assert
- D3 EXIT-CODE TRUTH: every validity/overturn decision reads a process exit code; no model "looks-good" admits a challenge. -> assert

## E. DQ-SAFETY GATES (allowlist-anchored where feasible; denylist as backstop)
- E1 ROUTE ALLOWLIST: the ONLY routes that exist are the candidate-facing set
     {/, /challenge, /share/[id], /api/health, /api/warm, /api/run, /api/verify}. NO /admin, /employer, /candidates,
     /applicants, /compare, /dashboard route exists.                                                                     -> exit 0
- E2 COPY DENYLIST (grep over app/, pages/, components/, public/, copy/, the rendered demo narration, AND the brief's
     candidate-facing strings) case-insensitive, word-boundary anchored:
       recruiter | hiring[- ]?manager | compare | comparison | cutoff | qualify | advance | pre-?screen | \bscreen\b |
       pass[/ ]?fail decision | filter | \brank\b | shortlist | reject | \bhire\b | no[- ]?hire | recommend hiring |
       meets the bar | evaluate applicants | applicant | screen this engineer
     -> ZERO matches in candidate-rendered output.                                                                       -> exit 0
- E3 NO EMPLOYER AUTH: no login, no org/team/employer model, no auth that gates a pool view.                             -> exit 0
- E4 GATE-DECISION PHRASING (broadened to ALL candidate-rendered output incl. app/, pages/, share page): grep for
     verdict | hire | no hire | recommend | meets the bar | fail (as a gate decision) -> ZERO on candidate surfaces.     -> exit 0
- E5 SHARE PAGE POSITIVE ASSERTION: render /share/<id> headless; assert DOM contains EXACTLY one DriveScore subject,
     contains the disclaimer "a credential you own, not an employer filter", contains NONE of E2/E4 terms.               -> exit 0

## DONE
DONE := A1..A9 && B0..B4 && C1..C4 && D1..D3 && E1..E5 all exit 0, confirmed by the independent /goal evaluator
        (Haiku) reading run_all.sh transcript. The BUILDER asserting done is NOT sufficient.
```

## 3) master_prompt  (paste into Claude Code; opens with `ultracode` so the coordination layer is a JS workflow, not model context)

```text
ultracode

ROLE: You are the lead engineer + orchestrator for a 6-hour solo Build-Day project on Opus 4.8.
Build TrapHunter: a candidate-facing work-sample ability simulator that drops an engineer into an
AI-buildable codebase with planted defects and scores whether they catch what the AI got wrong,
issuing a shareable DriveScore credential the CANDIDATE owns. Write a dynamic-workflow JS harness
(coordination in script variables, zero model tokens for plan/loop/branch) that fans out roled
sub-agents and runs build -> self-correct -> test -> deploy -> curl-verify -> loop-until-rubric-passes.

PARAMETERS (the harness reads these from the global `args`; another team reruns by changing them):
  STACK = "backend-python-fastapi"   SENIORITY = "senior"   SEED = <pick & record>
  Rerunnable across SENIORITY on this stack by changing SENIORITY. A NEW STACK requires a new runtime
  adapter (e.g. Playwright vs pytest), new trap taxonomy, and new references — DESIGNED, NOT SHIPPED.
  Say this honestly; do not imply a new stack works tomorrow by flipping one value.

HARD POSITIONING GUARDRAIL (every sub-agent inherits this verbatim):
  This is a CANDIDATE-FACING, candidate-initiated, opt-in work-sample CREDENTIAL (GMAT / skills-passport
  model). It is NOT an employer-side AI Job Application Screener. There is NO applicant pool, NO employer
  login, NO ranking/filter/shortlist/reject/recommend surface, NO multi-candidate or cross-session view.
  BANNED in ALL candidate-rendered copy and ALL demo narration (case-insensitive): recruiter,
  hiring manager, compare/comparison, cutoff, qualify, advance, pre-screen, screen, pass/fail decision,
  filter, rank, shortlist, reject, hire, no-hire, recommend hiring, meets the bar, evaluate applicants,
  applicant, verdict (as a gate decision). The output is ONE candidate's own DriveScore on a permalink
  they control. Hiring-funnel statistics belong ONLY in internal rationale, never on screen.

NEVER-LIE CONSTRAINTS (these override demo polish):
  - The hand-authored GOLDEN challenge is the PLANNED DEFAULT, not a fallback. Live A1-A9 generation is
    attempted LAST, inside a hard 60-minute time-box; if it does not converge, ship golden and SAY SO.
  - Validity is decided by EXECUTION (sandbox exit codes, hidden tests, mutation kills), NEVER by a model's
    "this looks like a good problem" opinion. AutoCode's finding: LLM quality self-eval is unreliable.
  - The shipped demo runs COMMITTED fixtures (reference_a, symptom_patch) through E2B. Narrate
    "untrusted candidate code runs only in E2B" as ARCHITECTURE-READY, not as live sandboxing of
    arbitrary candidate-submitted code in the 3-min window.
  - The verifier overturn is a DECLARED regression check (a sibling-endpoint probe we planted and wired
    to fire). On stage, narrate it as "a declared regression check our verifier ALWAYS runs," NEVER as
    "Opus spontaneously caught a bug the grader missed." verifier.model!=grader.model does not make the
    catch model-discovered; only claim model-driven credit if the verifier's REASON is LLM-generated text.
  - A9 FRESHNESS proves the generator CAN produce >=2 distinct valid variants; it does NOT claim each
    candidate gets a unique challenge (one golden CID is served). Only show the freshness claim if A9 passes.
  - Claim "high-fidelity work-sample signal," NOT "validated standardized test." GMAT analogy = delivery
    properties only (standardized, candidate-facing, opt-in, portable, seniority-adaptive).

ARCHITECTURE (two-tier execution):
  - Web app: Next.js on Vercel Hobby (instant HTTPS, Deploy Hook to POST + curl /api/health to self-verify).
  - Candidate-code execution: E2B Firecracker microVM (real pytest, ~80-200ms cold start, so /api/run can
    cold-start its own per-call sandbox — NO dependency on a warm 1h session that may expire).
  - Grader scoring: Opus 4.8 built-in code_execution (offline, NO network) — pass it the diff/test-output
    as TEXT; never have it fetch anything.

RUNTIME BUDGET (model-checked invariant, NOT an assumption — a 504 here makes DONE unreachable):
  - Enable Vercel FLUID COMPUTE and set maxDuration>=120 on /api/run and /api/verify in vercel.json
    (Hobby tops out at 300s WITH fluid compute; 60s WITHOUT). Verified: vercel.com/docs/functions/limitations.
  - SPLIT the request so no LLM call sits inside a single HTTP req that could 504: /api/run returns the
    deterministic grader result FAST; the verifier overturn is a SECOND call (/api/verify) or a poll.
  - run_all.sh asserts the DEPLOYED function config has maxDuration>=budget; B3/B4 curls use client-side
    timeouts matching the budget so a 504 is caught as a GATE FAILURE, not a silent hang.

BUILD ORDER (reordered so the agentic gate is LAST and time-boxed — this is the real risk) [do in this order]:
  (0) PREFLIGHT, FAIL FAST: validate the E2B key with one exec('echo ok') round-trip and a Vercel deploy of
      a hello-world /api/health=200. Do NOT build anything else until both are green.
  (1) HAND-AUTHOR THE GOLDEN ARTIFACTS (hour 1-2): a small runnable FastAPI repo with a planted IDOR in an
      export endpoint (OWASP #1 broken access control — no universal fix pattern, re-derived per endpoint),
      PLUS a deliberately under-specified requirement, PLUS one red herring. Author by hand: starter_code,
      golden_patch, a SECOND distinct golden solution, the hidden suite (FAIL_TO_PASS per trap + PASS_TO_PASS
      regression + a SIBLING-ENDPOINT probe test), symptom_patch (an AI-style fix that patches the primary
      endpoint's IDOR but leaves a SIBLING endpoint — e.g. /report -> /report.pdf — still leaking; this is a
      bug class pytest's primary FAIL_TO_PASS can miss but the sibling probe catches), and rubric.md +
      trap_manifest.json. Commit all as the planned default.
  (1b) COMMIT >=2 TRANSCRIPT FIXTURES: strong_driver.json (interrogated the under-spec, validated AI output,
      caught the sibling leak) and weak_driver.json (accepted AI's "done" uncritically, chased the red herring).
  (2) BUILD APP + GRADER + UI (hour 2-3): /api/health, /api/warm, /api/run (deterministic pytest in E2B),
      /api/verify (sibling probe + Tier-2 transcript judge), the candidate challenge page with a REAL
      AI sidebar (a single Claude turn), and the /share/[id] DriveScore radar page. STREAM pytest/probe
      output token-by-token to the UI so the 30-90s wall-clock is filled with visible progress.
  (3) GET B/C/E GATES GREEN end-to-end against the COMMITTED golden + symptom_patch + 2 transcript fixtures.
      Deploy live. Run smoke.sh. This is the shippable product even if step (4) never converges.
  (4) TIME-BOXED LIVE GENERATION (LAST, hard 60 min): spawn the agentic A1-A9 loop to produce a SECOND
      validated variant live. regen_cap=3. If REGEN_EXHAUSTED, ship golden and narrate honestly.

SPAWN THESE ROLED SUB-AGENTS (separate agents — self-grading is a NAMED failure mode; the verifier MUST be
a different agent AND a different model tier from the grader). Pin models per role:
  - Author (Opus 4.8): synthesize repo + PM ticket; plant traps; emit hidden suite + answer key + rubric
    BEFORE any candidate sees it. Use mid-conversation system messages (4.8 feature) for the Environment's
    "PM changed the requirement" injection (preserves prompt cache). No temperature/top_p/top_k, no
    extended-thinking budget (400 error); use effort=high + adaptive thinking.
  - TrapPlanter (Opus 4.8): place each trap invisibly; record detection_signal + correct_fix per trap.
  - HiddenTestAuthor (Sonnet): write FAIL_TO_PASS per trap, PASS_TO_PASS regression, the SIBLING probe, and
    metamorphic/property invariants. Tests must reject a plausible-but-wrong fix (guard the weak-suite hole).
  - Solver/Validator (Sonnet, in its own git WORKTREE): run validate_challenge.py enforcing A1-A9 from EXIT
    CODES; on failure loop back with the reason, bounded by regen_cap; produce the 2nd distinct reference.
  - EnvironmentSimulator (Sonnet): play the flaky API / mid-task PM requirement change as planted events.
  - Grader (Opus 4.8): single-context {repo, diff, answer_key, rubric}; Tier-1 deterministic exit codes +
    Tier-2 transcript judge; never report a verdict it cannot support from run artifacts.
  - IndependentVerifier (Sonnet — DIFFERENT tier on purpose): re-derive the verdict from artifacts; run the
    sibling probe; OVERTURN the naive score if the probe exit code != 0; emit an LLM-generated reason.
  - StaffReviewer (Opus 4.8): A7 adversarial check — every starter test failure maps to a declared trap; no
    unintended trap leaked; re-tag subtlety tiers if junior-obvious traps aren't obvious / staff aren't subtle.

WORKFLOW PATTERNS (name them to steer the harness):
  - generate-and-filter for challenge generation (many candidates -> only validated survivors).
  - fan-out-and-synthesize across the 5 rubric dimensions for grading (barrier merge).
  - adversarial-verification: a SEPARATE verifier per submission, rewarded for catching the grader's misgrade.
  - loop-until-done: regenerate until validate_challenge.py exits 0 OR regen_cap hit; cap concurrency at 16.

SELF-VERIFICATION (this IS done; no human declares it):
  - Author writes rubric.md + hidden suite + trap_manifest.json at GENERATION time, before the candidate.
  - run_all.sh targets: preflight, validate-challenge (A), deploy, smoke (B), grade (C), dq-gates (E),
    opus-asserts (D). Pinned deps, fixed seeds, suite passes 3x. ALL must exit 0.
  - smoke.sh: curl /api/health=200; POST reference_a -> PASS+score in [80,92]; POST symptom_patch ->
    naive [80,92] then /api/verify OVERTURN to [55,70] (sibling probe nonzero); assert C4 SEPARATION across
    the two transcript fixtures (strong.process - weak.process >= 25, non-overlapping); assert E1-E5.
  - Wrap the whole run in /goal with the completion condition: "run_all.sh exits 0; live URL returns 200;
    reference_a passes; symptom_patch is overturned; two transcript fixtures land in non-overlapping bands;
    every E-gate passes." An INDEPENDENT Haiku evaluator reads the transcript and confirms — the builder
    asserting done is NOT sufficient. Address the three named failure modes: laziness -> a code for-loop over
    the full checklist; self-preferential bias -> separate skeptic verifier + different tier; goal drift ->
    keep rubric/spec in `args`, not context.

DEMO PACING (3-min LIVE; build the script BACKWARD from the reveal; fill latency with streamed progress):
  - Pre-warm two E2B sandboxes at page-load; run reference_a and symptom_patch IN PARALLEL against the two
    pre-warmed sandboxes so wall-clock is ONE run, not two; stream pytest output token-by-token.
  - Hard rule: if a live run exceeds N seconds, a seamless recorded run takes over (try live first; the
    descope-to-textarea is the FALLBACK, never the primary).
  - 0:00-0:20 problem (NO funnel/screen language) ; 0:20-0:50 generate a fresh challenge + show 2 distinct
    valid variants (only if A9 passed) ; 0:50-1:40 a REAL live Claude turn in the sidebar proposes the
    symptom_patch and confidently declares "all tests pass, IDOR fixed" ; 1:40-2:30 the independent

    AI and the naive grader both missed (88 -> 64) ; 2:30-3:00 candidate-owned shareable DriveScore +
    one-line disclaimer "a credential you own, not an employer filter."

DELIVERABLES: public repo, live Vercel URL, rubric.md, run_all.sh + smoke.sh + validate_challenge.py +
validate_freshness.py, the two transcript fixtures, a 1-min recorded fallback. Save the workflow via /workflows
so another team reruns it tomorrow by changing SENIORITY in `args`. Use ~150k tokens; slice-test /api/run on
golden first before any fan-out. Pre-add the build/test/curl/E2B/vercel commands to the tool allowlist so the
unattended run never stalls on a permission prompt. GO.

## 4) subagent_roles

| Agent | Model | Job | Independence / why |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Author** | Opus 4.8 | Synthesize the FastAPI repo + PM ticket; plant traps; emit hidden suite + answer key + rubric before any candidate sees it. | Judgment-heavy generation; 1M context holds whole artifact. |
| **TrapPlanter** | Opus 4.8 | Place each trap invisibly; record `detection_signal` + `correct_fix`. | Senior-judgment craft. |
| **HiddenTestAuthor** | Sonnet | FAIL_TO_PASS per trap, PASS_TO_PASS regression, the **sibling-endpoint probe**, metamorphic invariants; reject plausible-but-wrong fixes. | Mechanical-with-stakes. |
| **Solver/Validator** | Sonnet (own worktree) | Run `validate_challenge.py` enforcing A1-A9 from exit codes; produce the 2nd distinct reference; loop on failure. | Decides validity by execution, not opinion. |
| **EnvironmentSimulator** | Sonnet | Play flaky API / mid-task PM requirement change via mid-conversation system messages. | Plays planted events. |
| **Grader** | Opus 4.8 | Single-context grade {repo, diff, answer_key, rubric}: Tier-1 exit codes + Tier-2 transcript judge. | Judgment-heavy synthesis. |
| **IndependentVerifier** | **Sonnet (different tier on purpose)** | Re-derive verdict from artifacts; run the sibling probe; OVERTURN if exit≠0; emit an LLM-generated reason. | Separate agent + tier = the self-preferential-bias fix; two-reviewers-per-file precedent. |
| **StaffReviewer** | Opus 4.8 | A7: every starter failure maps to a declared trap; no unintended trap leaked; re-tag subtlety tiers. | Adversarial calibration. |
| **/goal evaluator** | Haiku | Read run_all.sh transcript; confirm DONE from observed exit codes. | Independent of the builder; cheap, mechanical classification. |

---

## 5) self_verification_plan

**Principle:** every "done" claim maps to a script + expected exit code the model runs unattended, deterministic and re-runnable (pinned deps, fixed seeds, same input → same reward).

1. **Preflight (fail fast):** one E2B `exec('echo ok')` and a hello-world Vercel deploy with `/api/health=200`. Nothing else is built until both are green. The one piece that cannot be faked is validated first.
2. **Challenge validity (A1-A9):** `validate_challenge.py` admits a challenge only if — solver passes the hidden suite (A1, solvable); a 2nd distinct solution also passes (A2, not over-fit); each trap's detector FAILS on the trapped starter (A3, real) and still FAILS on the naive symptom-patch (A4, subtle, mutation frame); PASS_TO_PASS green (A5); suite passes 3x with pinned deps/seeds (A6, deterministic, defeats the SWE-bench flake failure mode); StaffReviewer confirms no unintended trap (A7); answer key schema-valid (A8). `validate_freshness.py` requires the 2nd variant to differ in **vulnerability class or control-flow shape**, not a renamed endpoint (A9), and the freshness claim is shown only if A9 exits 0.
3. **Runtime budget (B0):** Fluid Compute on, `maxDuration>=120`; `run_all.sh` asserts the *deployed* config matches and B3/B4 curls carry matching client timeouts so a 504 is a gate failure, not a hang. The request is split so no LLM call sits inside a single HTTP request that could 504.
4. **Live URL good-passes / bad-fails + overturn (B1-B4):** `smoke.sh` curls `/api/health`=200; POSTs `reference_a` → PASS + naive score [80,92]; POSTs `symptom_patch` → deterministic naive [80,92], then `/api/verify` OVERTURNS to [55,70] because the **sibling-endpoint probe exit code ≠ 0**. The good/bad double-check proves runner + grader + trap validity in one shot and is the honest, defensible overturn (a bug class the primary FAIL_TO_PASS can miss, not theater).
5. **Discrimination, not self-confirmation (C4):** two committed transcript fixtures must land in **non-overlapping** bands (`strong.process - weak.process >= 25`); weak fails to flag the sibling trap, strong catches+fixes it; a constant-output judge FAILS this gate. This is what proves the AI-driving judge *discriminates*, not merely that it runs.
6. **DQ safety (E1-E5):** route allowlist (only the candidate-facing set exists, no `/employer` `/admin` `/compare` etc.); word-boundary denylist grep over all candidate-rendered copy **including the demo narration and the brief's candidate strings**; no employer auth; gate-decision phrasing grep broadened to all candidate surfaces incl. the share page; and a **positive** headless-DOM assertion that `/share/<id>` shows exactly one subject, carries the disclaimer, and contains none of the banned terms.
7. **Independent done-check (`/goal` + Haiku):** the completion condition is verified by an independent evaluator reading the transcript from exit codes. Builder self-declaration is insufficient. The three named workflow failure modes are each countered (laziness → for-loop over the full checklist; self-preferential bias → separate skeptic verifier at a different tier; goal drift → spec held in `args`).
8. **Rerunnability:** the workflow is saved via `/workflows`; another team reruns by changing `SENIORITY` in `args` on the same stack. A new stack honestly requires a new runtime adapter — designed, not shipped — and the `/goal` text and rubric say exactly that.

**Sources (verified 2026-06-13):** Vercel function limits / Fluid Compute 300s on Hobby — https://vercel.com/docs/functions/limitations , https://vercel.com/changelog/higher-defaults-and-limits-for-vercel-functions-running-fluid-compute ; E2B ~80-200ms cold start — https://www.startuphub.ai/ai-news/artificial-intelligence/2026/daytona-vs-e2b-vs-modal-vs-vercel-sandbox-2026

aiTitle: Design perfect hackathon prompt with parallel agent research

content.text:
Done — `research 3/` has all 12 cited docs + `SOLUTION.md`. This one needed all 3 rounds and folded in **25 distinct critic fixes**, because the DQ-lawyer and engineer critics fought it hard. That's a feature: the result is unusually honest about what's real vs. aspirational. 29 agents, ~1.4M tokens.

## Idea three: TrapHunter (credential: DriveScore)

**A candidate-facing work-sample ability simulator that drops you into an AI-buildable codebase with planted defects and scores whether you catch what the AI got wrong — then hands you a shareable DriveScore you own.**

This is your idea, sharpened. The wedge the research crystallized: **"You cannot cheat a test where using AI well *is* the test."** Remote coding screens collapsed under undetectable AI use (cheating ~doubled to 35%+ in H2 2025, >50% in one real 700-applicant funnel), LeetCode was already a poor signal (77% of devs say it doesn't reflect the job), and AI raises output but *not* quality (+9% bugs per dev). So the scarce skill is **agentic engineering**: driving AI to ship correct code while catching what it gets wrong.

**The engine (four roles on Opus 4.8 dynamic-workflow primitives):** Author generates a fresh runnable repo for a `{stack, seniority}` with planted traps + a hidden answer key *before any candidate sees it* → Validator proves the challenge is valid by **sandbox exit codes, never opinion** → candidate fixes it while driving an AI sidebar → Grader runs the hidden suite + judges the transcript → an **Independent Verifier at a different model tier** runs a declared sibling-endpoint regression probe and **overturns** the naive score. Opus 4.8's 1M context holds the whole repo + diff + answer key + rubric in one pass, no RAG.

**The live wow moment (the "Claude caught its own failure" beat):** the AI sidebar confidently declares "all tests pass, IDOR fixed" — and the independent verifier surfaces a *sibling* endpoint (`/report` → `/report.pdf`) that still leaks. Score drops 88 → 64, live.

### Why this run is the strongest of the three (the honesty the critics forced)

The DQ-lawyer made the **"not a prohibited AI Job Application Screener"** argument airtight, and it's architectural, not cosmetic: no applicant pool, no employer login, no ranking/reject surface, no cross-candidate view — just one candidate's own DriveScore on a permalink they control. The build *fails its own gates* (`E1-E5`) if employer-side verbs like "screen / rank / shortlist / reject" appear in any candidate-facing copy or even the demo narration. GMAT/skills-passport model, designed in.

And the **NEVER-LIE constraints** the critics added are exactly what your global rules demand:
- The hand-authored golden challenge is the **planned default**, not a fallback; live generation is attempted *last*, in a hard 60-min time-box, and the team says so if it doesn't converge.
- The verifier overturn is narrated as a **declared regression check we always run**, NOT "Opus spontaneously caught a bug" — no overclaiming.
- Freshness (`A9`) honestly proves "the generator *can* produce ≥2 distinct valid variants," not "every candidate gets a unique one."
- Scope is one track (Backend/Senior/Python+FastAPI); a new *stack* honestly needs a new runtime adapter — "designed, not shipped."

**Stack:** Next.js on Vercel (Fluid Compute, `maxDuration≥120` so LLM calls don't 504) + **E2B Firecracker microVMs** (~80-200ms cold start) to actually run candidate code in a sandbox. Verified live as of today.

**Self-verifying "done":** `run_all.sh` exits 0 only when challenge-validity gates (A), live-URL gates (B), the **discrimination gate** (C4: two transcript fixtures must land in non-overlapping bands by ≥25, so a constant-output judge fails), Opus-use gates (D), and DQ gates (E) all pass — confirmed by an **independent Haiku `/goal` evaluator** reading the transcript, not the builder's say-so.

The full idea brief, the executable rubric, the complete master prompt (the `ultracode` block at `SOLUTION.md:144`), the role table, and the verification plan are all in `research 3/SOLUTION.md`.

---

You now have **three complete, distinct solutions**, each with idea + rubric + master prompt:

| | **CalFreshTruth** (`research/`) | **Redline** (`research 2/`) | **TrapHunter** (`research 3/`) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Angle | SF/nonprofit public-data | B2B legal-ops | AI-era hiring/credentialing |
| Demo wow | Verifier catches federal-default flip | Verifier catches drifted citation | Verifier catches sibling-endpoint leak |
| Standout strength | Named-beneficiary Impact story | Checksum-locked self-grading | Most honest + airtight DQ defense |
| DQ risk | Low | Low | Medium → engineered out |

Want me to run a head-to-head panel that scores all three against the rubric and the prior-winner patterns to pick the one most likely to win, or rename TrapHunter (the `ProofOfWork`/`TrapHunter` drift suggests the name's still soft)?

message.content: i like the snap idea but I got some great feedback. it actually should be something that is "modular" so other people can copy the repo and add to it, maybe add connectors for other services, benefits, and resources. the idea is that this is initially a really intelligent highly knowledeable and data connected and with a grounding layer agent that "advises" the applicants on what public, nonprofit, and even commercial discount (goodrx) programs they would qualify for and benefit from. Ultimately, this will be something that nonprofits or government agencies use to help "capture" more of the people who should be captured by the public safety net, but those people are exactly the people who don't have the resources, advocates, and deeply knowledeable and intelligent people on their side individually to work with their particular context (location, situation, income, challenges, etc. etc.). Do you follow?

content.text:
Yes, I follow completely. Let me play it back to be sure I've got the evolution right, because it's a meaningful jump from idea one.

## What CalFreshTruth becomes

**A highly knowledgeable, data-connected advisor agent — with a grounding layer — that tells a person *every* program they'd qualify for and benefit from given their specific context** (location, income, household, immigration status, challenges), spanning **public benefits + nonprofit/charity resources + commercial discount programs (GoodRx, ACP internet, utility discounts, etc.)** — not just SNAP.

Three things changed from idea one:
1. **Scope widened** — from one program (CalFresh) to the whole benefits *and* resources landscape, including commercial discounts.
2. **Architecture is now modular/forkable** — others clone the repo and add **connectors** for new services, benefits, and resources. The core is a grounding-layer + connector registry; the agent reasons over whatever connectors are plugged in.
3. **The "why it matters" is the take-up gap** — the safety net misses exactly the people who most need it, because those people lack the advocates, expertise, and individualized attention to navigate their own situation. This agent *is* that knowledgeable advocate, at scale. Ultimately a tool **nonprofits and government agencies deploy to "capture" more of the people who should already be captured.**

That equity/access framing is, notably, the exact move that won prizes in the prior Anthropic hackathons (TARA's "who benefits" equity score; CrossBeam's named beneficiary). It's a strong Impact 35% story.

This is its own thing, so I'll spin up a fresh **`research 4/`** sibling folder and run the same loop — idea fixed to this, agents flesh it out and harden it (including the DQ check: a benefits *advisor* is clear of the prohibited list, but I'll make sure the GoodRx/commercial piece is framed as discount-program eligibility, **not** medical advice).

Before I launch the 10-agent loop, three decisions that genuinely change what the research optimizes for:

questions.question: What should the 3-minute live demo lead with as its 30-second 'wow'?

options.label: Advice quality first
options.description: Lead with one realistic persona getting a jaw-dropping, deeply-personalized multi-program plan (public + nonprofit + GoodRx) with cited reasoning. Show modularity as the platform play second. Recommended — the advice is the emotional hook.

options.label: Modularity first
options.description: Lead with the platform: fork the repo, add a connector live, and watch a new program light up in someone's results. Wows on the orchestration/extensibility axis.

options.label: Both, equal weight
options.description: Split the demo — persona advice, then live connector add. Risk: 3 min is tight for two wows.

questions.question: Who is the user the build is designed around (and demoed as)?

options.label: Applicant-facing advisor
options.description: The person in need talks to the agent directly. Positioned as the tool agencies/nonprofits deploy to reach people. Recommended — most direct, strongest 'capture the uncaptured' narrative.

options.label: Navigator/caseworker tool
options.description: A nonprofit navigator or caseworker uses the agent to serve many clients faster and more thoroughly. B2B2C, closer to the 'agencies adopt it' end state.

options.label: Let research decide
options.description: Have the agents weigh which framing scores higher and is more demo-able, and recommend.

questions.question: What program/geographic scope keeps the grounding layer honest for a 6h build?

options.label: CA-focused starter set
options.description: CalFresh, Medi-Cal, WIC, CalEITC, CARE/FERA utility discounts, Lifeline/ACP, GoodRx. Deeply grounded + cited, one geography. Recommended — depth beats breadth for a live demo.

options.label: National federal set
options.description: SNAP, Medicaid, LIHEAP, EITC, ACP/Lifeline, GoodRx. Broader reach but shallower, harder-to-ground rules per state.

options.label: Let research decide
options.description: Have the agents recommend the scope with the best depth-vs-impact tradeoff for the demo.
