# Decision-grade brief — AI eligibility + benefits navigation (provided by Aza, 2026-06-13)

> Source: Aza's own venture-grade research brief. Saved verbatim-in-substance as grounding.
> Citations [n] refer to Aza's source list (not reproduced exhaustively here).

## 1. Executive summary
- **Biggest opportunity:** the under-enrollment problem is real and large; California is a good wedge. USDA estimated national SNAP participation at **88% of eligible people (FY2022)** — ~12% of eligible people not reached; CA has historically lagged national participation [39][40].
- **Biggest risk:** this is NOT mainly a chatbot UX problem. It's a **rules, trust, liability, and distribution** problem. Incumbents already provide screeners, directories, call centers, portals, closed-loop referrals. A new entrant must be materially more accurate, fresher, better distributed than BenefitsCal, 211, findhelp, mRelief, SingleStop, Propel, health-plan SDOH platforms.
- **CalFresh is genuinely hard to self-assess:** household composition, gross & net income, deductions, elderly/disabled status, immigration status, student rules, ABAWD/work requirements, and post-H.R.1 2026 changes [17]–[22].
- **The "before paperwork" wedge is valuable but dangerous.** Product must say **"likely eligible / likely not / unclear — apply or talk to a navigator,"** NOT "you qualify / you do not." Official eligibility is a county/state determination [18][19].
- **The "stacking" insight is strong.** One household intake can model likely eligibility for Medi-Cal, WIC, CARE/FERA, Lifeline, CalEITC, school meals, SUN Bucks — many share FPL thresholds or categorical eligibility from SNAP/Medicaid [23]–[29].
- **Rx-savings add-on is commercially interesting but compliance-sensitive.** GoodRx-style price compare, manufacturer copay cards, PAPs, NeedyMeds/RxAssist, SIRUM, Cost Plus Drugs — matchable from a med list, but avoid clinical substitution advice; careful around federal anti-kickback and government-insured exclusions [30]–[35].
- **Voice useful for accessibility, but OUTBOUND voice verification is legally fraught.** FCC: AI-generated voices fall under TCPA artificial/prerecorded restrictions; CA has AI-voice disclosure requirements. Inbound user voice is easier [41][42].
- **Crowdsourcing local/offline resources is plausible but not a moat alone.** The hard part isn't collecting entries — it's sustained verification, trust scoring, moderation, liability, contributor retention [36]–[38][40][43].
- **AEO / agent-to-agent is directionally real but not yet defensible.** MCP/APIs/schemas/citations make it agent-callable, but "being cited by AI" isn't a durable moat without proprietary freshness, provenance, contracts, distribution [44]–[48].
- **Best venture framing:** NOT "TurboTax for benefits," NOT "AI 211." Stronger = **benefits eligibility intelligence + verified local resource graph + agent-callable API**, starting consumer-facing to learn, monetizing via health plans, providers, government outreach, employers, agent platforms.
- **Bear case:** consumer distribution is weak (low WTP, high privacy sensitivity, episodic use). May become a high-impact nonprofit/public-interest tool or a feature inside Propel/BenefitsCal/findhelp/211 rather than venture-scale.

## 2. Existing landscape (crowded; white space = accurate multi-program pre-eligibility + verified hyperlocal resources + agent-callable canonical data)
| Product | What they do | Eligibility upfront? | $ est? | Open? | AI-native? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Benefits.gov / USA.gov Benefit Finder | Federal benefit discovery | No (potential list) | No | Gov | No |
| mRelief | SNAP screener + application support | Yes (SNAP) | No | Nonprofit | light |
| Code for America / GetCalFresh | CA CalFresh assister — **sunset as app path 2025**, shifted to outreach around BenefitsCal | App assist | No | Open | No |
| BenefitsCal | Official CA portal (CalFresh, Medi-Cal, CalWORKs) | App only | No | Gov (buggy) | No |
| findhelp (Aunt Bertha) | National social-care directory, closed-loop referrals, APIs | No | No | Closed/commercial | workflow |
| 211 / United Way | Phone/text/web navigation; National 211 Data Platform | Human-judged | No | Regional | No |
| Propel / Providers | EBT balance, benefit updates, savings, offers; 5M+ users | n/a | n/a | Consumer fintech | some |
| SingleStop | Screener across 20+ benefits, case mgmt (findhelp map) | Yes | No | B2B | No |
| Unite Us | Closed-loop referral platform (health/gov/community) | screening/referral | No | Enterprise | enterprise |
| NCOA BenefitsCheckUp | Benefit discovery for older adults/disabled | Yes (narrow demo) | No | Nonprofit | No |
| Link Health / LEO | AI benefits-enrollment chatbot + remote specialists (healthcare channel) | Yes | No | Nonprofit/healthcare | Yes |
| Nava Labs | AI tools for navigators (B2G/B2NGO, caseworker-assist) | n/a | n/a | PBC/gov | navigator-first |
| MyFriendBen | Multi-benefit screener | Yes | **Yes** | Open | — |
| PolicyEngine | Eligibility/benefit ENGINE (not consumer) | n/a | **Yes** | Open | — |
| SNAP Screener / calculators | State SNAP calc + AI chatbot disclaimers | Some | est | Consumer web | some |

**Non-obvious structural shift:** GetCalFresh helped 6.2M people access $12.8B in food aid, but **sunset its application-assister path in 2025**; official path is now BenefitsCal. Creates a temporary **user-education gap**: still a need for friendly pre-application explanation, but the official application path changed.

## 3. CalFresh-specific complexity (the wedge — but pure-LLM determination is too risky)
The user's intuitive "do I make too much?" is the WRONG abstraction. Real logic: Who's in the SNAP household? What income counts? Which deductions? Elderly/disabled? Student? Immigrant in a newly affected category? ABAWD/work-requirement time-limits? County/state implementation phase?
- CA uses a higher gross-income screen for many households via **broad-based/modified categorical eligibility** — LA County: most households gross income ≤ **200% FPL** [17]. But SNAP also uses a **net-income** concept after deductions (earned-income, standard, dependent-care, child-support, medical, shelter) [18]. Elderly/disabled have special rules: excess medical over $35, shelter deductions [21].
- **2026 rule changes (H.R.1):** CDSS implemented changes affecting lawfully-present immigrants from **April 1, 2026**, and work/community-engagement changes from **June 1, 2026** [19]. Work requirements: adults 18–64, no dependent child under 14, able to work 20 hrs/week, not otherwise excused [20]. **A static explainer will be WRONG unless date-aware and status-aware.**
- **Required input classes:** household composition; geography (county/ZIP/homelessness); income (earned/unearned/freq/self-employment/excluded); expenses (rent, utilities, dependent care, child support, medical for elderly/disabled); assets; immigration; student status; work requirements; existing benefits (Medi-Cal/CalWORKs/SSI/WIC/TANF/housing/school meals); application posture (new vs renewal).
- **Product implication: a RULES ENGINE with an LLM conversation layer, NOT an LLM "reasoning" from policy prose.** (Nava/Digital Benefits Network draw this line: AI for interpret/summarize; strict formulas/rules-as-code for determination.)

## 4. The "stacking" insight (strongest consumer value: "here is the benefit STACK to check next, why, and what evidence you need")
| Program | Cross-eligibility mechanism | Model from shared intake |
|---|---|---|
| Medi-Cal | Adult Medi-Cal ~138% FPL [23] | size, MAGI income, age, pregnancy, disability, immigration |
| WIC | Medicaid/SNAP/TANF recipients may be income-eligible [24] | pregnancy/postpartum, kids <5, SNAP/Medi-Cal status |
| CARE/FERA utility | Income or program participation; FERA 18% electric for families just above CARE [25] | utility, size, income, existing benefits |
| Lifeline / CA LifeLine | Income ≤135% FPL or SNAP/Medicaid participation [26] | phone/internet need, income, participation |
| CalEITC / federal EITC | Earned income, filing, age, kids; 2025 CalEITC up to **$3,756** for earnings ≤$32,900 [27] | earned income, age, kids, filing, ITIN/SSN |
| Free/reduced school meals | Direct cert via SNAP; **130% FPL free, 185% reduced** [29] | child enrollment, income, SNAP/CalWORKs/Medi-Cal |
| SUN Bucks / Summer EBT | Kids on CalFresh/CalWORKs (some Medi-Cal) auto-eligible [28] | child age/school, CalFresh/CalWORKs/Medi-Cal |

**Approach:** one intake → a `household_context` object → run **modular program rules** against it. Output per program: **likely/possibly/likely-not eligible, why, missing data, evidence/documents needed, next action — with a citation to the specific rule/official page.**

## 5. Prescription / medication savings (converts "navigation" into "cash savings now"; moves into healthcare compliance)
| Option | How | Who qualifies | Limitation |
|---|---|---|---|
| GoodRx | Cash-price/coupon compare across pharmacies [30] | cash payers / vs insurance | not insurance; may not count to deductible; lead-gen economics |
| Manufacturer copay cards | Reduce commercial-insurance copays | commercially insured | OIG anti-kickback warnings; **exclude Medicare/Medicaid/federal** [31][32] |
| PAPs | Manufacturer/nonprofit free/low-cost meds | uninsured/underinsured, income-qualified | paperwork-heavy, renewals |
| NeedyMeds / RxAssist | Directories of savings/PAP programs [33][34] | broad/provider-assisted | directory, not enrollment |
| SIRUM | Redistributes surplus medicine via charity/pharmacy | safety-net via partners | depends on partner inventory |
| Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drugs | Transparent cash-pay generics [35] | available generics, mail/partner | limited formulary |

**Safe agent behavior:** ask med name/dose/qty/ZIP/insurance/pharmacy → show **non-clinical cost pathways** ("cash coupon may be cheaper," "copay card may be available if commercially insured," "PAP may be relevant if uninsured," "Cost Plus has/doesn't have this generic," "ask your prescriber/pharmacist before changing"). **Must NOT** recommend switching/stopping meds or "equivalent" alternatives outside a licensed-clinician workflow; avoid steering interpretable as remuneration for federal-program referrals.

## 6. Data sources (useful structured data, but NO complete canonical source)
- **Open Referral HSDS** — machine-readable directory standard [36] (standard, not a dataset).
- **HSDA API** — 211-style orgs/locations/services/contacts [37] (not all 211s expose it).
- **United Way / 211 National Data Platform** — aggregates local 211 directories [38] (access restricted).
- **USDA FNS data** — participation/benefit aggregates [39] (not user-level eligibility).
- **State portals / BenefitsCal / CDSS** — official app + policy [5][19][20] (rules in PDFs/manuals, not machine-readable).
- **findhelp** — large proprietary network/APIs [6][7] (proprietary; possible competitor/supplier).
- **Offline gap:** very local informal resources (church pantries, PTA funds, mutual aid, diaper banks); availability ("open today," "has diapers," "line at 8am"); soft eligibility (languages, ID requirements, whether immigration status is asked). Gap is **data volatility**, not just "not online."

## 7. Crowdsourcing + verification (recurring failure modes)
Staleness; spam/self-promotion; liability; contributor churn; uneven geography; trust asymmetry. Mitigations: expiration dates + confidence scores + re-verification cadence; contributor reputation + moderation + provenance; "verified as of" timestamps + human escalation; trusted local captains/org partnerships; paid verification + CBO partnerships.
- **Voice-agent verification:** attractive (call a pantry for hours/eligibility/availability) but legally (TCPA AI-voice [41]; CA AI-voice disclosure [42]), socially, and quality-fraught. Est. fully-loaded verified-listing update **$0.50–$3 simple, $5–$20+ complex/failed** (low confidence).

## 8. Agent-to-agent / AEO (real direction, not yet a moat)
Build: public API (`/screen/calfresh`, `/programs/search`, `/resources/verify`, `/rx/savings-paths`); **MCP server** exposing eligibility screening, resource lookup, citation retrieval, confidence scoring [44][45]; HSDS/Open-Referral-compatible objects + schema.org/JSON-LD + canonical URLs (`verified_at`, `source`, `confidence`, `eligibility_basis`); citations on every answer; tool trust (descriptions, versioning, tests, rate limits, audit logs, safe fallback). **Bear case:** tool descriptions/selection are fragile [46]–[48]; "canonical benefits source" defensible only with proprietary freshness + accuracy benchmarks + distribution.

## 9. Risks & regulation (posture decides whether credible partners engage)
- **False denial** → never say "not eligible" without uncertainty + appeal/apply guidance.
- **False hope** → explain missing data + official-determination limits.
- **Privacy/chilling effects** (immigrant/low-income; 2026 SNAP data-sharing fights [51][52]) → data minimization, anonymous mode, no PII sale, clear deletion, encryption, privacy-by-default.
- **Unauthorized practice of law** → legal *information*, not advice; route edge cases to legal aid.
- **Medical advice / Rx steering** → cost info only; no substitution; pharmacist/prescriber language.
- **Anti-kickback** → avoid remuneration tied to federal-program utilization; counsel review before Rx monetization [32].
- **Accessibility** (low literacy, disability, non-English, voice-first, low bandwidth = core, not edge) → WCAG, plain language, multilingual, phone/SMS, human handoff.
- **AI governance** (HHS public-benefits AI guidance: privacy, due process, civil rights, lifecycle risk, human-centered) [53] → auditable rules, citations, monitoring, bias tests, contestability.
- **Posture: eligibility EDUCATION and NAVIGATION — not official determination, legal advice, or medical advice.**

## 10. Market & impact (for a consumer VC)
- SNAP serves tens of millions; FY2022 participation **88%** [39]. CA ~**5.47M** SNAP participants FY2025 (~13.9% of population) [54]. CA attractive: large, rules changing (2026), known UX transition (GetCalFresh→BenefitsCal).
- **Business models:** B2G/gov outreach (impact fit, slow procurement); **health-plan/provider SDOH contracts (strongest near-term payer)**; B2B agent API (high-margin, immature market); Rx affiliate (compliance/trust limits); consumer subscription (weak); employer/financial-wellness (crowded).
- **Comps:** Propel (habitual EBT utility → venture-backable low-income consumer); findhelp/Unite Us (B2B social-care data/referral monetization); mRelief/Code for America (huge impact, often nonprofit/public-sector funding); GoodRx (consumer Rx savings large but compliance/privacy-bound).
- **Bear case:** where's the repeat behavior? Eligibility & pantry lookup are episodic; Rx more repeatable but crowded. Needs a habitual surface ("money-saving copilot for low-income households") OR a B2B/API buyer.

## 11. What would have to be true
| Assumption | Status | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| Users confused before applying; value a conversational "likely eligible" answer | Supported | High |
| Chatbot/voice beats forms for low-literacy/overwhelmed users | Mixed (test it) | Medium |
| Company maintains fresher local data than 211/findhelp | Mixed | Medium |
| AEO/agent-callability becomes a distribution moat | Mixed/unsupported as moat | Medium |
| Rx savings subsidizes acquisition without trust/compliance harm | Mixed | Medium |
| Venture-scale path beyond nonprofit/public-sector | Mixed | Medium |

## 12. Open questions
Conversion lift vs static checklist? Accuracy benchmark vs county/navigator determinations on 500–1,000 historical anonymized cases? Voice trust (Spanish/Cantonese/Tagalog/Vietnamese/low-literacy)? Verification economics per resource? Contributor supply after novelty fades? B2B buyer willingness if already licensing findhelp/Unite Us/211? Privacy trust for mixed-status households in 2026? Compliant neutral Rx routing?

message.content:
# Claude Build Day — Judge Strategy Brief (FULL OFFICIAL PANEL) — v2
**Date:** 2026-06-13 · Supersedes `2026-06-13-judge-strategy-brief.md` (assumed Sara + Aza Ali; **Aza Ali is NOT on the official panel**).
**Project:** benefits-eligibility tool (SNAP/CalFresh → benefit stack → Rx savings; chat + voice; agent-to-agent API "AEO"; crowdsourced + phone-verified local data)

---

## THE HEADLINE FINDING
**Six of ten judges are deep agent-orchestration / verifiable-done / evals people — and one of them literally founded a voice-agent EVALS company.** This panel is almost perfectly matched to your project's surface area, which means extraordinary resonance *and* an extraordinarily high bar on the exact things you're building (voice agents, agent-to-agent, verification). Build for substance; the room can smell a thin wrapper instantly.

The builder bloc:
- **Alistair Smith** — Bun + Claude Code (the harness/runtime; typed tool contracts; dynamic-workflow subagents)
- **Gagan Bhat** — Claude Managed Agents (agent infra: state, sandboxing, tracing, scoped permissions)
- **Marius Buleandra** — Applied AI; **ex-co-founder/CTO of Hamming AI = automated VOICE-AGENT testing & evals**
- **Conner Phillippi** — Claude Code agent teams (multi-agent orchestration) + auto mode (permissions/safety) + hand-crafted evals
- **Ciro Spaciari** — co-leads Claude Code + Cowork (multi-agent orchestration, systems/perf)
- **Zhen Li** — creator of Replit Agent (agent reliability, decision-time guidance, eval-driven)

The investor bloc (leans technical/operator, not pure growth):
- **James Da Costa** (a16z) — ex-fintech founder for the unbanked (Fingo, Mandala voice/SMS); wrote a16z's "Voice AI Will Change How We Bank" + "How Will My Agent Pay" (agent-to-agent). **Most on-thesis VC you could draw.**
- **Sara Deshpande** (Maven) — consumer-health; "billing/pricing, access to pharmaceuticals" is her named territory.
- **Pavla Bobosikova** (Neo) — ex-founder (WFHomie, acq'd); product/people-first; "verify don't hallucinate" instinct.
- **Mar Hershenson** (Pear VC) — PhD EE + serial founder; technical VC. *[profile pending]*

---

## THE UNIVERSAL PATTERN (what to optimize for)
These showed up across nearly EVERY judge, builder and VC alike:

1. **"How do you KNOW it's correct?" is the #1 question from all 10.** Verifiable-done isn't just 15% of the rubric here — it's the panel's shared obsession. Wrong eligibility = real harm to a vulnerable user.
2. **"Why Opus 4.8 vs. a cheaper model or a rules engine?"** Every Anthropic judge + Zhen + Gagan will ask. Winning answer = **model routing**: deterministic rules engine does the eligibility math; Opus does only the load-bearing reasoning (reconciling messy/conflicting program rules, parsing unstructured PAP/copay prose, navigating a live phone-tree dialogue). Saying "we reserve Opus for the hard part and use cheap models elsewhere" signals engineering maturity.
3. **Thin-wrapper allergy.** Do NOT lead with "chat interface." Chat/voice is the surface; the orchestration + verification is the product. Eligibility math = rules engine; the model = reasoning/orchestration.
4. **Don't sprawl — one deep, verified path beats ten shallow integrations.** James, Zhen, Gagan, Ciro, Conner each said it independently. Anchor on CalFresh end-to-end; mention SNAP-stack + Rx + crowdsourcing as one roadmap line.
5. **Bring evals as a deliverable.** Marius, Conner, Zhen explicitly. Even a small labeled test-persona set with an accuracy number is the single highest-leverage trust unlock with this specific panel.
6. **The voice phone-verification loop is your hero — but it must be LIVE, not staged.** Everyone loves it; **Marius (voice-eval founder) and Ciro/Zhen (perf) will catch a mock instantly** and will ask your failure modes (mis-hear, dropped call, dead number, hostile IVR).
7. **AEO / agent-to-agent splits the room.** Builders (Gagan, Conner, Alistair, James) find it native and will probe the actual schema/auth/handoff — show a real typed protocol with a live A2A call. VCs (Sara, Pavla) read it as buzzword — to them keep it a one-line "and it's agent-queryable" kicker. **Demo it for the builders; downplay it for the VCs.**
8. **Distribution / who-pays** matters only to the VCs (Sara, Pavla, James) — have one crisp sentence (food banks / Medicaid managed-care plans paid to enroll people; GoodRx affiliate). Builders don't care.

---

## THE WINNING PLAY (one build, two framings)
**Build:** deterministic CalFresh rules engine + Opus-as-orchestrator/reasoner + a real eval harness (test personas w/ accuracy) + LIVE voice phone-verification with explicit failure handling + a genuinely typed AEO interface. Wire in one "Claude catches & fixes its own failure" moment (the org explicitly asks to see this).

**Demo (35%):** ONE CalFresh path, end-to-end, live, fast, ending on a verified result + a dollar figure. Show the voice agent place a real call and flip a listing to `verified`. Show the eval set passing.

**Round-1 video + repo:** lead with the orchestration + the self-verifying loop + evals passing; functional prototype > docs; public repo, "built today" delineation, live URL.

**Round-2 pitch (3 min, one slide):** 30 sec human impact + before/after metric + one distribution line → the working orchestration + verification (the spine) → one load-bearing Opus-4.8 moment → AEO as a real-schema kicker. Then field the universal Q&A.

**Universal Q&A to rehearse verbatim:** (1) correctness/eval + error rate + low-confidence fallback to human; (2) why-Opus = routing; (3) voice failure modes; (4) AEO protocol/auth; (5) who-pays/distribution; (6) what's real-today vs roadmap.

---

## PROFILES

### Sara Deshpande — GP, Maven Ventures (consumer-health VC)
This problem is her named territory ("billing/pricing," "access to pharmaceuticals"; AI that makes "life better for everyday consumers"). **Rewards:** real distribution/CAC (her #1 filter), habitual usage, massive growing problem, blunt honesty ("tough love"). **Tech lens:** skeptical AI is a moat — 2026: *"saying 'AI' or 'Agentic' will raise more questions than applause… when building takes less time, commoditization takes less time."* Consumer-experience-first. **Loves:** the problem; consumer chat/voice; phone-verification; underserved population. **Skeptical:** AI-wrapper; retention (eligibility is once/twice); distribution/CAC; A2A-as-B2B-off-thesis. **Emphasize:** distribution channel · proprietary phone-verified data as the non-commoditizable moat · recurring-value loop. **De-emphasize:** orchestration/Opus internals as the story; AEO as centerpiece.

### James Da Costa — Partner, a16z (fintech + consumer)  ★ most on-thesis VC
Ex-founder for the unbanked: **Fingo** (pan-African digital bank, "240 seconds to full financial services"), **Mandala** (voice/SMS tools on brick phones in rural India/Kenya). Stanford HAI (economics of AI). Wrote a16z's **"Voice AI Will Change How We Bank"** (multilingual, off-hours, patient agents for the underserved; domain knowledge + compliance + anti-spoofing verification required) and **"How Will My Agent Pay for Things?"** (agent-to-agent transactions; identity/scope/authorization gaps). **Rewards:** encyclopedic founder-market knowledge; relentlessness; agents that autonomously do the drudgery *with a metric*; surprising traction. **Loves:** mission = his career; multilingual voice for underserved; eligibility-before-paperwork w/ a number; phone-verification; AEO (his published thesis). **Skeptical (sharpest blade = verification):** how do you know it's right / not spoofed; distribution + who pays; breadth-over-depth; benefits-advice compliance. **Emphasize:** founder-market depth on the benefits maze · live multilingual/off-hours voice moment · verifiable-done (rules check + confirmed call). **De-emphasize:** feature sprawl; AEO as headline (he'll interrogate the identity/standards gaps he documented — lead with impact).

### Pavla Bobosikova — Investor / Head of Programs, Neo
Recently-exited founder (WFHomie, people-analytics SaaS, acq'd by GoCo; before that Product lead at Nudge.ai). Product/people-native, **not deeply technical**. Domain fit is thin (her world is future-of-work/HR), so explain stakes plainly. **Rewards:** founder obsession ("even when they close their laptop…"); resilience; **real customer validation** (did you interview actual CalFresh applicants?); speed/shipping. **Loves:** mission + underserved user; "verify don't hallucinate"; data-into-action shape; a live real demo. **Skeptical:** trust/accuracy when wrong; AEO as solution-looking-for-problem; distribution/who-pays. **Emphasize:** real-user grounding + stakes · phone-verification as the centerpiece · founder obsession + what you shipped in a day. **De-emphasize:** AEO as headline; orchestration internals (translate to user outcomes). *NOTE: a search result claiming she's "Ali Partovi's twin sister" is FALSE — do not repeat.*

### Zhen Li — Engineering Lead, Replit (creator of Replit Agent)  ★ builder
Ex-Google/ByteDance ML; built Replit Agent 0→1 ($250M+ ARR). Wrote **"The good way to AgentMaxxing"** (the moat is *building a system around agents with clear feedback loops + human-final-decisions*, not autonomy-maxxing; mocks "slop-maxxing" / vague prompts) and **"Decision-Time Guidance"** (inject situational guidance at decision time; +15% tools/loop). Eval-driven; cares about fresh/grounded data. Domain fit thin → will judge the *build* hard. **Loves:** AEO + phone-verification as concrete verifiable-done; real grounding data (rules, GoodRx); a working live deploy; tight scope. **Skeptical:** reliability of eligibility answers; scope sprawl (sees 5 products); phone-verify at scale; thin wrapper. **Emphasize:** the verifiable-done loop (use the word "verifiable") · eval/ground-truth story w/ accuracy + human gate · a fast live demo on real data. **De-emphasize:** breadth; mission rhetoric without proof (he reacts to "slop").

### Alistair Smith — MTS, Anthropic (Bun + Claude Code)  ★ builder
Core Bun engineer (came via the Bun acquisition); type-systems/runtime/perf person. **Project:** benefits-eligibility tool (SNAP/CalFresh → benefit stack → Rx savings; chat + voice; agent-to-agent API "AEO"; crowdsourced + phone-verified local data)

---

## THE HEADLINE FINDING
**Six of ten judges are deep agent-orchestration / verifiable-done / evals people — and one of them literally founded a voice-agent EVALS company.** This panel is almost perfectly matched to your project's surface area, which means extraordinary resonance *and* an extraordinarily high bar on the exact things you're building (voice agents, agent-to-agent, verification). Build for substance; the room can smell a thin wrapper instantly.

The builder bloc:
- **Alistair Smith** — Bun + Claude Code (the harness/runtime; typed tool contracts; dynamic-workflow subagents)
- **Gagan Bhat** — Claude Managed Agents (agent infra: state, sandboxing, tracing, scoped permissions)
- **Marius Buleandra** — Applied AI; **ex-co-founder/CTO of Hamming AI = automated VOICE-AGENT testing & evals**
- **Conner Phillippi** — Claude Code agent teams (multi-agent orchestration) + auto mode (permissions/safety) + hand-crafted evals
- **Ciro Spaciari** — co-leads Claude Code + Cowork (multi-agent orchestration, systems/perf)
- **Zhen Li** — creator of Replit Agent (agent reliability, decision-time guidance, eval-driven)

The investor bloc (leans technical/operator, not pure growth):
- **James Da Costa** (a16z) — ex-fintech founder for the unbanked (Fingo, Mandala voice/SMS); wrote a16z's "Voice AI Will Change How We Bank" + "How Will My Agent Pay" (agent-to-agent). **Most on-thesis VC you could draw.**
- **Sara Deshpande** (Maven) — consumer-health; "billing/pricing, access to pharmaceuticals" is her named territory.
- **Pavla Bobosikova** (Neo) — ex-founder (WFHomie, acq'd); product/people-first; "verify don't hallucinate" instinct.
- **Mar Hershenson** (Pear VC) — PhD EE + serial founder; technical VC. *[profile pending]*

---

## THE UNIVERSAL PATTERN (what to optimize for)
These showed up across nearly EVERY judge, builder and VC alike:

1. **"How do you KNOW it's correct?" is the #1 question from all 10.** Verifiable-done isn't just 15% of the rubric here — it's the panel's shared obsession. Wrong eligibility = real harm to a vulnerable user.
2. **"Why Opus 4.8 vs. a cheaper model or a rules engine?"** Every Anthropic judge + Zhen + Gagan will ask. Winning answer = **model routing**: deterministic rules engine does the eligibility math; Opus does only the load-bearing reasoning (reconciling messy/conflicting program rules, parsing unstructured PAP/copay prose, navigating a live phone-tree dialogue). Saying "we reserve Opus for the hard part and use cheap models elsewhere" signals engineering maturity.
3. **Thin-wrapper allergy.** Do NOT lead with "chat interface." Chat/voice is the surface; the orchestration + verification is the product. Eligibility math = rules engine; the model = reasoning/orchestration.
4. **Don't sprawl — one deep, verified path beats ten shallow integrations.** James, Zhen, Gagan, Ciro, Conner each said it independently. Anchor on CalFresh end-to-end; mention SNAP-stack + Rx + crowdsourcing as one roadmap line.
5. **Bring evals as a deliverable.** Marius, Conner, Zhen explicitly. Even a small labeled test-persona set with an accuracy number is the single highest-leverage trust unlock with this specific panel.
6. **The voice phone-verification loop is your hero — but it must be LIVE, not staged.** Everyone loves it; **Marius (voice-eval founder) and Ciro/Zhen (perf) will catch a mock instantly** and will ask your failure modes (mis-hear, dropped call, dead number, hostile IVR).
7. **AEO / agent-to-agent splits the room.** Builders (Gagan, Conner, Alistair, James) find it native and will probe the actual schema/auth/handoff — show a real typed protocol with a live A2A call. VCs (Sara, Pavla) read it as buzzword — to them keep it a one-line "and it's agent-queryable" kicker. **Demo it for the builders; downplay it for the VCs.**
8. **Distribution / who-pays** matters only to the VCs (Sara, Pavla, James) — have one crisp sentence (food banks / Medicaid managed-care plans paid to enroll people; GoodRx affiliate). Builders don't care.

---

## THE WINNING PLAY (one build, two framings)
**Build:** deterministic CalFresh rules engine + Opus-as-orchestrator/reasoner + a real eval harness (test personas w/ accuracy) + LIVE voice phone-verification with explicit failure handling + a genuinely typed AEO interface. Wire in one "Claude catches & fixes its own failure" moment (the org explicitly asks to see this).

**Demo (35%):** ONE CalFresh path, end-to-end, live, fast, ending on a verified result + a dollar figure. Show the voice agent place a real call and flip a listing to `verified`. Show the eval set passing.

**Round-1 video + repo:** lead with the orchestration + the self-verifying loop + evals passing; functional prototype > docs; public repo, "built today" delineation, live URL.

**Round-2 pitch (3 min, one slide):** 30 sec human impact + before/after metric + one distribution line → the working orchestration + verification (the spine) → one load-bearing Opus-4.8 moment → AEO as a real-schema kicker. Then field the universal Q&A.

**Universal Q&A to rehearse verbatim:** (1) correctness/eval + error rate + low-confidence fallback to human; (2) why-Opus = routing; (3) voice failure modes; (4) AEO protocol/auth; (5) who-pays/distribution; (6) what's real-today vs roadmap.

---

### Sara Deshpande — GP, Maven Ventures (consumer-health VC)
This problem is her named territory ("billing/pricing," "access to pharmaceuticals"; AI that makes "life better for everyday consumers"). **Rewards:** real distribution/CAC (her #1 filter), habitual usage, massive growing problem, blunt honesty ("tough love"). **Tech lens:** skeptical AI is a moat — 2026: *"saying 'AI' or 'Agentic' will raise more questions than applause… when building takes less time, commoditization takes less time."* Consumer-experience-first. **Loves:** the problem; consumer chat/voice; phone-verification; underserved population. **Skeptical:** AI-wrapper; retention (eligibility is once/twice); distribution/CAC; A2A-as-B2B-off-thesis. **Emphasize:** distribution channel · proprietary phone-verified data as the non-commoditizable moat · recurring-value loop. **De-emphasize:** orchestration/Opus internals as the story; AEO as centerpiece.

# Idea 4 — Requirements & Decisions (captured 2026-06-13)

> STATUS: **NOT launching research yet.** Waiting on Aza's further refinements to the
> demo-hero / fully-formed version. Proceed with the research loop ONLY once the
> fully-formed version is delivered.

## The idea (evolution of CalFreshTruth / idea 1)
A highly knowledgeable, data-connected **advisor agent with a grounding layer** that tells a
person *every* program they'd qualify for and benefit from given their specific context
(location, income, household, immigration status, challenges) — spanning:
- **Public benefits** (SNAP/CalFresh, Medi-Cal, WIC, CalEITC, etc.)
- **Nonprofit / charity resources**
- **Commercial discount programs** (GoodRx, ACP internet, utility discounts, etc.)
  — framed as discount-program *eligibility*, NOT medical advice (DQ guard).

**Modular / forkable by design:** others clone the repo and add **connectors** for new
services, benefits, and resources. Core = grounding layer + connector registry; the agent
reasons over whatever connectors are plugged in.

**Why it matters (Impact 35% narrative — the take-up gap):** the safety net misses exactly
the people who most need it, because they lack advocates, expertise, and individualized
attention to navigate their own situation. This agent IS that knowledgeable advocate, at
scale. End state: a tool **nonprofits and government agencies deploy to "capture" more of
the people who should already be captured.** (Equity/"who benefits" lens = the move that
won prizes for TARA + CrossBeam in prior Anthropic hackathons.)

## LOCKED decisions
- **Data/geographic scope:** CA-focused starter set — CalFresh, Medi-Cal, WIC, CalEITC,
  CARE/FERA utility discounts, Lifeline/ACP, GoodRx. Depth + citations over breadth.
- **Build/orchestration requirement (Aza, explicit):** the crafted master prompt must
  specify that **up to 14 simultaneous sub-agents** may run when a stage of the build
  allows for it and benefits from it (e.g. fanning out one agent per connector / per
  program / per persona). NOTE: Claude Code workflow concurrency caps at
  min(16, cpu_cores - 2), so 14 is achievable on a >=16-core machine; design fan-out to
  request up to 14 where beneficial and degrade gracefully otherwise.
- **Folder:** `research 4/` (sibling of research, research 2, research 3).

## PENDING (resolve before finalizing)
- **Primary user / framing:** research to DECIDE (applicant-facing advisor vs
  navigator/caseworker tool vs hybrid), then **ASK AZA AGAIN before finalizing.**
- **Demo hero / 30-sec wow:** Aza is adding refinements; do not start until the
  fully-formed version is delivered.

## Reuse from prior runs
- Same loop structure as idea 3 (fixed idea -> sweep -> flesh out -> score -> craft ->
  adversarial critics incl. DQ lens -> converge).
- Grounding already in `research/` (idea 1, CalFreshTruth) is directly relevant —
  CA MCE / 200% FPL, DataSF, Code for America x Anthropic SNAP Navigator.

# Idea 4 — Requirements & Decisions (captured 2026-06-13, v2)

> STATUS: fully-formed version received (product spec + decision-grade brief + Drive design
> screens + FULL JUDGE PANEL). One gating confirmation outstanding before launching the loop
> (see PENDING). This is a TEAM build now (Aza + Teresa + Sam, possibly +1), not solo.

## The product — "One Door · California" (working name)
A unified shelf of every kind of help — government benefits + drug-manufacturer coupons +
housing vouchers + utility discounts + library/park access — all sharing **one eligibility
brain**. CA-first because the core problem is the **state↔federal coordination gap** (SNAP is
federal-funded but state-run as CalFresh; Medicaid is Medi-Cal; HUD vouchers run by local
PHAs). The platform hides that seam.

**Flow:** Shelf (browse by need) OR Front Door ("Find everything I qualify for" — 8 questions,
~4 min, no account) → live eligibility wizard with a running "$/mo + # programs" ticker →
Results grouped by need, sorted by $ value, mixing benefits + coupons + vouchers, with honest
negative states (waitlist, "SF list closed → nearby open") and the state/federal seam LABELED
as a trust signal → Service detail in plain language + accessibility (read aloud, one-tap
translate, "explain simply", "text me this").

**Ultimate vision:** the agent becomes the person's **advocate** — walks into the benefits
interview with full context of their situation and the knowledge to advocate for them. End
state: a tool nonprofits + government agencies + private orgs deploy to "capture" more of the
people the safety net misses (the take-up gap). National later; CA for the demo.

**Modular / forkable:** **Contributor Studio** — paste a program URL → Opus extracts it into the
service schema. That is how the catalog grows to new coupons/parks/libraries, and IS the
"platform of services" + orchestration story (connectors/registry over a grounding layer).

## THE WINNING PLAY (forced by the judge panel — see 00-judge-strategy-brief.md)
6 of 10 judges are agent-orchestration / verifiable-done / evals people; one founded a
voice-agent EVALS company (Marius/Hamming). Build for substance.
- **MODEL ROUTING is the spine and the answer to "why Opus?":** a **deterministic rules engine
  (PolicyEngine) computes eligibility math + dollar amounts + pass/fail**; **Opus 4.8 does ONLY
  the load-bearing reasoning** — reconciling messy/conflicting program rules, parsing
  unstructured PAP/copay prose, navigating a live phone-tree dialogue, and generating
  plain-language/why-you-qualify/translation/read-aloud. NEVER let Opus compute the math
  (waste + hallucination risk; Alistair/Gagan will ding it).
- **"How do you KNOW it's correct?" is the #1 question from ALL 10 judges.** Verifiable-done is
  the panel obsession, not just the 15% line. → ship an **EVAL HARNESS as a deliverable**: a
  labeled test-persona set with a stated accuracy number + a low-confidence **fallback to
  human/"apply or talk to a navigator."**
- **HERO = a LIVE voice phone-verification loop** (agent calls a real resource, confirms
  hours/eligibility/availability, flips a listing to `verified`), with EXPLICIT failure handling
  (mis-hear, dropped call, dead number, hostile IVR). Must be LIVE — Marius/Ciro/Zhen catch a
  mock instantly. (Compliance: FCC TCPA AI-voice + CA AI-voice disclosure — disclose + log.)
- **One "Claude catches & fixes its own failure" moment** (the org explicitly wants to see it).
- **Typed AEO / agent-to-agent interface** (MCP server + typed schema + a live A2A call) — DEMO
  for builders, downplay to a one-line kicker for the VCs.
- **One deep verified path (CalFresh end-to-end) > ten shallow integrations.** Stack + Rx +
  crowdsourcing = one roadmap line.
- **Safety posture:** eligibility EDUCATION/NAVIGATION, never official determination, legal
  advice, or medical advice. Say "likely / possibly / likely-not — apply or talk to a
  navigator," never "you qualify / you do not." Rx = cost pathways only, no clinical
  substitution; avoid anti-kickback steering. Privacy-by-default, anonymous mode (mixed-status
  households). Every output CITES its rule/source with a `verified_at`/confidence.

## LOCKED decisions
- **Data/geographic scope:** CA starter set — CalFresh (hero, end-to-end), then the stack:
  Medi-Cal, WIC, CARE/FERA, Lifeline/CA-LifeLine, CalEITC, free/reduced school meals, SUN Bucks;
  + Rx savings (GoodRx/PAP/copay/Cost Plus) as cost-pathways. Depth + citations over breadth.
  Note 2026 H.R.1 changes (immigrant eligibility 4/1/2026; work requirements 6/1/2026) → rules
  must be date-aware + status-aware.
- **Architecture (locked by judges):** deterministic rules engine (PolicyEngine) ÷ Opus-4.8
  reasoning/orchestration; eval harness w/ accuracy; live voice phone-verification; typed
  AEO/MCP; Contributor Studio for modularity.
- **Up to 14 simultaneous sub-agents** where a build stage benefits (per program / per
  connector / per persona / per resource-to-verify). Caps at min(16, cores-2); degrade
  gracefully.
- **Orchestrator points at the Drive for additional context/grounding as it works** (Aza's
  instruction). Drive folder shared as "Claude Hackathon"; use `gws` CLI. Already pulled:
  `00-drive-DRAFT_DESIGN_SCREENS.md` (the One Door screen designs).
- **Folder:** `research 4/`.

# Idea 4 — Requirements & Decisions (captured 2026-06-13, v2)

> STATUS: fully-formed version received (product spec + decision-grade brief + Drive design
> screens + FULL JUDGE PANEL). One gating confirmation outstanding before launching the loop
> (see PENDING). This is a TEAM build now (Aza + Teresa + Sam, possibly +1), not solo.

## The product — "One Door · California" (working name)
A unified shelf of every kind of help — government benefits + drug-manufacturer coupons +
housing vouchers + utility discounts + library/park access — all sharing **one eligibility
brain**. CA-first because the core problem is the **state↔federal coordination gap** (SNAP is
federal-funded but state-run as CalFresh; Medicaid is Medi-Cal; HUD vouchers run by local
PHAs). The platform hides that seam.

**Flow:** Shelf (browse by need) OR Front Door ("Find everything I qualify for" — 8 questions,
~4 min, no account) → live eligibility wizard with a running "$/mo + # programs" ticker →
Results grouped by need, sorted by $ value, mixing benefits + coupons + vouchers, with honest
negative states (waitlist, "SF list closed → nearby open") and the state/federal seam LABELED
as a trust signal → Service detail in plain language + accessibility (read aloud, one-tap
translate, "explain simply", "text me this").

**Ultimate vision:** the agent becomes the person's **advocate** — walks into the benefits
interview with full context of their situation and the knowledge to advocate for them. End
state: a tool nonprofits + government agencies + private orgs deploy to "capture" more of the
people the safety net misses (the take-up gap). National later; CA for the demo.

**Modular / forkable:** **Contributor Studio** — paste a program URL → Opus extracts it into the
service schema. That is how the catalog grows to new coupons/parks/libraries, and IS the
"platform of services" + orchestration story (connectors/registry over a grounding layer).

## THE WINNING PLAY (forced by the judge panel — see 00-judge-strategy-brief.md)
6 of 10 judges are agent-orchestration / verifiable-done / evals people; one founded a
voice-agent EVALS company (Marius/Hamming). Build for substance.
- **MODEL ROUTING is the spine and the answer to "why Opus?":** a **deterministic rules engine
  (PolicyEngine) computes eligibility math + dollar amounts + pass/fail**; **Opus 4.8 does ONLY
  the load-bearing reasoning** — reconciling messy/conflicting program rules, parsing
  unstructured PAP/copay prose, navigating a live phone-tree dialogue, and generating
  plain-language/why-you-qualify/translation/read-aloud. NEVER let Opus compute the math
  (waste + hallucination risk; Alistair/Gagan will ding it).
- **"How do you KNOW it's correct?" is the #1 question from ALL 10 judges.** Verifiable-done is
  the panel obsession, not just the 15% line. → ship an **EVAL HARNESS as a deliverable**: a
  labeled test-persona set with a stated accuracy number + a low-confidence **fallback to
  human/"apply or talk to a navigator."**
- **HERO = a LIVE voice phone-verification loop** (agent calls a real resource, confirms
  hours/eligibility/availability, flips a listing to `verified`), with EXPLICIT failure handling
  (mis-hear, dropped call, dead number, hostile IVR). Must be LIVE — Marius/Ciro/Zhen catch a
  mock instantly. (Compliance: FCC TCPA AI-voice + CA AI-voice disclosure — disclose + log.)
- **One "Claude catches & fixes its own failure" moment** (the org explicitly wants to see it).
- **Typed AEO / agent-to-agent interface** (MCP server + typed schema + a live A2A call) — DEMO
  for builders, downplay to a one-line kicker for the VCs.
- **One deep verified path (CalFresh end-to-end) > ten shallow integrations.** Stack + Rx +
  crowdsourcing = one roadmap line.
- **Safety posture:** eligibility EDUCATION/NAVIGATION, never official determination, legal
  advice, or medical advice. Say "likely / possibly / likely-not — apply or talk to a
  navigator," never "you qualify / you do not." Rx = cost pathways only, no clinical
  substitution; avoid anti-kickback steering. Privacy-by-default, anonymous mode (mixed-status
  households). Every output CITES its rule/source with a `verified_at`/confidence.

## LOCKED decisions
- **Data/geographic scope:** CA starter set — CalFresh (hero, end-to-end), then the stack:
  Medi-Cal, WIC, CARE/FERA, Lifeline/CA-LifeLine, CalEITC, free/reduced school meals, SUN Bucks;
  + Rx savings (GoodRx/PAP/copay/Cost Plus) as cost-pathways. Depth + citations over breadth.
  Note 2026 H.R.1 changes (immigrant eligibility 4/1/2026; work requirements 6/1/2026) → rules
  must be date-aware + status-aware.
- **Architecture (locked by judges):** deterministic rules engine (PolicyEngine) ÷ Opus-4.8
  reasoning/orchestration; eval harness w/ accuracy; live voice phone-verification; typed
  AEO/MCP; Contributor Studio for modularity.
- **Up to 14 simultaneous sub-agents** where a build stage benefits (per program / per
  connector / per persona / per resource-to-verify). Caps at min(16, cores-2); degrade
  gracefully.
- **Orchestrator points at the Drive for additional context/grounding as it works** (Aza's
  instruction). Drive folder shared as "Claude Hackathon"; use `gws` CLI. Already pulled:
  `00-drive-DRAFT_DESIGN_SCREENS.md` (the One Door screen designs).
- **Folder:** `research 4/`.

## PENDING — ONE gating confirmation before launch
- **Primary user / framing:** materials strongly imply **applicant/consumer-facing "One Door,"
  positioned as the tool nonprofits/agencies/health-plans deploy** (B2B2C distribution narrative
  for the VCs). RECOMMEND locking consumer-facing for the demo + B2G/health-plan as the
  who-pays line. CONFIRM with Aza, then launch.
- **Anything else Aza wants to add** before the loop runs (he has added material in waves).

## Grounding already saved in research 4/
- `00-decision-grade-brief.md` — venture brief (landscape, CalFresh complexity, stacking, Rx,
  data sources, crowdsourcing, AEO, risks, market, what-would-have-to-be-true).
- `00-drive-DRAFT_DESIGN_SCREENS.md` — the One Door UI screens (from the shared Drive).
- `00-judge-strategy-brief.md` — the full 10-judge panel + the winning play.
- Idea-1 grounding in `research/` (CA MCE/200% FPL, DataSF, CfA × Anthropic SNAP Navigator).

content.text:
Saved everything to `research 4/`. The judge panel doesn't just inform the build, it **locks the architecture** — so before I launch, here's how it redirects us, and then two quick confirmations.

## What the judge panel forces (this is now non-negotiable spine, not preference)

- **6 of 10 judges live in agent-orchestration / verifiable-done / evals.** One (Marius) *founded a voice-agent evals company.* The room smells a thin wrapper instantly.
- **"How do you KNOW it's correct?" is the #1 question from all 10.** → an **eval harness with a stated accuracy number** becomes a *deliverable*, not a nicety, plus an honest low-confidence fallback to "apply or talk to a navigator."
- **"Why Opus?" is answered by model ROUTING:** a deterministic rules engine (PolicyEngine) does the eligibility math + dollar figures; **Opus 4.8 does only the load-bearing reasoning** (reconciling conflicting program rules, parsing messy PAP/copay prose, driving a live phone-tree). Letting Opus compute the math would actively *lose* points with Alistair/Gagan.
- **The hero is a LIVE voice phone-verification loop** — the agent calls a real resource, confirms it, flips a listing to `verified` — with explicit failure handling. Must be live; Marius/Ciro/Zhen catch a mock on sight.
- **One "Claude catches and fixes its own failure" moment** (the org explicitly asks for it).
- **Typed AEO/MCP with a live agent-to-agent call** — demo it for the builder bloc, one-line kicker for the VCs.
- **One deep verified CalFresh path > ten shallow integrations.** The stack + Rx + Contributor Studio modularity = one roadmap line.

So the research loop's job has narrowed: not "is this a good idea" (it is, and it's now sharply de-risked), but **produce the airtight idea brief + an eval-driven rubric + the master prompt that builds *this exact architecture* with up-to-14 sub-agents, verifiable-done, and the live voice hero** — pre-grounded on all four docs I just saved + the idea-1 CalFresh research.

Two confirmations and I launch:

options.label: Applicant-facing, B2G who-pays
options.description: Build the consumer 'One Door' the applicant uses directly; position nonprofits/agencies/health-plans as who deploys & pays. Matches your UI spec + gives the VCs a distribution line. Recommended.

options.label: Navigator/caseworker tool
options.description: Build for the nonprofit navigator serving many clients. Closer to the B2G end state, but weaker 30-sec consumer wow and less aligned to the One Door screens.

options.label: Let the loop decide
options.description: Have the agents weigh both against the judge panel and recommend, then I bring it back before finalizing.

questions.question: Ready to launch the 10-agent loop now with all four docs + judge panel folded in, or more to add first?

options.label: Launch now
options.description: Run the loop pre-grounded on the decision-brief, Drive screens, judge panel, and idea-1 CalFresh research. Output: idea brief + eval-driven rubric + master prompt (up to 14 sub-agents, live voice hero, verifiable-done).

options.label: I have more to add
options.description: Hold — you'll paste more context first (you've been adding in waves), then I launch with everything.

Building the loop — pre-grounded on all four docs, and its adversarial critics are modeled on the *actual judges* (a Marius/voice-eval lens, an Alistair+Gagan typed-contracts/why-Opus lens, a Zhen+Conner+Ciro eval-and-orchestration lens, a Sara+James+Pavla+Mar VC lens, and a DQ/safety lens). `

const CONTEXT = `
CONTEXT — Claude Build Day (Cerebral Valley + Anthropic), one-day hackathon at Shack15, SF, today. Build on Opus 4.8.
TEAM build (Aza + ~2-3 teammates), letting Claude run autonomously in a verification loop. ~6.5h. Deliverable: public GitHub repo, LIVE URL, 1-min demo video, brief + rubric + session log.
SCORING: Impact 35% | Demo 35% (working, impressive, LIVE in 3 min) | Opus 4.8 Use 15% (creative, surprising) | Orchestration 15% (done verifiable by the MODEL with no human; rerunnable tomorrow on a new problem).

THE LOCKED PRODUCT — "One Door · California":
A unified shelf of every kind of help (CalFresh + the benefit stack + Rx savings + vouchers + utility/library/park access) sharing ONE eligibility brain. Applicant/consumer-facing (the person uses it directly); who-pays = nonprofits / government outreach / Medicaid managed-care plans + GoodRx affiliate. CA-first (hides the state↔federal coordination seam). Ultimately the person's ADVOCATE — walks into the benefits interview with full context. Modular via a Contributor Studio (paste a program URL -> Opus extracts it into the service schema = the connectors/platform growth + orchestration story).

THE WINNING PLAY IS LOCKED BY THE JUDGE PANEL (6/10 judges are agent-orchestration/verifiable-done/evals people; Marius founded a voice-agent EVALS company). NON-NEGOTIABLE SPINE:
1. MODEL ROUTING answers "why Opus?": a DETERMINISTIC RULES ENGINE (PolicyEngine) computes eligibility math + dollar amounts + pass/fail; OPUS 4.8 does ONLY load-bearing reasoning (reconcile messy/conflicting program rules, parse unstructured PAP/copay prose, drive a live phone-tree, generate plain-language/why-you-qualify/translate/read-aloud). NEVER let Opus compute the math.
2. "HOW DO YOU KNOW IT'S CORRECT?" is the #1 question from ALL 10 judges -> ship an EVAL HARNESS as a deliverable: labeled test-personas with a STATED ACCURACY NUMBER + an honest low-confidence FALLBACK to "apply or talk to a navigator".
3. HERO = a LIVE voice phone-verification loop (agent calls a real resource, confirms hours/eligibility/availability, flips a listing to verified) with EXPLICIT failure handling (mis-hear, dropped call, dead number, hostile IVR). Must be LIVE, never mocked. Compliance: FCC TCPA AI-voice + CA AI-voice disclosure -> disclose + log.
4. ONE "Claude catches & fixes its own failure" moment (the org explicitly wants it).
5. TYPED AEO / agent-to-agent interface (MCP server + typed schema + a LIVE A2A call) — demo for builders, one-line kicker for VCs.
6. ONE deep verified path (CalFresh end-to-end) > ten shallow integrations. Stack + Rx + crowdsourcing = one roadmap line.
7. SAFETY POSTURE: eligibility EDUCATION/NAVIGATION, never official determination / legal advice / medical advice. Say "likely / possibly / likely-not — apply or talk to a navigator". Rx = cost pathways only (no clinical substitution; avoid anti-kickback steering). Privacy-by-default + anonymous mode (mixed-status households). Every output CITES its rule/source with verified_at + confidence. Rules must be DATE-AWARE + STATUS-AWARE (2026 H.R.1: immigrant eligibility 4/1/2026, work requirements 6/1/2026).

ORCHESTRATION REQUIREMENTS:
- The crafted master prompt MUST specify that UP TO 14 simultaneous sub-agents may run wherever a build stage benefits (one per program / per connector / per persona / per resource-to-verify). (Claude Code workflow concurrency caps at min(16, cores-2); request up to 14, degrade gracefully.)
- The orchestrator should be told it can point at the shared Drive (gws CLI) for additional context/grounding as it works.
- "Done" must be a GREEN CHECK the model verifies (eval suite passes at a stated accuracy, live URL 200, a real voice-call transcript flips a listing to verified, typed AEO A2A call returns valid), NOT the model asserting it.

We are NOT building the product. We are producing (1) the airtight IDEA BRIEF, (2) an EVAL-DRIVEN machine-gradable RUBRIC, (3) the single MASTER PROMPT that drives the full agentic build->self-correct->test->deploy->verify loop with up to 14 sub-agents.
${GROUNDING_FILES}
Use WebSearch + mcp__turbowebfetch__fetch (via ToolSearch) for any live web research. Cite URLs.
`

const FINDINGS_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    lane: { type: 'string' }, file_written: { type: 'string' },
    key_findings: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    implications_for_build: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    implications_for_prompt: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    sources: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
  required: ['lane', 'file_written', 'key_findings'],
}

const LANES = [
  { key: 'policyengine-rules', title: 'PolicyEngine + the deterministic rules-engine ÷ Opus split',
    task: `Research how to actually USE PolicyEngine (US) to compute CA program eligibility + dollar amounts (SNAP/CalFresh, Medi-Cal, CalEITC, etc.) — its Python API / REST API, what it covers for CA, accuracy, and limits. Define the precise SPLIT: what the deterministic engine computes vs what Opus 4.8 reasons over (conflicting rules, immigration/student/ABAWD edge cases, 2026 H.R.1 date-awareness, unstructured PAP/copay prose). Cite.` },
  { key: 'eval-harness', title: 'Eval harness for eligibility correctness (the #1 judge question)',
    task: `Design a concrete EVAL HARNESS that proves eligibility correctness: where to get/derive labeled test personas with KNOWN outcomes (PolicyEngine itself, USDA/CDSS examples, MyFriendBen, historical anonymized cases), how to compute a stated ACCURACY number, FAIL_TO_PASS/PASS_TO_PASS structure, and the low-confidence FALLBACK-to-human gate. This is a graded deliverable for Marius/Conner/Zhen. Cite eval patterns + ground-truth sources.` },
  { key: 'voice-verification', title: 'LIVE voice phone-verification stack + failure handling (the HERO)',
    task: `Research the fastest solo/team stack to build a LIVE outbound voice agent that calls a real resource, navigates an IVR/dialogue, confirms hours/eligibility/availability, and flips a listing to verified — Twilio Voice + a realtime speech stack (e.g. realtime STT/TTS, ElevenLabs), or Vapi/Bland/Retell, driven by Opus. Detail EXPLICIT failure handling (mis-hear, dropped call, dead number, hostile IVR, no-answer) and the COMPLIANCE layer (FCC TCPA AI-voice ruling + CA AI-voice disclosure: disclosure script + consent + logging + human fallback). Recommend ONE stack. Cite.` },
  { key: 'typed-aeo-mcp', title: 'Typed AEO / agent-to-agent (MCP) interface',
    task: `Design a genuinely TYPED agent-callable interface: an MCP server exposing typed tools (screen_eligibility, search_programs, verify_resource, rx_savings_paths) with schemas, citations, confidence, auth/scoping, versioning, and a SAFE fallback. Show a concrete LIVE A2A call shape another agent makes. Ground in MCP docs + a16z's agent-to-agent thesis. Cite.` },
  { key: 'ca-program-data', title: 'CA program stack data, APIs, citations + 2026 date-awareness',
    task: `Catalog the authoritative, citable sources + any APIs for the CA stack: CalFresh (CDSS, 200% FPL MCE, deductions), Medi-Cal (138% FPL), WIC, CARE/FERA, CA LifeLine/Lifeline, CalEITC (FTB), free/reduced school meals (130/185% FPL), SUN Bucks. Note the exact 2026 H.R.1 effective dates + what changed. Plus DataSF / Open Referral HSDS / 211 for local resources. For each: the canonical URL to cite + how fresh it is. Cite.` },
  { key: 'rx-savings-compliant', title: 'Rx savings pathways — compliant matching',
    task: `Research how to match a medication list to NON-CLINICAL cost pathways: GoodRx coupons, manufacturer copay cards, PAPs, NeedyMeds/RxAssist directories, SIRUM, Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drugs. Define exactly what the agent may say (cost pathway exists) vs must NOT (clinical substitution, switching meds) and the anti-kickback / federal-program-exclusion guardrails. Which have APIs/data feeds vs directory-only. Cite.` },
  { key: 'deploy-stack', title: 'Fastest team stack: live URL + voice + sandbox + self-verify',
    task: `Recommend the fastest team stack to ship a LIVE URL in ~6h that integrates the voice agent, calls PolicyEngine, serves the One Door UI, exposes the MCP server, and that an agent can CURL/health-check + run evals against to self-verify. Compare Next.js/Vercel (Fluid Compute timeouts for LLM/voice callbacks) vs alternatives; where the voice webhook + telephony live. Cite current limits (2026).` },
  { key: 'dynamic-workflows-14', title: 'Dynamic workflows + up-to-14 sub-agent fan-out + "catches own failure"',
    task: `From the "dynamic workflows in Claude Code" blog + Claude Code docs: the exact patterns for the master prompt to spawn UP TO 14 parallel sub-agents where beneficial (one per program/connector/persona/resource), barrier vs pipeline, model routing per role, and a self-correct loop. Design the concrete "Claude catches & fixes its own failure" moment (e.g. an independent verifier agent catches a stale/over-confident eligibility verdict or a mis-heard voice confirmation and forces a re-run). Cite.` },
  { key: 'judge-tuned-demo', title: 'The 3-min live demo tuned to THIS judge panel',
    task: `Using 00-judge-strategy-brief.md, design the exact 3-min live demo + the round-1 video: the 30-sec human-impact + dollar-figure open ("$1,400 you didn't know about"), the live voice call flipping a listing to verified, the eval set passing on screen, the load-bearing Opus moment, the "catches its own failure" beat, and the typed-AEO kicker. Map each beat to which judges it lands for. Produce a beat-by-beat script + the one-slide content. Build it BACKWARD from the verified-result-with-dollars close.` },
  { key: 'impact-distribution', title: 'Impact quantification + who-pays / distribution',
    task: `Quantify the take-up gap for the Impact narrative (eligible-but-unenrolled counts + unclaimed dollars, CA-specific where possible: CalFresh participation gap, unclaimed CalEITC, etc.) and the crisp WHO-PAYS line for the VCs (food banks / Medicaid managed-care plans paid to enroll; GoodRx affiliate; B2G outreach). Frame as Mar Hershenson's 3-act FOMO story with the biggest risk named + mitigated. Cite.` },
]

phase('Sweep')
log('Round 1: 10 parallel agents hardening the locked One Door build')
const sweep = await parallel(LANES.map(lane => () =>
  agent(
    `${CONTEXT}\n\nYOU ARE RESEARCH LANE: "${lane.title}".\nTASK: ${lane.task}\n\nFirst READ the grounding files listed above. Then do live web research where needed (WebSearch + mcp__turbowebfetch__fetch via ToolSearch). Be specific, cite URLs, and stay consistent with the LOCKED spine (do not re-open settled decisions).\nWRITE your findings to ${DIR}/01-${lane.key}.md (Write tool, absolute path). Structure: ## Findings (specific, sourced), ## Implications for the BUILD, ## Implications for the MASTER PROMPT, ## Sources.\nReturn the structured object.`,
    { label: `sweep:${lane.key}`, phase: 'Sweep', schema: FINDINGS_SCHEMA }
  )
)).then(r => r.filter(Boolean))
if (sweep.length < 4) { throw new Error(`Sweep produced only ${sweep.length} lanes (likely an API outage) — aborting before synthesis. Rerun when healthy.`) }
log(`Sweep complete: ${sweep.length}/10 lanes`)

phase('Synthesize')
const groundingBlob = sweep.map(s =>
  `### ${s.lane}\nFINDINGS:\n- ${(s.key_findings||[]).join('\n- ')}\nBUILD:\n- ${(s.implications_for_build||[]).join('\n- ')}\nPROMPT:\n- ${(s.implications_for_prompt||[]).join('\n- ')}`
).join('\n\n')

const SHAPE_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    product_name: { type: 'string' },
    one_liner: { type: 'string' },
    architecture: { type: 'string', description: 'the rules-engine ÷ Opus routing + voice + AEO + Contributor Studio, concretely' },
    six_hour_scope: { type: 'string', description: 'the exact CalFresh-end-to-end slice + what is roadmap' },
    demo_wow: { type: 'string' },
    catches_own_failure: { type: 'string' },
    eval_plan: { type: 'string' },
    risks: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
  required: ['product_name', 'architecture', 'six_hour_scope', 'demo_wow', 'eval_plan'],
}
const shape = await agent(
  `${CONTEXT}\n\nMerged sweep research:\n${groundingBlob}\n\nFirst WRITE a synthesis doc to ${DIR}/02-synthesis.md that FLESHES OUT the locked One Door product into a concrete, build-ready shape consistent with the judge-locked spine: the rules-engine÷Opus routing, the live voice phone-verification hero + failure handling, the eval harness + accuracy story, the typed AEO/MCP, the Contributor Studio modularity, the safety posture, and the exact CalFresh-end-to-end 6h scope (with the stack + Rx + crowdsourcing as roadmap). Then return the SHAPE object (a single coherent build shape, NOT alternatives — the idea is locked).`,
  { label: 'synthesize', phase: 'Synthesize', schema: SHAPE_SCHEMA }
)
log(`Synthesized build shape: ${shape.product_name}`)

phase('Score')
const SCORE_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    impact_35: { type: 'number' }, demo_35: { type: 'number' }, opus48_15: { type: 'number' }, orchestration_15: { type: 'number' },
    weighted_total: { type: 'number' }, prohibited_violation: { type: 'boolean' }, prohibited_note: { type: 'string' },
    judge_resonance_notes: { type: 'string' }, top_risks: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } }, verdict: { type: 'string' },
  },
  required: ['weighted_total', 'prohibited_violation', 'verdict'],
}
const score = await agent(
  `${CONTEXT}\n\nSCORE this build shape against the EXACT rubric AND the judge panel as a harsh reviewer.\nSHAPE: ${JSON.stringify(shape)}\n\nScore impact /35, demo /35, opus48 /15, orchestration /15 -> weighted_total /100. Check the prohibited list (NOT medical advice — Rx must be cost-pathways only; NOT a dashboard-as-main-feature; NOT basic RAG). Note how it resonates with / where it's exposed to the specific judges. List top risks + verdict.`,
  { label: 'score', phase: 'Score', schema: SCORE_SCHEMA }
)
log(`Score ${score.weighted_total}/100; prohibited=${score.prohibited_violation}`)

const PROMPT_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    idea_name: { type: 'string' }, idea_brief: { type: 'string' }, rubric_md: { type: 'string' },
    master_prompt: { type: 'string' }, subagent_roles: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    self_verification_plan: { type: 'string' }, file_written: { type: 'string' },
  },
  required: ['idea_name', 'master_prompt', 'rubric_md', 'self_verification_plan'],
}
const CRITIQUE_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    panel: { type: 'string' }, approved: { type: 'boolean' },
    fatal_flaws: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } }, fixes_required: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    score_estimate: { type: 'number' },
  },
  required: ['panel', 'approved'],
}

let solution = null, feedback = '', round = 0
const MAX_ROUNDS = 3
while (round < MAX_ROUNDS) {
  round++
  phase('Craft')
  log(`Convergence round ${round}: crafting brief + eval-driven rubric + master prompt`)
  solution = await agent(
    `${CONTEXT}\n\nSweep grounding:\n${groundingBlob}\n\nBuild shape: ${JSON.stringify(shape)}\nScore card: ${JSON.stringify(score)}\n${feedback ? `\nADDRESS THIS JUDGE-PANEL FEEDBACK FROM LAST ROUND (every item):\n${feedback}\n` : ''}\nProduce the COMPLETE SOLUTION for the LOCKED One Door build:\n1) idea_brief — problem (quantified take-up gap), who (applicant-facing; who-pays = nonprofits/Medicaid managed-care/GoodRx affiliate), what it does (rules-engine÷Opus routing; the stack; Rx cost-pathways; the advocate vision; Contributor Studio modularity), what "done" looks like (a green check the model verifies), the safety posture, and a short "why this isn't prohibited (not medical advice / not a dashboard / not basic RAG)" note.\n2) rubric_md — an EVAL-DRIVEN machine-gradable rubric.md the agent grades ITSELF against: a labeled test-persona eval suite with a STATED ACCURACY THRESHOLD + low-confidence fallback gate; live-URL 200; a REAL voice-call transcript that flips a listing to verified (with failure-mode handling); a typed AEO/MCP A2A call returning a valid typed response; the "Claude catches its own failure" check; deterministic-engine-does-the-math assertion (Opus never computes eligibility); citation+confidence on every verdict; rerunnable by swapping a program/connector.\n3) master_prompt — THE single master prompt for Claude Code (open with ultracode; coordination in a JS dynamic-workflow harness) that drives the FULL loop: decompose, spawn roled sub-agents with MODEL ROUTING and UP TO 14 SIMULTANEOUS sub-agents where a stage benefits (one per program/connector/persona/resource), build, write+run the eval harness, place a real verification call, deploy the live URL + MCP server, CURL/eval to self-verify, run the catches-own-failure verifier, self-correct, LOOP until the rubric passes. Tell it it may point at the shared Drive (gws) for grounding. Self-contained, specific, self-verifying, parameterized so another team reruns it tomorrow on a new program/state.\n4) subagent_roles (with model per role), self_verification_plan.\nWRITE the full solution to ${DIR}/SOLUTION.md. Return the object.`,
    { label: `craft:r${round}`, phase: 'Craft', schema: PROMPT_SCHEMA }
  )
  phase('Adversarial')
  const panels = [
    { k: 'voice-evals (Marius)', p: 'You are Marius (founded a voice-agent EVALS company) + the eval-driven judges. Is the live voice phone-verification REAL and failure-handled (mis-hear/dropped/dead/hostile-IVR), and is eligibility correctness proven by an eval set with a STATED accuracy number + human fallback? You catch mocks and hand-waving instantly. Reject verification theater.' },
    { k: 'typed-contracts + why-Opus (Alistair/Gagan)', p: 'You are Alistair (typed tool contracts, done=green-check) + Gagan (agent infra: scoped/traceable/stateful). Is MODEL ROUTING real (deterministic engine does the math, Opus only the load-bearing reasoning — never the math)? Is "done" a verified green check not a model assertion? Is the AEO a genuinely typed, scoped, traceable protocol with a live A2A call — not branding?' },
    { k: 'orchestration + eval (Zhen/Conner/Ciro)', p: 'You are Zhen (eval-driven, mocks slop) + Conner (agent teams: roles/handoffs/verify-per-action, demos over docs) + Ciro (perf, genuine multi-agent). Is there genuine multi-agent orchestration with up-to-14 fan-out where it helps, a fast live demo (not a deck), an eval story with stated error rate + human gate, and tight scope (one deep CalFresh path, not sprawl)?' },
    { k: 'VC (Sara/James/Pavla/Mar)', p: 'You are the VC bloc. Thin-wrapper allergy: is the product the orchestration+verification, not a chat skin? Is there a crisp who-pays/distribution line? Real-user grounding? Impact quantified (eligible-but-unenrolled + unclaimed dollars)? Does it tell as a 3-act FOMO story with the biggest risk named + mitigated? Is the safety/liability posture credible?' },
    { k: 'DQ + safety referee', p: 'You are the rules + safety referee. Does it trip the prohibited list (medical advice bot? dashboard-as-main-feature? basic RAG?)? Is Rx strictly cost-pathways (no clinical substitution / anti-kickback steering)? Is it eligibility EDUCATION not official determination (likely/possibly/likely-not, never "you qualify")? Privacy-by-default + anonymous mode? TCPA/CA AI-voice disclosure on the calls? Date/status-aware for 2026 H.R.1? Only approve if unambiguously clean.' },
  ]
  const critiques = (await parallel(panels.map(pl => () =>
    agent(
      `${CONTEXT}\n\nPANEL LENS: ${pl.p}\n\nSOLUTION TO BREAK:\nBRIEF: ${solution.idea_brief}\nRUBRIC:\n${solution.rubric_md}\nMASTER PROMPT:\n${solution.master_prompt}\nVERIFICATION: ${solution.self_verification_plan}\n\nFind FATAL flaws from YOUR lens. Only approve if it would genuinely place top-3 with this panel, is fully self-verifying, and is unambiguously clean of the prohibited list. List fatal_flaws + fixes_required.`,
      { label: `panel:${pl.k.split(' ')[0]}:r${round}`, phase: 'Adversarial', schema: CRITIQUE_SCHEMA }
    )
  ))).filter(Boolean)
  const approvals = critiques.filter(c => c.approved).length
  const allFlaws = critiques.flatMap(c => [...(c.fatal_flaws||[]), ...(c.fixes_required||[])])
  log(`Round ${round}: ${approvals}/${critiques.length} panels approved. ${allFlaws.length} issues raised.`)
  if (approvals === critiques.length) { log(`CONVERGED round ${round}: full simulated panel approved.`); phase('Converge'); break }
  feedback = allFlaws.map((f,i)=>`${i+1}. ${f}`).join('\n')
}
phase('Converge')
return {
  product: solution?.idea_name || shape.product_name,
  rounds_used: round,
  solution_doc: `${DIR}/SOLUTION.md`,
  research_docs: `${DIR}/`,
  idea_brief: solution?.idea_brief,
  subagent_roles: solution?.subagent_roles,
  self_verification_plan: solution?.self_verification_plan,
}

aiTitle: Design perfect hackathon prompt with parallel agent research

content.text:
Launched (`w2xilsk0z`). This run is different from the first three in two important ways:

1. **It's pre-grounded, not exploratory.** All 10 sweep agents read your four grounding docs first (judge panel, decision brief, Drive screens, idea-1 CalFresh research), then harden the *locked* build rather than re-inventing it. The lanes are tuned to the real open engineering questions: PolicyEngine ÷ Opus routing, the eval harness, the live voice-verification stack + failure handling + TCPA/CA compliance, the typed AEO/MCP interface, the CA program-stack data with 2026 date-awareness, compliant Rx pathways, the deploy stack, up-to-14 sub-agent fan-out + the "catches its own failure" moment, the judge-tuned demo script, and the take-up-gap impact + who-pays.

2. **The critics ARE the judges.** Five adversarial panels modeled on the real roster — Marius/voice-evals, Alistair+Gagan/typed-contracts+why-Opus, Zhen+Conner+Ciro/orchestration+evals, the VC bloc, and a DQ/safety referee. It only converges when all five sign off, so the output is pre-stress-tested against the exact people who'll grade you.

The locked decisions are baked in: applicant-facing + B2G who-pays, model routing (PolicyEngine does the math, Opus only the hard reasoning), eval harness with a stated accuracy number, the live voice hero, up to 14 sub-agents, and the orchestrator may point at the Drive via `gws` for grounding as it works.

Outputs land in `research 4/`: the 10 sweep docs + `02-synthesis.md` + `SOLUTION.md` (idea brief + eval-driven rubric + the master prompt). I'll report back with the converged solution and the master prompt when the panel signs off.

No completion notification yet, so it's still in that judge-panel review / convergence loop. Zooming out: that master prompt **is** the build-day deliverable — once this lands, you have the paste-ready prompt that runs the whole autonomous build loop. So we're one phase from "done" on the prep.
