## 3. MASTER PROMPT (single prompt for Claude Code)

```
ultracode

Build this as a SAVED DYNAMIC WORKFLOW (.claude/workflows/onedoor.js) and run it end to end.
Precondition: Claude Code v2.1.154+. Concurrency caps: 16 concurrent / 1,000 total agents;
request UP TO 14 simultaneous sub-agents wherever a stage genuinely benefits, degrade gracefully
to min(16, cores-2), exponential backoff on rate limits. You MAY consult the shared Drive via the
`gws` CLI for grounding (org phone numbers, sample listings, applicant-confusion stats). Do your
work, verify with verify.sh, and report success ONLY when verify.sh emits <promise>RUBRIC_GREEN</promise>.

=== PRODUCT (LOCKED — do not re-open) ===
"One Door · California": one applicant-facing eligibility brain for every kind of help (CalFresh +
benefit stack + Rx cost-pathways + vouchers + utility/library access), one verified resource graph,
two front doors (a web SPA + a typed agent API). CA-first. Education/navigation, NEVER official
determination, legal advice, or medical advice. Free to the person; who-pays = Medi-Cal managed-care
plans via CalAIM Community Supports + food banks/gov outreach + GoodRx affiliate.

=== IMMUTABLE GOAL CLAUSE (restate VERBATIM in EVERY sub-agent prompt) ===
"ONE Cloudflare Worker serves the SPA + /api/* + /voice/* + /mcp + /.well-known/agent-card.json
(single `wrangler deploy`, one deterministic *.workers.dev URL; CPU-only metering so awaiting Opus
mid-call is safe — do NOT move LLM/voice callbacks to a hard-wall-clock serverless function).
PolicyEngine (self-hosted policyengine-us) computes ALL eligibility tests + dollars + pass/fail.
Opus 4.8 NEVER does benefit arithmetic; Opus does ONLY: (1) messy NL -> correctly period-keyed
household object, (2) rule-currency/date/status/county/program-path judgment, (3) parse unstructured
prose (Rx PAP/copay, Contributor-Studio URLs), (4) drive the live phone-verification dialogue + DTMF,
(5) plain-language/why-you-qualify/translate/read-aloud. Telephony is Vapi calling our Worker webhook;
Opus is a custom LLM via an OpenAI-compatible SSE proxy. Every output CITES {rule, source_id,
source_url, verified_at, confidence}. today = 2026-06-13 (explicit arg; no argless new Date())."

=== ALL NONDETERMINISTIC INPUTS VIA args ===
args = { today:"2026-06-13", county_default:"San Francisco", ruleset:"calfresh-ca-2026",
  scenarios:"test/personas.yaml", callee_allowlist:"config/callee_allowlist.json",
  worker_subdomain:<precomputed>, secrets:{CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN, CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID,
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, VAPI_API_KEY, VAPI_PHONE_NUMBER_ID, POLICYENGINE:"self-host-or-package"} }
Parameterized so another team reruns TOMORROW on a new program/state by editing args + RULESET.md only.

=== STEP 0 — PRECONDITIONS (STOP if any hard-fails) ===
- Verify all five secret classes are present.
- Stand up PolicyEngine in the FIRST hour: try `pip install policyengine-us` and call it directly
  (NO HTTP service) as the primary, lowest-risk path; confirm it returns SNAP dollars for a seed
  household BEFORE any app code. Only if you choose the HTTP route, pre-cache seed-persona results
  to a static asset so the demo has zero external dependency. If neither returns dollars, STOP.
- Pre-compute the single onedoor.<subdomain>.workers.dev URL into args (do NOT parse wrangler stdout).

=== EVAL-DRIVEN ORDER (contract BEFORE app code) ===
1. RULESET.md: every rule carries {value, source_id, source_url, verified_at, effective_from,
   effective_to}; ground each in published text; mark ungroundable rules KNOWN_GAP. RE-VERIFY DHCS
   Medi-Cal and CPUC CARE/FERA numbers live and lower confidence if you cannot.
2. test/personas.yaml in PolicyEngine/OpenFisca YAML (name/period/absolute_error_margin/input/output),
   each tagged oracle_source ∈ {POLICYENGINE_YAML, INDEPENDENT} and fail_to_pass|pass_to_pass.
   DERIVE the INDEPENDENT cases from hand-worked CBPP/USDA-FNS/LSNC/CDSS examples (transcribe the
   worked arithmetic + citation into RULESET.md). >= 10 INDEPENDENT cases required. Include flip-01
   (~160% FPL CA, federal-NOT -> CA-ELIGIBLE), the H.R.1 date/status/county seam personas, an Rx
   clinical-substitution-REFUSAL pass_to_pass case, and a college-student persona.
3. Prove the oracle discriminates: a deliberately-broken ruleset MUST FAIL the suite ON THE
   INDEPENDENT cases specifically. Only then write app code.

=== THE PIPELINE (barrier fan-out where independent; stage-gate where N+1 consumes N's VERIFIED output) ===
extract (Opus: NL -> household object) -> compute (PolicyEngine, deterministic) -> reason (Opus: why[],
date/status judgment) -> verify (a SEPARATE blackbox agent) -> deploy (single wrangler deploy) -> re-verify.

=== TWO ADVERSARIAL producer -> verifier -> re-run LOOPS (verifier is a DIFFERENT agent_id; producers
never grade themselves) ===
- FIRE-1 (verdict): independent verifier catches flip-01's federal default vs the CA-MCE oracle, forces
  re-run, flips NOT->ELIGIBLE; non-convergence -> "unclear — apply or talk to a navigator" fallback.
  Also wire FIRE-1b: a month-keyed input + annual-output request -> engine warnings[] -> Opus re-keys
  -> number corrects (use PolicyEngine's own warnings array, not a mock).
- FIRE-2 (voice): seed a mis-hear "four"->"nine"; the deterministic post-call transcript grep refuses
  the flip, Opus re-confirms, corrects before verifying.
Capture both to session_log.md so they survive a re-run and show on screen.

=== MODEL ROUTING (enact in the script, do not just claim) ===
Opus 4.8 ONLY for the 5 reasoning lanes + the fresh-context verifier. A cheaper model for fetch/dedup/
format/eval-mechanics. NEVER route eligibility math to any model — call PolicyEngine. Emit trace.jsonl
(one line per sub-agent: {stage, agent_id, model, parent, inputs_ref, outputs_ref, gated_by, tokens,
wall_ms, verdict}); FIRE-1/FIRE-2 catch events MUST appear with verifier agent_id != producer agent_id.

=== FAN-OUT (honest, traced) ===
Spawn up to 14 where it helps — one ruleset-curator+eval-builder per program (CalFresh, Medi-Cal, WIC,
CARE/FERA, CA LifeLine, CalEITC/YCTC, school-meals, SUN Bucks), one per persona-family, one per
resource-to-verify, one per Rx pathway. Capture a CONCURRENCY trace (overlapping start/end timestamps)
proving >= N agents ran SIMULTANEOUSLY on the genuinely-parallel stage. If you cannot show real overlap,
STATE HONESTLY "fan-out used where it helped (~4-6 concurrent); the eligibility pipeline is serial by
data-dependency." Do NOT claim 14-wide everywhere.

=== VOICE (the HERO) ===
Vapi owns telephony/STT/TTS/turn-taking/DTMF; Opus is a custom LLM via an ~80-line OpenAI-compatible SSE
proxy and decides ONLY say-next / press-digits / record-field. record_verification({field,value,
confidence,quote}) is gated by a deterministic post-call grep (quote MUST appear verbatim in transcript).
First spoken turn = the VERBATIM disclosure: "Hi, this is an AI assistant calling on a recorded line for
One Door California to confirm your hours and services — is now a good time?". Only dial numbers in
args.callee_allowlist (deterministic guard; refuse otherwise). Log {disclosure_spoken, recording_consent,
callee_is_published_business_line:true, consent_basis, opt_out_honored}. AMD => NEVER leave AI voicemail.
Latency budget: first-token < ~1.5s or Vapi filler plays; route ONLY hard turns to Opus. Implement the
full failure matrix as individually-tested handlers. PLACE A REAL CALL TODAY that flips a real DB row to
verified; commit the transcript + call_id (timestamped today) as the rubric-enforced fallback so the
green check survives a dropped on-stage dial WITHOUT showing a mock. Pre-stage failure-matrix cases on
the ACTUAL venue network the morning of and capture those transcripts too. Add a test that feeds a
transcript NOT containing the claimed value and assert the record_verification write is REFUSED.

=== AEO / MCP + A2A ===
Author schema/*.json ONCE (four tools + a shared trust-envelope base {confidence, citations[],
as_of_date, status_assumptions, disclaimer} enforced in outputSchema so a citation-less output FAILS).
MCP server at /mcp (Streamable HTTP, rev 2025-06-18+, inputSchema+outputSchema+structuredContent). A2A
AgentCard at /.well-known/agent-card.json imports the SAME schema/*.json by path. A separate
consumer-agent makes a LIVE typed A2A call and captures request+response JSON. Prove single-source: a
test tampers ONE field in schema/ and asserts BOTH the MCP tools/call AND the A2A artifact fail.
rx_savings_paths carries non_clinical:true and excludes Medicare/Medicaid/TRICARE/VA from copay-card
paths AT SCHEMA LEVEL (assert a Medicaid-copay payload is REJECTED); GoodRx never cached and never
exposed via AEO (assert no GoodRx path on the tool surface, no GoodRx value persisted). OAuth2 scope
tiers; anonymous-by-default read/screen; verify:request privileged + rate-limited + audit-logged.

=== PRIVACY / SAFETY (enforced, not asserted) ===
Anonymous mode: a no-PII household still screens (assert it). No household PII persisted beyond the
session; the voice DB row stores only the business listing + transcript (assert via grep/test). Every
verdict renders "Screening estimate, not an official eligibility determination" + a real low-confidence
"apply or talk to a navigator" branch in the engine.

=== DELIVERABLES ===
RULESET.md, test/personas.yaml, RUBRIC.md, verify.sh, EVAL_REPORT.md (persona count, BOTH accuracy
numbers + coverage + dollar-error + λ derivation + FAIL_TO_PASS/PASS_TO_PASS table + flip-01 before/after
+ comparison to MyFriendBen >90%), session_log.md, trace.jsonl, FINAL.md (sourced impact constants +
one-line who-pays + wedge-vs-incumbents), PITCH_SCRIPT.md (3-act FOMO: Act1 2.7M told "no" by a
federal-default screener; Act2 incumbents sunset the assister path + H.R.1 makes explainers wrong on
dated seams + wrong = real harm; Act3 the live verified verdict + dollar figure for Maria, computed by
the engine, reasoned by Opus, proven by the independent eval number + the live call; name the weakness:
consumer WTP is weak -> that's why who-pays is B2B/CalAIM), the .claude/workflows/onedoor.js workflow.
State Maria is a CITED COMPOSITE, not a real user; cite Nourish California 2024 + GetCalFresh sunset 2025
+ one pulled real applicant-confusion artifact as the real-world grounding.

=== DEFINITION OF DONE (a GREEN CHECK, not an assertion) ===
Run verify.sh verbatim. Do NOT report success until it confirms: A1-A2 (no benefit math outside the
adapter), B2 (independent_oracle_precision >= 0.90 on N>=10) with B1(a) consistency printed separately,
B3 (coverage >= 0.70 AND flip-01 + every H.R.1 seam ANSWERED+correct), B4/D3'/E7 adversarial rejections
all bite, C1-C2 (live URL 200), D1 (a real transcript dated today flipped a real row, gated by D3,
callee in allowlist per D1'), E1-E8 (typed MCP + single-source schema proven by one edit breaking two
checks), F (date-deterministic), G (anonymous + no-PII-persisted), H (a FIRE catch fired and is logged),
I (trace.jsonl + concurrency overlap or honest statement), J (Tier-1 reproducible offline). Emit
<promise>RUBRIC_GREEN</promise> only then; log any exception explicitly. LOOP build->self-correct->
test->deploy->verify until green.
```

---

## 4. SUBAGENT ROLES (model per role) + SELF-VERIFICATION PLAN

### Subagent roles (model routing enacted in the workflow script)
1. **household-object-builder [Opus 4.8]** — messy NL → correctly period-keyed structured household object; ambiguity/date/status/county judgment. Boundary: no math.
2. **policyengine-adapter [Sonnet]** — typed object builder + `policyengine-us` client + period-key validation + readback of named engine variables. Boundary: no reasoning/NL/math of its own.
3. **ruleset-curator (×8, one per program) [Sonnet]** — writes its slice of `RULESET.md` with `{value, source_id, verified_at, effective_from/to}`; marks ungroundable rules KNOWN_GAP; re-verifies DHCS/CPUC live. Genuinely parallel.
4. **persona-builder (×N, one per persona-family) [Opus 4.8 for INDEPENDENT cases, Sonnet for PE-derived]** — derives personas from named oracles (INDEPENDENT from hand-worked CBPP/USDA-FNS/LSNC/CDSS). Genuinely parallel.
5. **eval-harness [Opus 4.8]** — runner diffing system end-to-end vs personas; computes BOTH accuracy numbers + coverage + dollar-error; calibrates and documents λ; oracle-discrimination check on INDEPENDENT cases.
6. **worker-backend [Sonnet]** — single Cloudflare Worker (SPA + `/api/*` + `/voice/*` + `/mcp` + AgentCard), one `wrangler deploy`.
7. **frontend-spa [Sonnet]** — the four screens incl. the on-screen Rules-engine | Opus-4.8 split panel; visible "Screening estimate" string + navigator fallback.
8. **voice-agent [Sonnet]** — Opus custom-LLM SSE proxy, Vapi assistant config (disclosure opener + DTMF policy + `record_verification`), post-call grep gate, latency budget, the full failure matrix; places the real captured-today call.
9. **mcp-aeo [Sonnet]** — `schema/*.json` (four tools + shared trust envelope), MCP server (in+out schemas + structuredContent), A2A AgentCard importing the same schema files; OAuth2 scopes + anonymous default + Rx compliance at schema level.
10. **rx-pathway (×6, one per pathway) [Sonnet; Opus for PAP/copay prose parsing]** — copay-card parser (federal-exclusion hard gate), PAP matcher, Cost Plus formulary, SIRUM router, NeedyMeds/RxAssist extractor, GoodRx live-only fetch (never cached/never via AEO).
11. **consumer-agent [Sonnet]** — a separate process that makes the LIVE typed A2A call on stage and captures request+response JSON. Genuinely parallel with the server.
12. **resource-verifier (×N, one per listing) [Sonnet]** — drives a verification call per listing; genuinely parallel; each returns a VERIFIED artifact joined at a barrier.
13. **fresh-context blackbox verifier [Opus 4.8]** — re-grades every verdict (must catch flip-01's federal default), re-grades voice writes; DIFFERENT `agent_id` than any producer; anti-self-preference. Performs the FIRE catches.
14. **orchestrator [session model, Opus 4.8]** — holds the loop/branching, enforces model routing per stage, emits `trace.jsonl` + the concurrency trace, runs `verify.sh`, loops until `RUBRIC_GREEN`.

*Haiku is intentionally NOT used here — every lane is either irreversible (voice/submit-like), correctness-gating, or craft. Nothing is purely low-stakes enough for Haiku in this build.*

### Self-verification plan
- **The model proves done, never asserts it.** `verify.sh` (committed, run verbatim) curls the single Worker origin and grades every Section A-J MUST against the LIVE deployment + the pinned engine, capturing all output verbatim to `session_log.md`, and emits `<promise>RUBRIC_GREEN</promise>` only when all pass.
- **Two reproducibility tiers `[FB-17, FB-12]`:** Tier-1 (offline, no live secrets — health, /api/screen, eval suite vs pinned engine, MCP/A2A schema validation incl. the one-edit-breaks-two-checks proof, citation guard, the independent-oracle accuracy gate, anonymous-mode, no-PII-persisted) is reproducible by a judge in <3 min tomorrow. Tier-2 (the real voice flip) asserts against the captured-today real transcript + real DB row + real call_id, so a non-answering callee at re-run time keeps GREEN.
- **Adversarial gates must BITE, proven by deliberate breakage:** B4 (broken ruleset fails the INDEPENDENT cases), D3' (fabricated-field transcript is refused), E4 (one tampered schema field breaks both MCP and A2A), E7/E7' (Medicaid-copay payload rejected; GoodRx not in AEO/cache). Each is a check that fails when the guard is removed, not a claim.
- **Two honest accuracy numbers `[FB-1, FB-4, FB-16]`:** B1(a) engine-agreement (consistency, informational) printed separately from B2 independent-oracle precision (the gate, ≥0.90 on N≥10), with coverage ≥0.70 and flip-01 + every H.R.1 seam in the ANSWERED set. The demo states both aloud. λ derivation documented (B5).
- **The self-catch is a forced, reproducible orchestration event (H1/H2):** a separate verifier `agent_id` catches FIRE-1 (or FIRE-2), forces a re-run, and the green check is contingent on the catch having fired and been captured to `session_log.md` + `trace.jsonl` (I2).
- **Traceability for Gagan (I1-I3):** `trace.jsonl` makes per-stage model routing + who-gated-whom + token/latency machine-inspectable; the concurrency trace proves real parallel overlap or states the honest serial-by-data-dependency truth.
- **Rerunnable tomorrow (J1-J2):** swap `args.ruleset` + `args.scenarios`; the saved `.claude/workflows/onedoor.js` reruns the whole loop with zero code edits.

Done — I've stopped the loop. Here's the honest picture and what you've got.

**What I cut into:** the round-1 `SOLUTION.md` is on disk and it's genuinely strong — the craft agent was thorough enough to pre-address ~25 anticipated judge concerns (tagged `[FB-n]` inline). import re,sys
t=open(sys.argv[1]).read()
# split into panel blocks
blocks=re.split(r'\n### PANEL:',t)
for b in blocks[1:]:
    name=b.split('\n',1)[0].strip()[:70]
    appr=re.search(r'APPROVED:\s*(\w+)',b)
    fixes=re.findall(r'  (?:FATAL|FIX):\s*([^\n]+)',b)
    print(f"\n● {name}  -> APPROVED={appr.group(1) if appr else '?'}  ({len(fixes)} notes)")
    for fx in fixes[:4]:
        # first sentence only
        s=re.split(r'(?<=[.])\s',fx)[0]
        print("   -", s[:160])
PY

The product is locked and well-formed: **One Door · California** — one eligibility brain (PolicyEngine does the math, Opus does only the 5 reasoning lanes), the CalFresh→stack cascade, compliant Rx cost-pathways, a live voice phone-verification hero with a real captured-today fallback, typed MCP/AEO with a single-source-schema proof, a "Claude catches its own failure" moment, anonymous-mode privacy, and the CalAIM Community Supports who-pays line.

## The honest part: 3 real items the round-2 panel left open
I cut it mid-round-3, so these are unfixed. 4 of the 5 panels would pass; these three blocked:

1. **🔴 Real technical bug (Alistair) — fix this regardless of anything else.** The rubric stakes the headline accuracy gate (F2/B3) on **ABAWD work-requirement *waiver-county* personas that PolicyEngine structurally cannot compute.** You can't gate accuracy on cases the engine can't answer. Fix: add a **Step-0.5 engine-capability probe** (Zhen suggested the same) that runs *before* any persona is promised "ANSWERED" — anything PolicyEngine can't compute moves to the Opus date/status-reasoning lane or is marked `KNOWN_GAP`, never the engine gate.

2. **🟡 Eval independence (Marius).** He still reads the headline accuracy as PolicyEngine-vs-itself. The doc *does* split it into two numbers (B1a consistency vs B2 independent-oracle ≥0.90 on N≥10) — so it's largely addressed, but he wants the independent set to clearly carry the Opus-reasoning lanes where the system can be wrong in a way the engine can't self-catch. Tighten the ≥10 INDEPENDENT cases to lean on extraction/date/status reasoning, not engine math.

3. **🟡 Who-pays evidence (VC bloc).** They want a **sourced per-enrollment dollar number** (a real CalAIM Community Supports / county-outreach rate band with a citation) and **one real applicant-confusion artifact**, both in `FINAL.md` — not just a reference to them. Plus naming Maria as a cited composite out loud (already in the brief).

None of these touch the architecture — they're a rubric correction + two evidence/framing additions. The master prompt itself is sound.

message.content: additional context that you should have - we're pushing the boundaries of intelligence, and we want ideas that previously wasn't possible. let's hyperfocus on one idea. give us the pros and cons. we want to showcase the new possibilities that didn't exist previously. furthermore, we want this idea of shapeshifting UX that meet the user wherever they are - including language, accessibility, device, etc. We also want this intelligence to be an advocate in the room, prepping in-persons conversation, AND connecting online with offline requirements from government docs. traditionally, govt services are expansive but also siloed, and it can take a whole day to apply for special accomodations for public schools or healthcare. we want to showoff the reasoning, we want actions taken, and we want the connect of digital to physical spaces.

content.text:
Locking onto the one idea, now elevated with everything you just added. Here's the crystallized version, then a straight pros/cons.

## The idea (hyperfocused)

**One Door** — an intelligence that does what previously took a benefits navigator, a policy expert, a translator, and a full day of office visits, all at once, for one person:

- **Figures out everything you qualify for across siloed systems** (CalFresh ↔ Medi-Cal ↔ WIC ↔ school accommodations ↔ utility/housing) — the systems that don't talk to each other and each eat a day.
- **Shapeshifting UX** — the interface regenerates *per person*: their language, reading level, modality (voice / large-type / screen-reader / SMS), and device. Not pre-built locale files — generated in the moment by the model.
- **Advocate in the room** — preps the in-person conversation (what they'll be asked at the county office or the IEP/504 meeting, what to bring, how to answer), and can be the live voice on the phone.
- **Bridges digital → physical** — reads the government docs/forms/requirement lists, turns them into a concrete action plan + a printable packet + the offline steps (which office, what appointment, which signatures), and places the call that verifies it.

The trifecta you want on stage: **reasoning** (reconciling conflicting siloed rules) + **actions taken** (a real call, a generated packet) + **digital-to-physical** (online eligibility → the offline requirements and the room you walk into).

---

## PROS

- **It showcases genuinely new capability, not a faster old thing.** Shapeshifting UX (a UI that's generated for *you* — Tagalog, 5th-grade reading level, voice-first, on a $40 Android — with no i18n team or accessibility audit) is something that *could not have been built* before models this good. That's exactly the "didn't exist previously" bar you're aiming at.
- **The siloed-services pain is real and visceral.** "A whole day to apply for a public-school accommodation or a healthcare benefit, across offices that don't share data" is a problem every judge instantly believes. Collapsing it to minutes is the demo.
- **Perfect fit for this judge panel.** Reasoning over conflicting rules + real actions + verifiable-done is what 6 of 10 judges live for. The "advocate in the room" + live call is the kind of agent-does-real-work moment Marius/Ciro/Conner reward.
- **Digital→physical is the freshest axis.** Most AI demos stay on-screen. An agent that reads a government PDF's offline requirements and produces the physical packet + the appointment + the call is a memorable, rarely-seen bridge.
- **Impact narrative is unbeatable.** The advocate that 2.7M eligible-but-unenrolled Californians don't have — equity, the take-up gap, "$X you didn't know about." This is the move that won prizes for prior Anthropic winners.
- **Shapeshifting UX *is* a load-bearing Opus use**, which directly answers "why Opus?" — generating a correct, accessible, translated, reading-level-appropriate interface in real time is reasoning, not a template.

## CONS / RISKS

- **Scope is now the #1 threat.** Shapeshifting UX + advocate-prep + IEP/504 + healthcare accommodations + the benefit stack is *five products*. The judges explicitly punish "ten shallow integrations." If you try to demo all of it, you get a thin, fragile demo. **This ambition must be the *narrated vision*, with ONE path built deep and live.**
- **Shapeshifting UX can read as a gimmick if it's cosmetic.** Translating a label is unimpressive. It only lands if the *reasoning and the content* change with the person (a screen-reader user gets a genuinely restructured flow, not just ARIA tags). Easy to over-promise, hard to make real in 6h.
- **The digital→physical "packet + offline steps" is where correctness gets dangerous.** If the agent tells someone the wrong documents to bring to an IEP meeting or a Medi-Cal interview, that's a wasted day for a vulnerable person — the exact harm the panel will probe. Needs the same cited/verifiable-done discipline as eligibility.
- **Special-ed (IEP/504) and healthcare accommodations are their own legal thickets** — different rules, different agencies, less clean than CalFresh's rules-as-code (PolicyEngine doesn't cover IEP). Adding them widens the surface where the model can be confidently wrong. (Also a light DQ flag: keep it clearly *accommodations-navigation*, not an "AI for education" tutor — it's fine, but don't let it drift.)
- **The live demo's variance goes up with every modality.** A live multilingual voice call on venue wifi, generating an accessible UI live, *and* a real phone verification — each adds a failure point Marius/Ciro will stress. More surfaces = more yellow-at-3:00 risk.
- **6 hours is brutal for this surface area.** Every dimension you add to the *build* (vs the *narration*) trades against getting one path verified, deployed, and demo-solid.

---

**Bottom line:** the expanded vision is the right *story* and genuinely shows new possibility. The discipline that wins: **build ONE flow deep and live — one persona, in a non-English language, on a phone, who gets a shapeshifted accessible verdict + a real verification call + a prepped "here's what to bring and say at your interview" packet** — and *narrate* the siloed-services / IEP / healthcare expansion as the platform vision. Reasoning + a real action + the digital-to-physical bridge, all in one tight path.

Want me to fold this elevated framing (shapeshifting UX + advocate-in-room + digital→physical) into `SOLUTION.md` as the locked direction, keeping the one-deep-path discipline — then wait for your "ready to go"?

content.text:
Pausing the edits there — the live-languages + live-fact-check refinement is already in `SOLUTION.md` (lane 5 and 6), and it's consistent with any direction, so I'll leave it. Now to your actual question: **which idea, and how narrow.**

## First: which of the four to run with

Honest ranking against your real goals (show *new* possibility + this judge panel + a 6h demo that holds up live):

| Idea | New-possibility | Judge-panel fit | Mission/energy | Demo safety | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **One Door** | **Highest** — shapeshifting UX + a live agent dispatched to verify unknowns is genuinely *not buildable before* | **Best** — voice, verifiable-done, real-world action, underserved mission (James, Sara, Mar all on-thesis) | **Highest** — it's what the team is fired up about | Medium (scope risk) | **Run with this** |
| CalFreshTruth | Medium | Good | Subsumed by One Door | High | It's a *subset* of One Door |
| Redline | Medium (review tools exist) | Good on orchestration, weak on voice/impact | Off-mission | High | No — wrong room |
| TrapHunter | Medium | Niche; DQ-adjacent (job-screener) | Off-mission | Medium | No |

**Run with One Door.** It's not really a 4-way choice — CalFreshTruth is a slice of it, and Redline/TrapHunter don't match what excites you or what this specific panel rewards. The decision that matters is **how narrow.**

## The real call: narrowing One Door

Your scope is ballooning (eligibility stack + shapeshifting + advocate-in-room + multilanguage + live fact-check + IEP/healthcare). The judges *punish* sprawl. So: **build ONE deep path live, narrate the rest as the platform.** Three candidate paths — each still shows reasoning + a real action + digital→physical, but they differ in risk and wow:

questions.question: Which ONE deep path do we build live (the rest becomes the narrated roadmap)?

options.label: Verified verdict in your language
options.description: Persona speaks Spanish → live multilingual verdict + dollars (engine-computed) → a key fact is unverifiable → agent places a LIVE call to confirm it on screen → flips to verified, in-language. Hits multilanguage + live fact-check + digital→physical. Tightest + safest. RECOMMENDED.

options.label: The advocate in the room
options.description: Verdict → agent generates the prepped in-person packet (what to bring, what they'll be asked, your answers) + the offline steps. Strong 'advocate' story, but more static, less live-action wow.

options.label: Cross-silo (benefits + school IEP)
options.description: Collapse two siloed domains — CalFresh + a public-school accommodation (IEP/504). Highest ambition, but IEP rules aren't in PolicyEngine → correctness risk, and the riskiest 6h build.

options.label: Narrate as roadmap
options.description: Show the one deep path live; pitch shapeshifting UX, advocate-prep, IEP/healthcare, and the full stack as the platform vision in the 3-min story. Keeps the demo safe. RECOMMENDED.

options.label: Build a second shallow path
options.description: Add one more visible path (e.g. a second program) to show breadth. Riskier — trades demo solidity for surface area the judges may read as sprawl.

questions.question: Which ONE deep path do we build live (the rest becomes the narrated roadmap)?

options.label: Verified verdict in your language
options.description: Persona speaks Spanish → live multilingual verdict + dollars (engine-computed) → a key fact is unverifiable → agent places a LIVE call to confirm it on screen → flips to verified, in-language. Hits multilanguage + live fact-check + digital→physical. Tightest + safest. RECOMMENDED.

options.label: The advocate in the room
options.description: Verdict → agent generates the prepped in-person packet (what to bring, what they'll be asked, your answers) + the offline steps. Strong 'advocate' story, but more static, less live-action wow.

options.label: Cross-silo (benefits + school IEP)
options.description: Collapse two siloed domains — CalFresh + a public-school accommodation (IEP/504). Highest ambition, but IEP rules aren't in PolicyEngine → correctness risk, and the riskiest 6h build.

content:
Opus 4.8 orchestrates:
    •    Routes pure math (gross/net income tests, deductions, categorical eligibility, ABAWD/work requirements, post-April/June 2026 immigrant and work-rule changes) to a deterministic rules engine.
    •    Uses Opus only for the hard parts: parsing unstructured inputs, reconciling conflicting or ambiguous rules, spotting uncertainty, and deciding exactly what fact needs external verification.
    •    Triggers a live, disclosed voice agent call to a real county benefits information line or authorized CBO to confirm one specific high-uncertainty fact (e.g., current treatment of a part-time student in a mixed-status household with an elderly member, or required documents under the new rules).
    •    Opus then reviews the full trace (its own initial reasoning + rules output + call transcript), explicitly catches and corrects its own error or overconfident assumption, updates the output with citations and a confidence score, and produces: “Likely eligible. Estimated benefit if approved: $XXX/month. Verified fact from county line at [timestamp]. Next step: apply via BenefitsCal with these exact 3 documents. Full audit log attached.”

content.text:
Both locked:
- **Live path: "Verified verdict in your language"**
- **Everything else: narrated as roadmap**

That's the scope discipline this panel rewards. Let me lock it into `SOLUTION.md` — a scope header up top, plus making the hero (live language switch + live fact-verification) consistent across the prompt and actually graded in the rubric.

input.old_string: ## 1. IDEA BRIEF
input.new_string:
## 0. LOCKED DEMO SCOPE (narrowed 2026-06-13)

**Build ONE path live; narrate the rest.** The judges punish sprawl — so one deep, verified, live path, and the platform vision stays in the pitch.

- **THE LIVE PATH — "Verified verdict in your language":** a persona describes their situation **in Spanish** → One Door returns a live, in-language verdict + dollars (PolicyEngine-computed, cited) → a fact needed for the result is **UNVERIFIABLE** (e.g. a local food bank's current hours, a listing detail not in any dataset) → the agent **dispatches a live verification agent on screen** (a real outbound call to an allowlisted published line) → the listing flips to `verified` and the answer updates, still in the user's language. The user can switch **English ⇄ Spanish ⇄ 中文 ⇄ فارسی** at any moment; the engine result is byte-identical across languages.
- **The three things on stage:** *reasoning* (PolicyEngine + Opus over conflicting/dated rules) · *a real action* (the live verification call) · *the digital→physical bridge* (online eligibility → a real-world fact confirmed by phone).
- **NARRATED AS ROADMAP — NOT built live:** the full benefit stack beyond the hero, the advocate-in-the-room interview prep + packet, IEP/504 + healthcare accommodations, the Contributor Studio. Pitch as the platform; do not build in the 6h window.
- Everything in Sections 1–4 below is scoped to serve THIS path first.

---

input.new_string:
- **THE LIVE PATH — "Verified verdict in your language" (canonical orchestration flow):**
  1. A persona describes their situation in plain language, **in Spanish** (switchable to English ⇄ 中文 ⇄ فارسی at any moment; the engine result is byte-identical across languages, Farsi RTL).
  2. **Opus routes the pure math to the deterministic rules engine** (PolicyEngine): gross/net income tests, deductions, categorical eligibility, ABAWD/work requirements, and the post-April/June-2026 immigrant + work-rule changes.
  3. **Opus does only the hard parts:** parse the unstructured input, reconcile conflicting/ambiguous rules, **spot uncertainty, and decide the ONE specific fact that needs external verification.**
  4. It **triggers a live, disclosed voice-agent call to a real county benefits information line (or authorized CBO)** to confirm that single high-uncertainty fact — e.g. *the treatment of a part-time student in a mixed-status household with an elderly member*, or *the exact documents required under the new rules*.
  5. **Opus reviews the full trace** (its own initial reasoning + the rules-engine output + the call transcript), **explicitly catches and corrects its own error or overconfident assumption**, and updates the result with citations + a confidence score.
  6. It produces: *"**Likely eligible.** Estimated benefit if approved: **$XXX/month**. Verified fact from the county line at **[timestamp]**. Next step: apply via BenefitsCal with these **exact 3 documents**. Full audit log attached."*

input.old_string: ### GREEN definition
input.new_string:
### Section K — Live multilingual switch + unverifiable-fact → live agent + canonical output (the locked hero)
- **K1 (MUST — language is presentation, math is invariant):** switching EN → ES → 中文 → فارسی on a rendered verdict re-renders the card + why-you-qualify + next-steps + read-aloud in that language; `verify.sh` asserts the PolicyEngine verdict, dollar figures, and `citations[]` are **BYTE-IDENTICAL** across all four (Farsi RTL). A language whose dollars/verdict differ ⇒ FAIL.
- **K2 (MUST — uncertainty triggers a live county-line/CBO call, never a guess):** the fact Opus flags as the single highest-uncertainty item (no `engine_provenance`, no citation, or a low-confidence rule reading) MUST trigger a live disclosed call to an allowlisted **county benefits information line / authorized CBO** (or a live official-source fetch), written back with `{verified_at, method, provenance}` BEFORE the result is finalized. A test injects an ungrounded fact and asserts it is live-verified OR withheld behind a "we're checking this" state — never rendered as fact. An ungrounded fact shown without verification ⇒ FAIL.
- **K3 (MUST — canonical output shape + self-catch reflected):** the finalized result contains ALL of: `{verdict (likely/possibly/likely-not), estimated_benefit, verified_fact + timestamp, next_step, exact_documents[], confidence, audit_log_ref}`, and the audit log shows Opus reviewed the full trace and the corrected value differs from its pre-call assumption (ties to H1's self-catch). A result missing any field, or one where no review/correction step is logged, ⇒ FAIL.

### GREEN definition

content.text:
Folded in. Here's what changed in `SOLUTION.md`:

- **Section 0 (the demo)** is now the canonical 6-step flow you wrote: Spanish intake → math to PolicyEngine → Opus spots the one high-uncertainty fact → live disclosed call to a **county benefits line / authorized CBO** → Opus reviews the full trace, **catches and corrects its own overconfident assumption** → emits the exact output ("Likely eligible. $XXX/mo. Verified fact from county line at [timestamp]. Apply via BenefitsCal with these exact 3 documents. Full audit log attached.").
- **Master prompt immutable clause** — updated to the 6 lanes and the same canonical output, so every sub-agent inherits it verbatim.
- **Rubric** — new **Section K** grades the hero: `K1` language switch is presentation-only (engine result byte-identical across EN/ES/中文/فارسی), `K2` the flagged-uncertain fact must trigger the live county-line/CBO call (never a guess), `K3` the output carries every field + a logged self-catch where the corrected value differs from the pre-call assumption. Added to the GREEN gate.

**One consistency gap I did *not* touch (your call):** the older **Section D** voice rubric + the disclosure opener still say "confirm your *hours and services* / flip a *listing* to verified" — that was the food-bank-hours framing. The hero now verifies an *eligibility fact* via a county line. Want me to reconcile Section D so the disclosure becomes "to confirm a benefits eligibility detail" and the verified artifact is the eligibility fact, not a listing's hours? That's the last thread to make the doc fully internally consistent.

Otherwise, keep the refinements coming — or when the team's happy, say **"ready to go"** and I'll hand you the exact block to paste into a fresh Claude Code session.

content.content:
111:### Section D — Live voice phone-verification (the HERO) with enforced REAL fallback `[FB-3, FB-6, FB-12, FB-15]`
112:- **D1 (MUST):** A REAL outbound call placed **TODAY** flipped a real DB listing row `unverified → verified`. `verify.sh` asserts the row's `verified_at` is dated today AND is backed by a real `call_id` AND a real transcript on disk. The on-stage live dial is the *primary* attempt; this captured-today real artifact is the rubric-enforced backstop. **`verify.sh` accepts "a real transcript dated today flipped row X" — it does NOT require the flip to happen inside the 3-min window.** This is a real call, replayed; never a mock.
113:- **D1' (MUST — scoped-egress allowlist, deterministic guard) `[FB-10]`:** the dialed number MUST be in a committed allowlist of consented, published business lines (`config/callee_allowlist.json`). `verify.sh` asserts the call's `to` number is in the allowlist; any number not in it ⇒ the call is refused and the check FAILS. No applicant cell can ever be dialed.
114:- **D2 (MUST — compliance audit log) `[FB-23]`:** the per-call audit log records `{disclosure_spoken:true, recording_consent, callee_is_published_business_line:true, consent_basis, opt_out_honored}`. The verbatim AB-2905/TCPA disclosure opener ("Hi, this is an AI assistant calling on a recorded line for One Door California to confirm your hours and services — is now a good time?") MUST be the first spoken turn and MUST appear in the transcript.
115:- **D3 (MUST — post-call grep gate bites):** every `record_verification({field,value,confidence,quote})` write is gated by a deterministic grep that the `quote` appears verbatim in the transcript and supports `value`. A field not backed by an exact quote ⇒ the write is REFUSED.
116:- **D3' (MUST — gate-rejects-fabrication, the voice analog of B4) `[FB-7]`:** a test feeds a transcript that does NOT contain the claimed value; `verify.sh` asserts the `record_verification` write is REFUSED. Proves the gate bites; does not merely assert it gates.
117:- **D4 (MUST — latency budget, no dead air) `[FB-15]`:** the realtime path asserts first-token < ~1.5s OR Vapi filler audio plays; only HARD turns (ambiguity, hostile IVR, structured extraction) route to Opus, trivial turns to a fast model. A logged turn with >1.5s silence and no filler ⇒ FAIL.
118:- **D5 (MUST — failure matrix, each individually tested, run on venue network the morning of):** no-answer (retry w/ backoff in open window), dead/disconnected (no retry, flag stale), voicemail (AMD → hang up, NEVER leave AI voicemail), hostile/deep IVR (DTMF then abort after K hops), mis-hear / low-ASR (read-back-confirm before recording), drop (re-call once, dedupe on call_id). Each has its own logged outcome + human-fallback queue; none auto-verifies. Captured transcripts committed.
144:  - **FIRE-2 (voice, preferred demo beat):** a seeded mis-hear "four"→"nine"; the post-call grep (D3) catches the recorded field is not backed by an exact quote, refuses the flip, forces Opus to re-confirm, corrects 9pm→4pm before verifying.
162:`<promise>RUBRIC_GREEN</promise>` iff: A1-A2, B1-B7, C1-C2, D1-D5 (+D1',D3'), E1-E8 (+E7'), F1-F2, G1-G3, H1-H2, I1-I3, J1-J2, K1-K3 all PASS or carry a logged documented exception. The headline accuracy spoken aloud is **B2's independent-oracle precision**, with B1(a) consistency stated separately.
265:One Door California to confirm your hours and services — is now a good time?". Only dial numbers in
308:B3 (coverage >= 0.70 AND flip-01 + every H.R.1 seam ANSWERED+correct), B4/D3'/E7 adversarial rejections
309:all bite, C1-C2 (live URL 200), D1 (a real transcript dated today flipped a real row, gated by D3,
310:callee in allowlist per D1'), E1-E8 (typed MCP + single-source schema proven by one edit breaking two
342:- **Adversarial gates must BITE, proven by deliberate breakage:** B4 (broken ruleset fails the INDEPENDENT cases), D3' (fabricated-field transcript is refused), E4 (one tampered schema field breaks both MCP and A2A), E7/E7' (Medicaid-copay payload rejected; GoodRx not in AEO/cache). Each is a check that fails when the guard is removed, not a claim.

### Section D — Live voice phone-verification (the HERO) with enforced REAL fallback `[FB-3, FB-6, FB-12, FB-15]`
- **D1 (MUST):** A REAL outbound call placed **TODAY** flipped a real DB listing row `unverified → verified`. `verify.sh` asserts the row's `verified_at` is dated today AND is backed by a real `call_id` AND a real transcript on disk. input.new_string:
### Section D — Live voice verification of a COMPLEX eligibility question (the HERO) with enforced REAL fallback `[FB-3, FB-6, FB-12, FB-15]`
> The call is reserved for a genuinely complex, NON-searchable **eligibility** question — never facts a lookup resolves (hours, address, phone). Easily-searchable or engine-computable facts are answered by PolicyEngine or a cited official-source fetch; only an ambiguous / edge-case eligibility reading escalates to a live call.
- **D0 (MUST — call only when the fact is complex + non-searchable):** before any dial, Opus records a `call_justification` explaining why this fact could NOT be resolved by PolicyEngine or a cited official-source fetch (it is an ambiguous / edge-case eligibility reading — e.g. treatment of a part-time student in a mixed-status household with an elderly member, or the exact documents required under the 2026 rules). `verify.sh` asserts `call_justification` exists AND the verified field is NOT a trivially-searchable listing attribute. **A call placed to confirm hours / address / phone ⇒ FAIL** (that is the engine's or a web fetch's job, not a call).
- **D1 (MUST):** A REAL outbound call placed **TODAY** to a county benefits information line / authorized CBO **resolved a high-uncertainty eligibility question** and wrote the answer + attribution to the audit record. `verify.sh` asserts the record's `verified_at` is dated today AND is backed by a real `call_id` AND a real transcript on disk. The on-stage live dial is the *primary* attempt; this captured-today real artifact is the rubric-enforced backstop. **`verify.sh` accepts "a real transcript dated today resolved question X" — it does NOT require it to happen inside the 3-min window.** This is a real call, replayed; never a mock.

content.text:
Voice rubric reworked — calls are now gated to complex, non-searchable eligibility questions (the new **D0** fails the build if a call is placed for hours/address/phone), the disclosure opener and the mis-hear self-catch are reframed to eligibility, and the verified artifact is an eligibility answer rather than a listing.

## What `research 4/SOLUTION.md` now contains (paragraph summary)

It's the complete, paste-ready build package for **One Door · California**, scoped to one deep live demo path. **Section 0** locks the demo: a person describes their situation in Spanish (switchable live to English/中文/فارسی, engine result identical across languages), Opus routes all benefit math to the deterministic PolicyEngine and reserves itself for the hard parts, it spots the single highest-uncertainty eligibility fact and places a live disclosed call to a real county benefits line or authorized CBO to resolve it, then reviews the full trace, catches and corrects its own overconfident assumption, and emits the canonical output ("Likely eligible. $XXX/mo. Verified fact from the county line at [timestamp]. Apply via BenefitsCal with these exact 3 documents. Full audit log attached."), with everything else (the full benefit stack, advocate interview-prep, IEP/healthcare, Contributor Studio) narrated as roadmap rather than built. **Section 1** is the idea brief — the quantified take-up gap, applicant-facing with a CalAIM/Medi-Cal-managed-care who-pays line, the model-routing architecture, the safety posture (eligibility education not determination; cited, date/status-aware), and why it isn't on the prohibited list. **Section 2** is the eval-driven, machine-gradable rubric (Sections A–K): the engine-does-the-math proof, a two-number eligibility accuracy gate with an independent oracle and a coverage floor, the live URL checks, the complex-eligibility voice-call gate with real-fallback and fabrication-rejection tests, typed MCP/AEO with a single-source-schema proof, date-aware H.R.1 personas, enforced privacy/anonymous mode, the "Claude catches its own failure" requirement, an orchestration trace with honest fan-out, rerunnability, and the new Section K (live language switch is presentation-only, uncertainty triggers a real county-line call, and the canonical output shape with a logged self-catch). **Section 3** is the single `ultracode` master prompt that drives the whole autonomous build→test→deploy→verify loop with up-to-14 sub-agents and an immutable goal clause every sub-agent inherits. **Section 4** lists the 14 model-routed sub-agent roles and the self-verification plan where "done" is a green check `verify.sh` produces, never a model assertion. Around it sit the grounding docs: the full judge-panel brief, your decision-grade venture brief, the Drive UI screens, and the idea-1 CalFresh research.

Ready for the next step (the copy-paste kickoff) whenever you are, or more refinements.

content.text:
Edits locked in. Now your question — here's my understanding of **how the UI shows all CA benefits while only CalFresh runs the verified engine.**

## The two-tier shelf

The UI is the "One Door" shelf (per your Drive screens): all CA programs shown, grouped by need (Food / Medicine / Housing / Utilities / Childcare / Free access), sorted by value. But the cards come in **two visually distinct tiers**:

**Tier 1 — CalFresh (the one verified card):** full engine output — verdict (likely/possibly/likely-not), a specific **$XXX/mo** figure from PolicyEngine, cited reasoning, the verified-fact-from-the-live-call, confidence score, exact next-step documents. Gets a "✓ verified" treatment.

**Tier 2 — every other program (discover cards):** what it is in plain language, a cited **"you may also qualify — here's why,"** and the next step — but **no dollar figure** and no claim of a computed verdict.

## How Tier 2 gets its "you may also qualify" signal without the deep engine

This is the key part. Three honest, cheap mechanisms, all from the **same intake** already collected for CalFresh:

1. **Adjunctive/categorical signal (the strongest):** a CalFresh "likely yes" is itself the eligibility key for several programs by law — SNAP receipt → WIC income-eligible, school-meal direct certification, SUN Bucks, CA LifeLine. So a card can honestly say *"Because you likely qualify for CalFresh, you're likely also eligible for WIC"* — a **cited cross-program rule**, not a computed number.
2. **Lightweight threshold flags:** simple published-cutoff checks against the household already entered (income < 138% FPL → "check Medi-Cal"; ≤ 200% FPL → "check CARE utility discount"). A rule-of-thumb signal with the threshold cited — explicitly *not* a full benefit calculation.
3. **Catalog content:** each program's card text (summary, eligibility basis, next step, citation) comes from the connector/Contributor-Studio schema — the same machinery that lets the platform grow.

## The honesty line (now enforced in the rubric, C3)

Only CalFresh shows a dollar figure. Every Tier-2 card carries a citation + a "worth checking" signal + a next step, and the build **fails** if any non-CalFresh card displays a computed dollar amount. That's what keeps the breadth from becoming overclaiming — which this judge panel would punish instantly.

**So in one line:** the UI shows the whole California shelf; CalFresh is the single deep, verified, dollar-bearing, call-checked card; every other program is a discover card driven by cited categorical/threshold signals from the same intake — visible breadth, honest depth.

Does that match what you had in mind — specifically, are you good with Tier-2 cards being **cited "likely worth checking" signals (no dollars)**, or did you want them to show estimated dollar ranges too (which would mean widening the verified engine beyond CalFresh)?

attachment.snippet:
1	# One Door · California — Complete Build Solution
2	
3	> Claude Build Day (Cerebral Valley + Anthropic), Shack15 SF, 2026-06-13. Build on Opus 4.8.
4	> Deliverables: (1) idea brief, (2) eval-driven machine-gradable rubric, (3) single master prompt, (4) subagent roles + self-verification plan.
5	> This file folds in EVERY item of last-round judge-panel feedback (items 1-25). Where a feedback item changed the design, it is tagged inline like `[FB-1]`.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 0. LOCKED DEMO SCOPE (narrowed 2026-06-13)
10	
11	**Build ONE path live; narrate the rest.** The judges punish sprawl — so one deep, verified, live path, and the platform vision stays in the pitch.
12	
13	- **THE LIVE PATH — "Verified verdict in your language" (canonical orchestration flow):**
14	  1. A persona describes their situation in plain language, **in Spanish** (switchable to English ⇄ 中文 ⇄ فارسی at any moment; the engine result is byte-identical across languages, Farsi RTL).
15	  2. **Opus routes the pure math to the deterministic rules engine** (PolicyEngine): gross/net income tests, deductions, categorical eligibility, ABAWD/work requirements, and the post-April/June-2026 immigrant + work-rule changes.
16	  3. **Opus does only the hard parts:** parse the unstructured input, reconcile conflicting/ambiguous rules, **spot uncertainty, and decide the ONE specific fact that needs external verification.**
17	  4. It **triggers a live, disclosed voice-agent call to a real county benefits information line (or authorized CBO)** to confirm that single high-uncertainty fact — e.g. *the treatment of a part-time student in a mixed-status household with an elderly member*, or *the exact documents required under the new rules*.
18	  5. **Opus reviews the full trace** (its own initial reasoning + the rules-engine output + the call transcript), **explicitly catches and corrects its own error or overconfident assumption**, and updates the result with citations + a confidence score.
19	  6. It produces: *"**Likely eligible.** Estimated benefit if approved: **$XXX/month**. Verified fact from the county line at **[timestamp]**. Next step: apply via BenefitsCal with these **exact 3 documents**. Full audit log attached."*
20	  7. **Closing beat — recommend the rest of the stack.** Using the *same* `household_context`, the agent then recommends the other California benefits the household would likely qualify for, driven by **categorical / adjunctive eligibility** off the CalFresh result (a CalFresh "yes" is itself the signal): *"Because you likely qualify for CalFresh, you're also likely eligible for WIC, CA LifeLine, CARE, school-meal direct-cert, SUN Bucks, and CalEITC — here's why, and the next step for each."* Each recommendation cites its adjunctive/threshold rule and gives a next step, but carries **no computed dollar figure** (only CalFresh is engine-verified; deepening each is the roadmap).
21	- **The three things on stage:** *reasoning* (PolicyEngine + Opus over conflicting/dated rules) · *a real action* (the live verification call) · *the digital→physical bridge* (online eligibility → a real-world fact confirmed by phone).
22	- **ENGINE scope vs UI breadth (locked):** the verified engine computes **only CalFresh** (eligibility + dollars + the live-call fact-check); the **UI shows the full California benefits shelf** (Medi-Cal, WIC, CalEITC, CARE/FERA, LifeLine, school meals, SUN Bucks, Rx, vouchers) as discover cards so the breadth is visible — but **only CalFresh carries a computed dollar verdict**; the other cards show "likely worth checking + why + next step," never a fabricated number.
23	- **NARRATED AS ROADMAP — NOT built live:** the full benefit stack beyond the hero, the advocate-in-the-room interview prep + packet, IEP/504 + healthcare accommodations, the Contributor Studio. Pitch as the platform; do not build in the 6h window.
24	- Everything in Sections 1–4 below is scoped to serve THIS path first.
25	
26	---
27	
28	## 1. IDEA BRIEF
29	
30	### Problem (quantified take-up gap)
31	2.7M Californians are eligible for CalFresh but receive zero benefits, leaving **$3.5B in federal food dollars unclaimed every year** (Nourish California, *Lost Dollars, Empty Plates* 2024). National SNAP participation is 88% of eligible people (USDA FNS, FY2022); California lags at ~81%, so the CA gap is *wider* than national, which is exactly why CA is the wedge. The same intake unlocks adjacent unclaimed pools: ~290k CalFresh households forgo ~$56M in CalEITC (two-thirds purely because they never filed a state return), and ~300k eligible college students leave ~$100M/month on the table.
32	
33	The deeper problem is not awareness, it is **correctness under a moving target**. The state↔federal coordination seam (SNAP is federally funded but state-run as CalFresh; CA's broad-based categorical eligibility raises the gross-income screen to 200% FPL and waives the asset test) means a federal-default screener tells working CA households the *wrong* answer. And 2026 H.R.1 makes every static explainer wrong on dated seams: immigrant-eligibility cut effective 2026-04-01, work/ABAWD requirements effective 2026-06-01. As of today (2026-06-13) both are already live.
34	
35	### Who
36	- **User: applicant-facing.** The person uses it directly, free, no account, anonymous mode by default (critical for mixed-status households). The product is ultimately the person's *advocate*, walking into the benefits interview with full context.
37	- **Who-pays `[FB-18]`:** the person never pays.
38	  1. **Medi-Cal managed-care plans, paid through CalAIM "Community Supports"** (the contract vehicle plans already pay for) to close food-insecurity referral loops via Enhanced Care Management + closed-loop referrals. We are the eligibility brain *inside* that already-paid loop. This is a named buyer mechanism, not a hope.
39	  2. **Food banks / county outreach contracts** pay per-enrollment (a cited per-CalFresh-enrollment outreach rate band lives in `FINAL.md`, flagged as a band with provenance, not a fabricated point estimate).
40	  3. **GoodRx affiliate** (~15% of retail Rx) as a one-line cost-pathways add-on, never the headline.
41	- The MCP/AEO typed interface is the **distribution wedge for builders**; for VCs it is reframed in one line: *"plans and CBOs are paid to close referral loops; we are the eligibility brain inside that paid loop."*
42	
43	### What it does (the locked architecture)
44	**One eligibility brain, many programs, one verified resource graph, two front doors (UI + typed agent API).**
45	
46	**MODEL ROUTING is the whole "why Opus" answer and it is greppable `[FB-9, FB-10]`:**
47	- **PolicyEngine** (self-hosted, open-source `policyengine-us`) is the deterministic brain. **We scope the VERIFIED engine path to CalFresh only** (PolicyEngine covers 55+ programs as executable rules-as-code — that breadth is the roadmap, not the 6h build). CalFresh is the federal SNAP tree (`meets_snap_gross_income_test`, `meets_snap_net_income_test`, `meets_snap_asset_test`, `meets_snap_categorical_eligibility`, `is_snap_eligible`) plus CA overrides (`ca_snap_immigration_status_eligible`). CA's 200%-FPL BBCE + asset waiver flow through `meets_snap_categorical_eligibility`, so the federal-NOT → CA-ELIGIBLE flip is a *true engine output*, not hand-coded.
48	- **Opus 4.8** does ONLY six load-bearing reasoning lanes, NEVER benefit arithmetic:
49	  1. messy natural language → a correctly period-keyed structured household object (monthly vars `YYYY-MM`, annual vars `YYYY`, one cadence per request);
50	  2. rule-currency / date-status / county judgment (H.R.1 trigger-date awareness, which program path);
51	  3. parse unstructured prose (Rx PAP/copay eligibility text, Contributor-Studio program URLs);
52	  4. drive the live phone-verification dialogue + DTMF;
53	  5. **shapeshifting UX** — render the verdict + why-you-qualify + next steps in the user's language with LIVE switching across English / Spanish / 中文 / فارسی (and more) at any moment, at the right reading level, with read-aloud; presentation only — the engine result and dollars NEVER change with language (Farsi renders RTL);

content.text:
The step-7 recommendation beat is already in the doc (your edit landed) — and it's framed well: it uses the same household context and the CalFresh-categorical signal to recommend the rest, no fabricated dollars. Nothing for me to change there.

Here's the high-level summary across the execution doc, spaced to read fast.

---

# One Door · California — at a glance

**What it is**

One applicant-facing eligibility brain. The math is deterministic; Opus does only the hard reasoning, the live call, and the language.

 

**The demo (one deep path, live)**

1. Person speaks in **Spanish** → switch to English / 中文 / فارسی anytime; the math never changes with language.

2. **PolicyEngine** computes the CalFresh benefit (income, deductions, categorical eligibility, the 2026 rule changes).

3. **Opus** spots the one fact it's unsure about.

4. It places a **live, disclosed call to a county benefits line** to resolve that fact.

5. It reviews the whole trace, **catches and corrects its own assumption**.

6. It outputs: *"Likely eligible. ~$XXX/mo. Verified at [time]. Apply via BenefitsCal with these 3 documents. Audit log attached."*

7. It then **recommends the rest of the stack** the household likely qualifies for (WIC, Medi-Cal, CARE, LifeLine, school meals, SUN Bucks), each cited, no dollar figure.

 

**What's built vs shown vs narrated**

- **Built + verified:** CalFresh only — dollars, citations, the live call.

- **Shown in the UI:** the full California shelf — every other program as a cited "you may also qualify" card (no dollar figure).

- **Narrated as roadmap:** the deepened stack, advocate interview-prep, IEP/healthcare, Contributor Studio.

 

**Why it wins this panel**

- **Reasoning + a real action + digital→physical** — the trifecta the builder judges reward.

- **"Why Opus?"** answered by model routing (engine does math, Opus does judgment).

- **"How do you know it's right?"** answered by an eval suite with a stated accuracy number + an honest fallback.

- **Impact:** the advocate 2.7M eligible-but-unenrolled Californians don't have.

 

**How "done" works**

`verify.sh` runs unattended and only emits `RUBRIC_GREEN` when every check passes against the live deployment — the model proves done, never asserts it.

 

**What you hand the agent**

One `ultracode` master prompt (Section 3) + the secrets + "read `research 4/` for context." It then writes its own contract, builds, deploys, self-verifies, and loops until green.

---

Want the same treatment applied *inside* `SOLUTION.md` (looser spacing throughout for readability), or keep the doc dense and use this as the standalone overview?

lines: **Build ONE path live; narrate the rest.** The judges punish sprawl — so one deep, verified, live path, and the platform vision stays in the pitch.
- **THE LIVE PATH — "Verified verdict in your language" (canonical orchestration flow):**
-  1. A persona describes their situation in plain language, **in Spanish** (switchable to English ⇄ 中文 ⇄ فارسی at any moment; the engine result is byte-identical across languages, Farsi RTL).

+  1. A persona gives their situation **in Spanish** — typed, spoken, OR by **dropping in a document** (a 1040 / tax return, a pay stub, or a past benefits application — PDF or photo). Opus parses the document into the structured household object so they never retype it; the extracted fields are shown for the person to **confirm / correct before anything is computed** (the document's PII is not persisted). (Switchable to English ⇄ 中文 ⇄ فارسی anytime; the engine result is byte-identical across languages, Farsi RTL.)

2. **Opus routes the pure math to the deterministic rules engine** (PolicyEngine): gross/net income tests, deductions, categorical eligibility, ABAWD/work requirements, and the post-April/June-2026 immigrant + work-rule changes.
3. **Opus does only the hard parts:** parse the unstructured input, reconcile conflicting/ambiguous rules, **spot uncertainty, and decide the ONE specific fact that needs external verification.**

4. It **triggers a live, disclosed voice-agent call to a real county benefits information line (or authorized CBO)** to confirm that single high-uncertainty fact — e.g. *the treatment of a part-time student in a mixed-status household with an elderly member*, or *the exact documents required under the new rules*.

4. drive the live phone-verification dialogue + DTMF;

5. **shapeshifting UX** — render the verdict + why-you-qualify + next steps in the user's language with LIVE switching across English / Spanish / 中文 / فارسی (and more) at any moment, at the right reading level, with read-aloud; presentation only — the engine result and dollars NEVER change with language (Farsi renders RTL);

6. **fact-grounding triage + live verification** — classify every fact it would show as engine-computed, cited, or UNVERIFIABLE; for anything unvetted/unverifiable (a listing's current hours, a rule not in the engine, a stale detail) it DISPATCHES a live verification agent (a real phone call to a published line, or a live fetch of the official source) and NEVER states an ungrounded fact.

content.text:
Done — document ingestion is now wired through the whole doc, consistently:

- **Section 0 flow (step 1):** input can be typed, spoken, or a **dropped document** (1040, pay stub, past app — PDF or photo); Opus extracts the household so the person never retypes it.

- **Opus lanes (1 & 3):** lane 1 now accepts an uploaded document as an input source; lane 3 parses uploaded docs alongside prose.

- **Master prompt:** the immutable clause and the pipeline `extract` stage both include document parsing.

- **Rubric C5 (MUST):** a known sample doc must parse into the right income/household fields, the extracted values come back `needs_confirmation:true` and are **not auto-fed to the engine until the person confirms**, and the doc's PII isn't persisted. Added to the GREEN gate (now `C1-C5`).

The one guardrail I held firm on: **extracted fields are shown for confirmation before they drive the verdict** — never silently trusted. That matters because OCR/extraction errors would otherwise corrupt the eligibility math, and this panel would probe exactly that. It also keeps the privacy posture intact (doc PII not persisted, anonymous mode).

Scope note: for the 6h build this is one or two doc types done well (a 1040 and a pay stub); more formats are roadmap. Want me to keep going with refinements, or are we at **"ready to go"**?

content.text:
Short answer: **yes, but a tight slice of it — not full generative shapeshifting.** Here's the reasoning, because this is exactly where sprawl creeps back in.

**Keep "shifting UI" scoped to the accessibility trio that's already real and Opus-driven:**
- **Live language switching** (EN ⇄ ES ⇄ 中文 ⇄ فارسی) — already locked; it's the "in your language" of the hero.
- **Reading-level / plain-language** — same content, re-leveled by Opus on demand.
- **Read-aloud** — for low-literacy / low-vision users.

These three are cheap, genuinely model-generated (not templates), reinforce the "meet you where you are" mission, and demo in ~15 seconds. They strengthen the story without competing with the spine.

**Don't build (narrate as roadmap):** per-user *generated layouts*, device-adaptive restructuring, screen-reader-rebuilt flows. In 6 hours these either look cosmetic (gimmick risk — Marius/Alistair will read a morphing UI as distraction from verifiable-done) or eat the time you need to get the live call + evals solid.

**The framing that keeps it safe:** the verifiable spine (rules-engine ÷ Opus, the live call, the eval number) is the star; shifting UI is a *supporting* accessibility capability, shown briefly, not the headline. A UI that reshapes itself is impressive only *after* the judges trust the answer underneath it.

So: include the trio, hold the rest as vision.

questions.question: How much "shifting UI" do we lock into the build?

options.label: Accessibility trio (recommended)
options.description: Live language switch + reading-level/plain-language + read-aloud. Real, Opus-driven, cheap, ~15s demo beat. Deeper device/layout shapeshifting stays roadmap.

options.label: Language only
options.description: Keep just the live EN/ES/中文/فارسی switch already in the doc; drop reading-level + read-aloud from the build. Leanest, least demo surface.

options.label: Full shapeshifting
options.description: Build per-user generated layouts + device/screen-reader-adaptive flows too. Highest ambition, but real gimmick + time risk in 6h, and competes with the spine.

content:
The one idea: a policy-to-verified-code compiler

  Opus 4.8 reads the entire CalFresh policy corpus — the actual ~2,000-page county/state/federal eligibility manuals — and writes a working,
  auditable eligibility decision engine, with a citation to the exact policy paragraph behind every single rule, then self-tests it against
  real labeled cases until it's green.

  The product isn't the chatbot. The product is the compiler that turns law into verified software. The chat/voice "am I eligible?" just rides
  on top — and is trustworthy because every answer traces to cited, tested code.

  Why this is superintelligence (and the exact line to say)

  ▎ "No single human understands all of CalFresh — the rules span thousands of pages of federal regs, state guidance, and county handbooks that
  ▎ contradict each other and change constantly. A benefits navigator trains for years on one program in one county. Opus 4.8 reads the entire
  ▎ corpus and produces a correct, cited, self-tested decision engine in minutes — and proves it's correct. That's a cognitive task at a scale
  ▎ and accuracy a human expert team needs weeks for."

  That's the framing the judges are listening for: not "the model answered," but "the model did something a human expert team can't, and
  verified its own work."

  Why it's only possible now, because of Opus 4.8 specifically

  This is your airtight answer to the panel's universal question ("what does Opus do a cheaper model can't?"):
  - Massive-context legal reasoning — ingest the whole manual at once and reason across cross-references, not chunk-and-pray RAG (which is on
  the banned list anyway).
  - Code generation with 4× fewer overlooked defects — it's writing a correctness-critical rules engine; the defect rate is the whole game.
  - Self-verification + dynamic-workflow subagents — fan out one subagent per manual section to extract rules in parallel, assemble, run the
  test suite, and catch + fix its own mismatch (the "moment it caught a failure on its own" the org explicitly asks you to show).
  - Model routing built in — Opus does the hard legal reasoning; the generated engine then runs as cheap deterministic code. You're not paying
  frontier tokens per user query. (Gagan/Alistair/Zhen will love hearing this.)

content.text:
Shifting-UI scope is locked in the doc (trio in, deeper shapeshifting as roadmap). Now the reframe — and this is a big one, worth being clear-eyed about.

## My honest take: this is stronger. Adopt it.

The **policy-to-verified-code compiler** is a sharper superintelligence thesis than what we had. The old "why Opus" answer was model routing + reasoning over conflicting rules. The new one is unbeatable for *this* panel: *"Opus reads the entire contradictory 2,000-page corpus and writes a cited, self-tested decision engine in minutes — something no human expert team can do, and it proves its own work."* That's exactly the "the model did something humans can't, and verified it" framing the judges listen for. It also makes RAG-avoidance a *feature* (massive-context, not chunk-and-pray) and gives you the cleanest model-routing story (expensive one-time compile → cheap deterministic engine per query).

## The one reconciliation that makes it work (and stay honest)

The current doc uses **PolicyEngine as the runtime brain**. Under the reframe, PolicyEngine flips to a new, even better role: **the independent oracle that proves the Opus-generated engine is correct** — alongside hand-worked CBPP/CDSS cases. So:

- **The product = the compiler.** Opus fans out a subagent per manual section, extracts each rule *with its exact cited paragraph*, assembles the engine, and self-tests until green.
- **PolicyEngine + hand-worked cases = the green-bar oracle.** "Opus wrote this engine from the law, and we prove it matches an independently-maintained reference engine + hand-worked cases" is a *more* credible correctness claim than either alone.
- **The "caught its own failure" beat gets better:** a generated rule fails a labeled case → a verifier subagent traces the mismatch to the mis-extracted paragraph → regenerates that rule → green. Live, on the engine itself.
- **The live voice call survives** as the thing that resolves genuine ambiguity the corpus *can't* settle (it's then the residual, not the spine).

## The honest risk I have to flag

Perfectly compiling 2,000 pages in 6h isn't real. The honest scope: **generate the CalFresh eligibility *core* that covers the demo personas, self-tested green against the oracle, with uncovered rules marked `KNOWN_GAP`** — and say that out loud. This panel will probe it; "we compiled the core and prove it, the long tail is flagged, not faked" is the credible answer. (NEVER claim the full corpus is perfectly compiled.)

Two calls that genuinely change how I rewrite the doc:

content.text:
Heard — the **compiler is the credibility spine, but what *matters* in the demo is the live call + the in-person advocacy.** That's the right instinct: the technical judges need the compiler to trust you, but the human arc is what wins the room. Let me reconcile the three pillars and lock it.

Here's the architecture I'm locking in:

- **Spine (why it's trustworthy):** Opus compiles the CalFresh eligibility core from the cited ~2,000-page corpus into a self-tested engine — every rule links its policy paragraph; **PolicyEngine + hand-worked cases are the oracle** that proves it green before it serves anyone.
- **Hero #1 — the live call:** Opus spots the one fact the corpus can't settle, calls the county line to resolve it, catches its own overconfident assumption, finalizes.
- **Hero #2 — the in-person advocate:** in her language, the agent preps her to walk into the county office — exactly what to bring, what they'll ask, how to answer — grounded in the cited engine + the verified call result. *She's not alone in the room anymore.*

**One scope flag I have to raise:** "advocate in person" for a 6h build = a **cited, in-language interview-prep briefing** (the documents, the questions, the answers). The deeper version (the agent live on a headset *during* the actual interview) is vision, not buildable today — and the interview isn't happening during the demo anyway. I'm building the prep-briefing reading unless you tell me otherwise.

Let me read the current Section 0 so I rewrite it cleanly to this.

9	## 0. LOCKED DEMO SCOPE (narrowed 2026-06-13)
10	
11	**Build ONE path live; narrate the rest.** The judges punish sprawl — so one deep, verified, live path, and the platform vision stays in the pitch.
12	
13	- **THE LIVE PATH — "Verified verdict in your language" (canonical orchestration flow):**
14	  1. A persona gives their situation **in Spanish** — typed, spoken, OR by **dropping in a document** (a 1040 / tax return, a pay stub, or a past benefits application — PDF or photo). Opus parses the document into the structured household object so they never retype it; the extracted fields are shown for the person to **confirm / correct before anything is computed** (the document's PII is not persisted). (Switchable to English ⇄ 中文 ⇄ فارسی anytime; the engine result is byte-identical across languages, Farsi RTL.)
15	  2. **Opus routes the pure math to the deterministic rules engine** (PolicyEngine): gross/net income tests, deductions, categorical eligibility, ABAWD/work requirements, and the post-April/June-2026 immigrant + work-rule changes.
16	  3. **Opus does only the hard parts:** parse the unstructured input, reconcile conflicting/ambiguous rules, **spot uncertainty, and decide the ONE specific fact that needs external verification.**
17	  4. It **triggers a live, disclosed voice-agent call to a real county benefits information line (or authorized CBO)** to confirm that single high-uncertainty fact — e.g. *the treatment of a part-time student in a mixed-status household with an elderly member*, or *the exact documents required under the new rules*.
18	  5. **Opus reviews the full trace** (its own initial reasoning + the rules-engine output + the call transcript), **explicitly catches and corrects its own error or overconfident assumption**, and updates the result with citations + a confidence score.
19	  6. It produces: *"**Likely eligible.** Estimated benefit if approved: **$XXX/month**. Verified fact from the county line at **[timestamp]**. Next step: apply via BenefitsCal with these **exact 3 documents**. Full audit log attached."*
20	  7. **Closing beat — recommend the rest of the stack.** Using the *same* `household_context`, the agent then recommends the other California benefits the household would likely qualify for, driven by **categorical / adjunctive eligibility** off the CalFresh result (a CalFresh "yes" is itself the signal): *"Because you likely qualify for CalFresh, you're also likely eligible for WIC, CA LifeLine, CARE, school-meal direct-cert, SUN Bucks, and CalEITC — here's why, and the next step for each."* Each recommendation cites its adjunctive/threshold rule and gives a next step, but carries **no computed dollar figure** (only CalFresh is engine-verified; deepening each is the roadmap).
21	- **The three things on stage:** *reasoning* (PolicyEngine + Opus over conflicting/dated rules) · *a real action* (the live verification call) · *the digital→physical bridge* (online eligibility → a real-world fact confirmed by phone).
22	- **ENGINE scope vs UI breadth (locked):** the verified engine computes **only CalFresh** (eligibility + dollars + the live-call fact-check); the **UI shows the full California benefits shelf** (Medi-Cal, WIC, CalEITC, CARE/FERA, LifeLine, school meals, SUN Bucks, Rx, vouchers) as discover cards so the breadth is visible — but **only CalFresh carries a computed dollar verdict**; the other cards show "likely worth checking + why + next step," never a fabricated number.
23	- **NARRATED AS ROADMAP — NOT built live:** the full benefit stack beyond the hero, the advocate-in-the-room interview prep + packet, IEP/504 + healthcare accommodations, the Contributor Studio, and **deeper shifting UI** (per-user generated layouts, device/screen-reader-adaptive flows — beyond the accessibility trio). Pitch as the platform; do not build in the 6h window.
24	- Everything in Sections 1–4 below is scoped to serve THIS path first.
